Excellent pants with some caveats I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

Buy these long and flattering pants!!! I bought these pants because of the comments of them being tall girl friendly. I'm 5'7, 145 lbs but have long legs and a short torso. Unexpectedly in love! So, let me start off by saying they don’t look as good in the pictures as I feel they do in real life. I am 5’9” and between 175-180 depending on the week of the month 🙄. I thought they’d be a little longer, but I really don’t need to wear heels at all with these. I actually have to wear them a little lower than I wanted because they don’t go as long as I’d like - I wanted them to sweep the floor honestly. So I’d say, definitely size up bc I’m usually a large in pants and also definitely buy these pants !

I actually really like these I’m a vet tech student so I end up spending a lot of time bending down, sitting on my knees, walking around, etc. I need a lot of room in my scrubs in order to move and not show my butt to the whole world. With that being said, this material does not allow a lot of stretch, like at all. So if you’re iffy about sizes, just go up a size. I bought two pairs of scrubs: one set in XL and another in XXL. The XL fit most comfortably, although they can be a little snug in the thigh/butt region. I’m a larger, curvy gal with thunder thighs so I’m really not shocked. Also, the pants are a little too long for my 5’5” stature, so they’re gonna have to be hemmed to stop them from dragging on the floor constantly. The XXL set was so big, however, I was practically swimming in them. It was unflattering, so I returned them for a full refund. Now, back to the material: it’s mostly just polyester. No cotton or spandex. It’s a bit thick so yes, it does get hot (gotta love that +100 degree Cali weather). However, the plus side to the material is hair just comes right off it! Which is so exciting for me (haha). So if you work with animals all day, like I do, then you don’t need to carry around a lint roller all the time with these scrubs. Also, there are so many pockets! Love it! I’m gonna give these scrubs the seal of approval.

Fits as expected, plenty of pockets (none zippered, though.) I'm 5'6" and 145lbs. In pants, I fit somewhere between a size 10 and 12 (US). I bought the black pants in a size Large, and I'd say it's true to size as it's a bit big around the waist and a tiny bit long (which I prefer over too short!), but I'm fairly certain the Medium would be too small for me (which is in sync with the size guide.) I find them comfortable. So far, about 8 months in, they've held up to hot-water machine washes 1-3x/week without fading or distorting. I just bought a second pair and can't tell the difference. Just FYI: There are hot pink accents on the black pants, which I didn't see in the advertised photos. I bought these for volunteering at an animal shelter. I needed something for my phone, cat treats, dog treats, gloves, poop bags, etc. The multiple pockets are handy for this. I wish there was a zippered, deep pocket for my phone, but so far, it's sat in the top (unzippered) pockets without falling out. (And at a shelter, you're always moving, bending, etc.) These DO gather animal fur and lint up quickly, so be aware. I used to be a paramedic, as well, and these pants seem like they'd be good for healthcare. I could easily stuff multiple emergency items in these, though like I said above, a zippered pocket or two would be nice for extra security of personal or important items. If you need to carry more than a couple things, these beat out traditional scrub bottoms by a long shot.

A Review from a girl who Actually Collects These Styles of Pants Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.

Good for the price. Could be better. I got the plum colored pants in size medium. I am 5'6" and I typically wear a size 4-6. The length on these pants was just right. I'm unsure how I feel about the fit of the pants on me, but I'd say these are comparable to a 2-4 sized pant. I wanted these to look like skinny jeans, but feel like leggings. For the most part, they accomplish that. The fabric is thin, soft, and stretchy, which is good for physical comfort, but also means that the fabric really conforms to your body and doesn't feel like it's hiding anything. For this reason, I'd personally stick to darker colors that might not show every dimple off.

No size standards whatsoever These are SO comfortable. The length is just right (I am 5’4”) However, you have to play a guessing game on the size. I purchased the black/blue combo pack in XL. They are both labeled XL, but the blue pants are GIGANTIC. I am not a small woman but the blue ones were way too big in the waist. The black ones fit perfectly. I can’t imagine why the factory doesn’t use some sort of size standard, but I think it comes from China and they don’t have standards so you just have to hope you’re getting what you ordered. I like the black ones though. I’ll be wearing them often! EDIT: One year later... The fabric is thin and the seams ripped due to the stitching creating a "perforated line" that splits the fabric, especially in the inner thigh. The stitching itself seems okay, I don't think the thread they used was the problem. It's the thin fabric.

Love these need more! So I recently switched jobs and now have to dress business casual. As someone tall and thin 5’8.5” I have the hardest time finding pants that fit especially in the length let alone online shopping. I’ve tried the Try Before You but option (highly recommend it) on a pair similar to this so I said why not if they don’t fit I can return them. Let me just say I LOVE these!!! The color is beautiful especially during the summer with my nice tan. They were a little tight pulling up but they’re brand new so they’ll loosen up. Perfect length for me to wear with either open toe heels, sandals or the right pair of boots/booties. I’m wearing a size Medium in the turquoise. They fit great in the waist even with a little bit of fluff in my mid 30’s lol. They are mid rise which I like I can’t do high rise pants. They give me a little bit of a booty just couldn’t tell by the way I was standing. Just a suggestion for your undies to wear something smooth and possibly nude or white as you can see pattern material through the front (see circled photo).

SO WARM, AMAZING These pants are awesome. Exactly like the description, fit me perfect (ordered XS - 5’5” 125 lb) and so warm/comfortable I never want to take them off. They are also a true black color, not an off black.

Comfy, not flattering I’m 5’1”, around 165 pounds & ordered a L. The pants are several inches too long on me but wearable cuffed or not due to the elastic ankles. The pants are comfortable & warm. I walked the dog in them, at about 25 F without layering & was fine. They fall down a bit on me, despite the waist cord, but a smaller size would expose too much behind if I bent over. Given how these look, there’s no excuse for the shallow pockets. They ought to help deep pockets but they don’t- pockets aren’t even deep enough for a phone. Arrived very quickly. Very cozy. I do recommend them. But don’t plan on using the pockets & don’t plan on feeling pretty. If you’re short, do plan on them being bunched.

Nice, thick and really warm I was really surprised...They Fit Perfectly! I bought these pants to wear when I remove the snow and in temps below 36°, I live in a Northern state in the U.S near Canada, so you know that we are cold which is now 34° out. I stand at 5,1" and weight 118 lbs, I normally weara petite small to some medium like a 6p in a stretch fabric paint and a size 2/4 in misses size pants a size 2 if it has stretch in it. I bought these fleece lined pants on the flash deal in a size small and for me being Petite, I thought that they would be loose, and long, but guys, they were not! I do not have long legs just advantage length and eventho I am small these pants are thick and I don't have to shorten them or roll them up, my inseam is a 29" with flats on. I wished that I would have bought more than one pair. If you have a child in college, in the cold these are perfect for the campus walking and catching the bus and yes, walking in the cold, those 2000 steps and for shoveling snow, I feel no cold. I know that I am going to have to Wash them in wool lite and hung dry to keep the heavy fleece lining good and not twisted and to keep them from shrinkage. The fit is perfect!

I am (5'2" - 145lbs) a person who feels cold a lot. Bought these in medium. These keep me so warm and comfy. I have worn them outside when temps are below 32 degrees and windy and the cold didn't bother me. They also make me look like I have elephant legs but that matters so much less than comfort for me. If you are trying to keep heating cost low but want to be warm and comfy, these should be a serious consideration.

Incredibly thick fleece I am an older woman, 5’1”, 145 lbs and have a short torso. I normally wear a size 10 pant. I bought large because I wanted comfortable pants for lounging in the evenings. I keep my thermostat at 63 degrees. These are comfortably warm. I’m pretty sure that a size medium would be a bit more flattering, but as thick s the fleece lining is, they probably would be. Bit awkward. There is a lot of bulk in the crotch area. The fabric is soft. I haven’t washed them yet, but they look like they will shed. Hopefully not too much. The only negative is the pockets are shallow. I pulled the waist rope, it is a bit tacky. If you want warm pants, I would recommend these.

One pair fit amazing, the other was a bit small. These are very nicely made fleece lined leggings. These are kind of in the middle for how thick they are. They aren't super heavy weight but aren't so thin the wind blows right through them. I got the two pack with navy/blk.I am 5'7" and 150lbs and usually wear between a M and a L. I got these in a L. I tried the navy ones on first and they fit like a dream; they were so comfy and absolutely perfect, I was amazed. Then I tried the black ones on and was not as amazed; they were shorter in the back or something and super tight over my butt. They weren't unwearable but just a different fit, less comfortable fit, than the navy ones.There are three pockets on these; a front waistband one and two side pockets. The side pockets were a decent size and can fit a typical phone fine. The front waistband pocket was bigger than normal waistband pockets and actually fit my whole wallet very easy.Overall I think these are high quality and well made. They were comfy and warm. I am tentative to give them 5 stars though because the two pairs of leggings fit so differently. So, I guess buy with caution. They seem pretty nice though.

NOT what everyone raves about I wanted to love these like so many people do, but these simply aren’t the same quality many have claimed. Maybe they’ve skimped on new batches since they are so popular? I got them in January 2022. Here are my pros and cons. Unfortunately there are too many cons for me to like these leggings. For reference, I am 116 lbs and 5’4”. I got a medium. Pros -They are buttery soft, like everyone says. -They have pockets. -They were $16 Cons -The quality of the material is sub par. They are thin to the point that if they were any other color than “dark”, they could be see through. -They aren’t made well. I’m wondering how long the seams will hold. -The fabric bled when I got them. I tried them on and put my hands in the pockets. My hands, fingernails and legs had black dye on them! -I washed them (with a color catcher sheet to protect my other dark clothes) and they faded on the first wash. -One leg is more than an inch longer than the other. I’m 5’4” and had to roll one leg up 3 times and the longer one 4 times. -The logo on the back was faded and “crumbly” upon arrival. It was already peeling off. I’m keeping them only because returning them is more of a hassle than keeping a $16 pair of leggings. I won’t buy more. I’ll wear them to work in the yard or possibly the gym, but definitely not for anything else. They aren’t flattering to me. They are so thin and remind me of tights. I hope this info helps you! I always read reviews before buying anything in Amazon.

Best Work Pants EVER!!! I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

The waist is comfortably loose but the pants don’t fall down as long as I’m not, say, jogging with a phone in my pocket. The main downside to the pants is that they aren’t super flattering. They have a pleated front so you could have a narrow waist and a big underbelly and they’d fit comfortably but if you don’t have a big underbelly, the pants seem awfully loose there. The material is medium thick and a soft knit and they seem well made.

The fabric has a sleek feel to it and the texture is somewhere between a jean and a knit pant, it's a very unique fabric. The fabric moves well and feels great on, it also doesn't seem to attract pet hair which I love since I have cats and always struggle with keeping black pants pet hair free.Unfortunately, these do seem to run a bit small. I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got these in a size L which says it has a 32-33" waist because I really don't like my waistbands tight. As you can see this waistband was pretty tight, not uncomfortably so, but I definitely have a bit of belly bulge going on here. The waistband has enough stretch that when I sit down it's still comfortable but not the most flattering.There are functioning pockets on the front and back. They are slightly smaller than normal pants pockets but still work to fit my wallet or phone (I have a Z4 fold).These pants are comfy and look cute if I have a shirt on that doesn't show the waistband. I really do like them but wish the waistband was an inch wider. I also think they are a bit pricey for what you are getting. However, overall I would recommend...just size up and don't go by their size chart.

LOVE LOVE These pants fit perfectly. They are a tad long but I paired it with high heels and it was fine. They are soft and have a good stretch. High in the waist. They’re very comfortable! I’m 5’3” 146lbs and I ordered a medium, which is my size and they fit better than I expected.

LOVE LOVE These pants fit perfectly. They are a tad long but I paired it with high heels and it was fine. They are soft and have a good stretch. High in the waist. They’re very comfortable! I’m 5’3” 146lbs and I ordered a medium, which is my size and they fit better than I expected.

No roll down on the waist band. They're truly a "tall" pant. I'm only 5'5", but I have long legs, and they are only a scooch too long for me. Extremely comfy and light weight. Perfect for spring and fall weather. I'm 5'5", 185lbs and got an XL for a perfect fit.

I ordered these to wear with trainers and they are the perfect length! I am 5’11 a UK 14 and the XL fit me well. There is a pocket on each hip. The fabric is soft, stretchy and it does not look see through on me. The waist is also elasticated and I find it flattering. I would describe the style as leggings that flare at the feet. I love these trousers.

My daughter is 5ft 10 and a size ten, her legs are disproportionately long which means she hardly ever fits into normal size clothing or leggings and cannot wear leggings with trainers as they always sit above her ankles. I’ve ordered these in a size medium in long and they fit her perfectly. She is at university so these are great for relaxing in the evenings with friends or for wearing whilst studying. She reports that these are very comfy and the leg length is perfect for her. They look great and are fairly thick and fit well around the waist and lower torso. She likes to wear them with a loose slouchy T-shirt and finds them figure flattering.

On the fence with this pair of pants I was looking for some inexpensive lightweight joggers to wear hiking or while cleaning the house or yard as I've always been afraid to wear my ridiculously expensive Athleta joggers doing either. I received this pair yesterday and here are my thoughts: Pro: 1. Color options are virtuously infinite and price point is excellent. I ordered the khaki, but was going to consider other colors if the fit was right. 2. Fabric is lightweight, but opaque, doesn't show panty lines, very stretchy, nice drape and soft to the touch, just like the Athleta joggers. 3. Lots of pockets: slant (not bulky) pockets in the front, one zippered pocket over the lumbar spine (for keys or a very tiny pet maybe? too shallow for a phone). 4. Length works for me. I am 5'2", the bottom sags some which is totally fine with me, even gives it a bit of a cool vibe. This pant should easily work for average height women, lengthwise. (Only one) Con: 1. The waist is a tad tight. I ordered a medium which is what Amazon recommended and, while the fabric is overall very stretchy, the very top of the pants where it falls on the waist has a double seam on the inside which gives that area much less give. Picture of waist unstretched is attached. I don't think I'd exchange it for a large, that would probably be too big. In all, I've decided to keep these and see if the waist thing becomes a non-issue later. For reference, my natural waist is 30 inches and I wear a size 6 in Athleta joggers.

Perfect for short girls If you need joggers that will fit your short legs, these are for you! I will be ordering more. I’m 4’11” and I got a M and they fit well, a little tighter around the booty, but still an overall good fit. Very comfy, good material, has pockets on the sides and in the back.

Perfect for short girls If you need joggers that will fit your short legs, these are for you! I will be ordering more. I’m 4’11” and I got a M and they fit well, a little tighter around the booty, but still an overall good fit. Very comfy, good material, has pockets on the sides and in the back.

Soft, but…….. I ordered two pairs of these buttery soft joggers. They are great because they come in different lengths. I opted for the petites which was perfect for my 5’3” body. However, the attention to detail just isn't there. Both pairs of pants are full of bunches of threads both inside and outside of the pants. The one pair I received had very clearly been opened and returned. They were wrinkled beyond words. If you don’t mind purchasing something and then trimming the mounds of threads left behind by the manufacturer, buy these pants otherwise, move on.

Fits perfectly, light weight These joggers are soft on your body and fit true to size. . Good sized pockets. They are a good spring or summer pant. You can wear them casually or dress them up. Length is good without being to long. I'm 5’5 and they fit nicely in length. I got the black color and it’s a really rich looking pant.

Perfect, comfy, and stretchy. 5”6’ 170 ordered L long These are really perfect, I wear them all the time for work. They pass as trousers. These will be a wardrobe staple for years to come.

Slimming + truly high waisted! A beautiful pair of pants that are truly high waisted. The pants makes my waist look slimmer, legs longer, and overall taller. For reference, I got a XS. When barefoot, these pants just hovers over the ground, making me think they’re around 30-31” (but don’t quote me on that cause I didn’t do the actual measurement)

The fabric is super soft, nice and stretchy, and honestly just really comfy all around. They hug in the right places and move with you, not against you. BUT — I ordered the Tall expecting a 33” inseam, and what I got was 32”… which might not sound like much, but at 5'9” it makes a difference. They're juuust short enough to keep me tugging them down every so often and wondering if I imagined the "tall" part of the listing. I really want to buy more (because they are that comfy), but I’m hesitant, since I’m not sure if I’ll end up with the same slightly-too-short length again. If you’re on the tall side but not too tall, they might work perfectly for you. But if you’re like me and want that full ankle coverage without doing a "where’s the flood" shuffle, maybe double-check the measurements or bring backup socks. 😅

COMFORTABLE ! Super comfortable pants, easy to bend and move in all day. I'm only 5'0, the short fit perfect! Definitely recommend.

Exceeded Expectations! Comfort, Fit, Feel I NEVER buy pants online. I’m 5’3-1/2”, 145, almost 70 yo mom of 4 (including twins) grandmother of 6. I work remotely now and sit at a desk all day. Thick in the thighs. I was looking to replace a pair of Calvin Klein straight-legged cotton blend sweats I’ve loved and worn for years. I tried these based on other reviews and I knew I loved them the moment they hit my waist. These sweaties are buttery soft inside and out, no hang-up or pulling along thighs or across the butt. They’re opaque and perfectly dense enough for cold Arizona winter mornings with no added bulk or lining. I love pockets but usually forfeit the convenience for vanity’s sake, given my short stocky frame. I love these sweats even more because the pockets don’t accentuate my thighs. These pants fit like a soft cozy cloud from the waist down. They allow flowing, breathable movement, and the petit length is chef’s kiss perfect. I’m so comfortable in these I’m going to order a Medium just to test and see if the L is almost too roomy. Either way, I love everything about these.

Cute, Comfortable and Made for Long Legs A fantastic pajama set by Zylioo. It is so soft and comfy. You can lounge in it all day. I washed and dried on low and they came out even softer with no noticeable shrinkage. The fit is great. These are actually floor length on me which I love. And the sleeves actually go past my wrists which is rare. I do wish the top was a little more fitted but that's a preference and doesn't take away from the quality and comfort of the pajamas. I ordered the Small XTall and I am 5'10, 120 lbs, 31A-25-34 with a 32.5 inch inseam and 24 inch long arms for reference. These would make a great gift for those that love cute and comfy loungewear and pajamas.

Fabulous leggings! Everything I could ask for! Very supportive but still very comfortable. They are not too thick, but not thin either. I tend to get too hot working out in thick leggings. I am 5’6” and 140lbs and bought the medium in the 32.5” length. They just skim the floor barefoot, which is perfect for pilates, yoga, or everyday wear. For when I do wear shoes, I prefer the longer length. Pics are after a few washes. They hold up great, did not really shrink, and the best thing I love is that every piece of lint or pet hair does not stick to them. The material is fabulous for not being clingy!

Soft & Comfy / Supportive in the Waist & Rear These flare leggings are soft and comfortable and fit okay. I'm 5'2" so regular length pants are typically way too long on me--the size small graze the floor, so while they're a tad too long, I can still wear them. I like how supportive yet stretchy the waistband is, and they're supportive in the rear, even giving it a bit of lift. One issue I have is that they bunch up at the backs of my knees, and it looks weird. Maybe it's because they're a little too long on me.

WOW! These are soooo comfortable! I can’t remember the last time I had workout pants like these that are this comfortable. The fabric is smooth and buttery soft. It fits and looks amazing on. These are great for yoga, pilates, etc. I personally wouldn’t walk around in them for everyday wear but if you have to run into the store after working out in them I totally wouldn’t be embarrassed - just saying! I think having different lengths is such a bonus. I hate pants that are too short or too long so picking it according to your height is great. I think it runs true-to-size. For reference I’m about 5’5, about 140 lbs and my waist is a 27, hips 40. The medium fits very well. The waistband isn’t tight at all and it does have that tummy control ability which is like a miracle that it isn’t tight, yet it smooths the stomach. How the heck did they achieve that?!

Super soft Here’s a polished, insightful review you can use: --- This pajama set is a win for anyone who struggles to find comfy loungewear that actually fits a taller frame. The fabric blend (65% polyester, 30% rayon, and 5% spandex) makes it incredibly soft with just the right amount of stretch, so it feels warm and cozy without ever getting too hot. The color is beautiful in person, and the overall quality stands out right away. I’m 5'6" and 118 lbs and went with the size S. It fits really well—especially the top, which runs a bit longer in a flattering way. That length makes it perfect for tall people, but still wearable even if you're not super tall. The pants are also comfortable and stretchy, and while they’re obviously designed with longer legs in mind, I can still wear them without any issue. Overall, this set is thoughtfully made, incredibly comfortable, and a great option for anyone who wants pajamas that feel elevated and fit properly.

More like ankle jeans for me I ordered the size 8 that has a 30” inseam. I’m 5’ 5.5” and about 139#. I wouldn’t say I have long legs; my legs and torso are proportional for my height. These jeans fit more like ankle length as you can see in the photos. I am returning them and am debating whether I should buy the long length to see if they fit better.

Could be a tad bit longer Satisfied with my purchase, I wish the inseam was a bit longer for wearing heels. I’m a little taller than 5’7” height and I weight is 172 lbs. I ordered a size 14 long; the inseam on these jeans is 30-31 inches.

Tall-friendly scrub joggers that are comfortable. As a dental assistant, I’ve gone through plenty of scrub pants that claimed to be “long,” but these are the first that actually deliver. I’m 5 ft 8 in with long legs and have had challenges finding pants that properly fit. The inseam options on these Sevegos make it easy to get a true full-length fit, and the legs don’t creep up throughout the day. The jogger style looks clean and modern without feeling tight or restrictive. While I wish there was cotton in this blend, the fabric feels smooth, flexible, and comfortable enough. The waistband sits comfortably and stays put, and the cuffs at the ankles are soft but secure. The pocket layout is genuinely useful, with enough space for everything I need without adding bulk. The stitching and overall construction feel solid, and they wash well without losing shape. If you’re tired of scrub pants that come up short or feel stiff, these joggers are a major upgrade. They look good, move well, and actually fit longer legs the way they should. p.s. I don’t know why one reviewer said these are made in the USA, when both the packaging and tag say, “Made in China.” This is the information on the tag: 70% Polyester 20% Rayon, 5% Elastane Machine wash cold. Do not bleach. Do not tumble dry. Iron cool heat reverse side. Do not wash with other items with any drycleaning methods. Keep away from fire. Wash inside out. Wash with similar colours. Made in China.

I need to order these in more colours! - Update - I did Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Lee Legendary midrise straight jeans As a young woman I wore a 6-8 L Lee jeans. Then I had my son 20 years ago and I was a 14L getting down to a 12L sometimes. Well I went up to a size 22 before my knee surgeries, and at 44 yrs old I am back down to a 14/16 L. I absolutely love these jeans. I ordered a 16 L to be safe and I absolutely love these Lee mide rise straight leg jeans. They fit me perfectly. They never wrinkle. My backside looks good in them and I love where the pockets are positioned. I will be ordering them in a 14 L when I lose another 5 lbs as I am still recovering from my knee replacements. The material is perfect and I love the look and fit of these jeans.

Trust the inseam length! As any tall girlie knows, it’s hard to find “tall” pants that are ACTUALLY tall, especially when it comes to scrubs. These are actually tall! I’m only 5’9” so I probably could’ve gone with the 32” inseam, but I’m traumatized from all the pants that have lied to me in the past so I ordered the 34”. They’re longer than needed, but thanks to the stretchy fabric around the ankle they just tuck under and look absolutely fine. I saw another review that said they aren’t made for curvy girls - but yes they are. I have a booty and these pants fit it well, you just have to be tall for them to hit you right. Five pockets total, two in the front, two in the back, and one on the right leg. The two on the front and the one on the leg are especially deep. Elastic waistband makes these extra comfy. They’re honestly so spacious, I can’t wait to wear these. You won’t be disappointed!! If you’re tall at least.

Comfortable, deep pockets, very well made. I ordered an XL and I love them. Disclaimer: I’m NOT tall but I love the extra material at the ankle feel so I chose these. I’m 5’0 and got exactly what I’d hoped for. Now they are made for taller people and I believe that generally we are built different (short vs tall) and I did notice less rear end space and a longer front space but it works for me - just a heads up. The material is so soft, flexible, and durable. The pockets are deep and hold up well. The ankle cuffs are so comfortable and do not irritate the skin. The waist band is comfortable and the adjustment strings are thick and sturdy. I 100% recommend these. They are worth their price for a good pair of high quality and durable scrubs.

Its always a good sign when she wears something I bought her more than once, and she's been living in these for casual stuff and lounging around the house

Comfortable, very tall These leggings are nice, comfortable, and have a smooth feel to them. Somehow I hadn’t noticed that the product listing says they’re made for tall people, and since I’m 5’3”, they turned out very long on me (as you can see in the pictures). Still, they look very pretty overall. The back design seemed a little strange before I put them on, but once worn, it actually made sense and looked flattering. The waistband is snug with a good tummy control feature, though the very top of the waist does roll down slightly when bending. The color is beautiful in person, and I really like how they look once on. They don’t have any pockets, which is a drawback, but overall I’d recommend them if you don’t mind the extra length.

Read the name (because I didn’t): TALLEMON. I didn’t realize the name of the brand or read the description. I received these and didn’t realize how long these were. I’m 5’1 and will be wearing this in the house so please be mindful of this haha. Overall, the quality is great, VERYYY stretchy and great for activewear. It’s fitting and well. Bunched up because obviously it’s not made for petite people (my bad). I’d recommend if I were taller!

Finally leggings for tall women! I have a 35” inseam and have struggled my entire life to find pants that fit. These leggings are amazing. I wore them on a hike to really test them out and they stayed in place without needing to be pulled up, and the thickness is just right. The high waist gives some support (not extreme tummy control, but enough to feel comfortable). I ordered the 36” inseam, and when they say they’re designed for women 6’9” and taller, they are NOT kidding, they are long! For the first time ever, I’ll actually be hemming leggings shorter, and I couldn’t be happier about that. Other details I love: no front seam (huge selling point), and the scrunch seam in the back gives a nice lift to the bum. Where have these been all my life? Tall ladies, if you’ve been frustrated by too-short leggings, these are the real deal.

They really are for tall people These really are for tall women. I am 5'9 and even with their shortest 32-in inseam it is slightly long on me. However, I do love these for wearing with boots or sandals since I can bring the pant all the way down past my ankles. In addition, it does not pull on the waistband since there's plenty of length in the legs. The material itself has a great tight stretch to it. I really love the extra wide waistband which also has quite a good cinch. They are very thick and do have quite a good compression.

Listen closely to this review I’m 5’7 on a good day and about 135 pounds. Bought I size 6. I have long legs. Literally no ass muscles I honestly don’t know how I get around… my gluteus Maximus has been compared to a pancake. I just keep waiting for that to become cool. As a 90s teen, I prefer my jeans dragging on the ground but these are close enough since people wear pee wee Herman floods these days (what have we come to ). Super comfy, nice and stretchy. High wasted —almost a bit much but better than the alternative. 10/10 recommend

Best. Pants. Ever. These are by far my most favorite pants ever!!! I am a teacher but can get away with wearing them to work. They look dressy enough that people don't know they're yoga pants. The fake back pockets are great. I have 2 black and 1 navy. I tried a lighter color but I think it shows too many bumps (cellulite) on the legs and back side. I'm a size 6-8 (135lb) and love the medium fit of these. I get them when they go on sale that way I feel like I'm getting more bang for my buck. They wash great too. Just comfortable and nice looking. I've even worn them out to a nice dinner before with a sweater. They look cute with sneakers (adidas type) or I'll often wear them with black loafers.

Love!! These pants are amazing! At 165 lbs and 5’9”, I usually struggle to find the right length, but these fit like they were made for me. They cinch my waist perfectly.my picky teenage daughter told me she loves the way these fit on me, they are so very flattering,

Pretty comfy; not the most flattering on an apple body type. I've been wearing these pants around the house and out on errands for a couple weeks, and all-in-all, I do enjoy wearing them. I like the wine color. The feel of the fabric isn't particularly high quality (it feels a bit like material I'd expect on a budget swimsuit, but thicker). The fabric is comfortable, but doesn't offer much warmth. I'm 5'8" with long legs, so I'm always excited to see pants with a long/tall inseam option! These pants don't have a gusset sewn in so I wear underwear with them, and the material isn't terribly forgiving as far as Visible Panty Line goes. I do like the relatively large pockets, and I wish there were back pockets, too. As you can see from the photo, these aren't terribly flattering on me, but that's the case with most pants unfortunately. The waistband is comfortable, and with a longer top on, I can still hide my belly. Overall, these pants are pretty average. I got them because of the long inseam option, and I'll continue to wear them, but I think you could likely find something higher quality at a similar price point.

Great for Tall Curvy Ladies!! I'm a 5'11", just over 200lbs, curvy girl. These jeans are fabulous!! The length in the photos are at the original cuffed lenght. If you need something really long to wear with heels they can be unruffled, but have a great tall cuff to be worn with flats.

Tallllll girls - this is what you want ! I am 6ft tall & 145 pounds long torso, long legs! Uncuffed, I would say these are 36-37 inseam and perfect for me with a 2.5 inch heel. Great for business casual or can be dressed up. Machine washable so pretty low maintenance but I did hang them to dry. I’m 5’1 and bought the 28” inseam to wear with shoes.

Nice and long! I've been buying this brand of pants for quite some time now, and if you are tall and can never find pants long enough, you should be buying them too! These fit true to size, and are nice and long. I have a 32 to 34 inch inseam and I opted for the medium 32 inch inseam and they are perfectly to the floor in length. These pants have pockets in the front, and pockets on the back, although the back pockets are quite small. The material has stretch to it and is pretty comfortable. They just pull on. There is not a lot of compression or tummy control at the top, so if you are looking to hide that tummy pooch these pants are not going to do that. If they did, they'd be my favorite.

Super comfortable and look like real pants! ***edited a year postpartum and these are some of my favorite work pants. I'm around 170lbs** The product page is unavailable at time of review, so I can't attest to the monetary value of this product, but I LOVE these pants!!!!! They actually look and feel like a pair of maternity jeggings I have. They have real pockets on the back, so they look like legit pants and not yoga pants (I'm going to try to get away with wearing these suckers to work). The material is a little on the thicker side (not running legging material, thicker than that), but not too sweltering or uncomfortable. I like the boot cut style as well. I feel like this style is more flattering and slimming if you have wider hips (like I do) than the typical "skinny" style that is so popular. These remind me a lot of an active/lounge pair of pants I have from old navy. I like the high rise wait to help tuck in the mom tummy. These are going to be perfect for postpartum wear! The length is great on my as well, not high waters, but not dragging the ground when I have tennis shoes on. For size reference (ignore the preggo belly in the pics...I pulled the waist up so you can see where it's supposed to sit, but not comfy on the bump...will be GREAT once baby comes out for some tummy support): I'm 5'5", currently 185 pounds at 36 weeks pregnant (expect to be around 170 for awhile after baby comes out). I typically wear a Large in work out leggings and these are an XL. I would say these fit true to size. They fit really well on my pregnancy body, which is about 40 pounds heavier than my non preggo body. These will fit great for the first few months postpartum, but then I'd most likely need to size down to a Large. Would absolutely recommend!

Comfortable but Way Too Long I love how comfortable these pants are. They are great for going on long walks, running errands or just lounging around the house. The fabric is thick and stretchy. It's easy to move in these. As for fit, these fit great in every area except for the length. I'm 5'3". I know I'm a on the short side so I expect my pants to be longer. However, these are at least 4 inches too long for me. The only way these will work without me walking on them is to hem them myself which is a hassle. They don't roll well. Overall, I like these and might like them more once I hem them.

I am 5’5 and I got a size 8.5.

GREAT LENGTH FOR TALLER WOMEN BUT YOUR GOING TO NEED A LINT ROLLER! These GoFlex Skechers 3 Pocket High Waisted Pants are Super Comfortable and Great For "Lazy" days or Slounging.. After wearing them all day they did not "slide" down or bunch up around the knees, so they have a good solid stretch. I'm 5'10 180 pounds and the Large fit me just right, even in the length. The Measurements For The Large: 17" Across the waist, 11" Rise and 32" Inseam. The description said 3 pocket but I had a little trouble finding the 3rd pocket, Spoiler Alert: It's sewn inside the waistband near your bellybutton. They're made from 88% Nylon 12% Spandex, moisture wicking material that feels good and moves with you but it seems to attract every dog hair in my house, including the ones on my dogs. If you are an owner of an animal with hair, be prepared to have a lint roller.

This feels great but not for petites I’m thought I might be ok on length, but it’s too long. Im 5’6 and they are dragging. This are super duper comfortable & look like real pants. I’m giving it to my sister, she is 5’8 and they will be absolutely perfect for her. I just wish they would of been a bit shorter. Also I’m between M/L and Large is a bit too big, should of gone with medium.

Tall girl approved - thicker leggings I'm 6'1 and 154lbs. I ordered a size Medium with 32 inch inseam and the fit was perfect. They'll go all the way over my ankles. Maybe just a tiny tiny bit loose in the waist, but I'll see how that goes. The material is thick (thicker than the normal legging), and you could say it has a fleece lining, but it's minimum. Stretchwise i would say: it has a perfect amount of stretch. It is not suuuuper stretchy. Because it is a little thicker. No stitches popped when putting it on, so that's another plus. The color looks like the picture and i think it is indeed suitable for the colder weather, however, i don't have a problem wearing it in hot weather either. It has two deep and stretchy pockets. And one zipper pockets on the side. This comes in very handy. Would I buy this again? Yes! Love these tall-girl approved leggings and hope they'll produce more of them and with more different designs and styles.

Great compression, comfort, and length! I "5 star" liked a different brand I bought **until this pair** came in. THIS DESERVES THE FIVE STARS. It has the compression I like, length, and it's comfortable. Length is sooooo hard to find when your a 6ft tall woman with a footballer's bone structure. We need more size XL/14 length options like this in women's clothing. I will buy another color, for sure.

How sizing fits a 6' 185lb Woman First - I like them!Me: 6'. 185 pounds. Size 12-14. Normally XLSizing: I have gotten several Tallemon products. Their jeans/pants run bigger, but their leggings are more true to size. So I have to size down in their jeans to a Large, but in their leggings I am an XLLength: I always struggle to get bottoms long enough. So I jumped at the 36". But the 36" are actually a bit too long on me! Who knew!!! So I tried the 34" and they fit perfectly. So with this brand don't necessarily try to go longer, they may be too long!Calves: I don't have wide calves. And they felt snug on my calves. Perfect for me. I wonder if you have wide calves if they would be too snug??Material: They feel nice and thicker. The inside is soft. I plan to use them in the winter as normal leggings and under ski clothes instead of thermals (so I can just take off the ski clothes and still be dressed appropriately. They would be too warm to wear inside working out. But perfect for taking a walk outside.Pockets: I am LOVING the pockets! 1 zipper and 2 cell phone sized pockets.Waist: not super high waisted - more mid waisted.In sum: Loving these leggings for the winter - they seem perfect!!!!

Perfect fit for taller person; Warm, soft, comfortable I got these high waisted fleece lined leggings with a 32" inseam (long), medium, in grey. I am a guy and weigh around 165 pounds and am 6'2". I got them because I like warm leggings that actually fit a taller person. Most are noticeably too short for me. For whatever reason, tall womens leggings work well for me. These are very soft, comfortable, and fit me perfectly. I like that they actually have some usable pockets that can hold an actual phone and things. The pockets are deep and one a zipper. I like that they feel a little bit like compression leggings. They are warmer than my regular (not fleece lined) leggings. I am very happy with these and looking forward to wearing them as it gets colder again. I am giving these 5 stars. They fit well and are better quality than many clothes you find online.

Long enough, finally! AFFOLE's Wide Leg Pants and super comfy. I use them for around the house. They just won't be my go-to pair.

Consider sizing down twice for working out I’m really torn, because these are actually super high quality for the price. They don’t lint, they’re silky in an athletic way, the pockets are SUPER deep. They’re genuinely good quality. I sized DOWN to a Large. I am 5’9, 236 pounds, 22 weeks pregnant in these photos. I purposely got 34” inseam because I wanted to wear them over the heel, so that’s not the issue. The issue is normally I wear a L/XL in most things, I chose an L, and I really needed an M. I literally don’t wear Mediums in any clothing ever, so this is a first. I tried to do a workout in these and they just kept sliding down the whole time. That being said, instead of returning these and purchasing from a different brand, the quality is good enough that I’m going to keep these (for later in the pregnancy???) and order a couple Mediums because the quality is there. It’s just odd that I basically need to size down twice. Especially given that I carry much of my weight in my legs. They’re even loose on my legs. It’s odd.

Tall girl friendly Do you know how hard it is to find pants that are actually long enough?!? It’s basically the quest for the Holy Grail, but these SEVEGO 36" inseam leggings have answered my tall-person prayers. They fit as expected, the dark gray color is sleek and versatile, and the material is soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to make me briefly forget I’m actually wearing pants. The high waist is supportive without feeling like a corset, and the deep pockets are a life-changing gift!! I can carry snacks, my phone, and maybe even the remote if I’m too tired to move. Quality-wise, they feel solid and worth the money. Value-wise, also a win! it's basically getting comfort, style, and functionality in one stretchy package. My only complaint is that the back seam sits just a little lower than my waist, which makes me tug at them occasionally, but it’s nothing deal-breaking. Honestly, I’m already planning to buy these in every color and build an army of tall leggings...so I never have to feel betrayed by “capri-length” imposters again. Pros: Fit tall people without accidental ankle exposure, soft and stretchy material, high supportive waist, deep pockets, nice color, good quality, good value. Cons: Back seam rides a little lower than ideal, may cause occasional adjusting.

Functional pockets! I've gained some weight recently and regular jeans just don't work for me anymore. I find them to be too stiff or too tight or too restrictive so I've purchased a couple "jeans" from this brand before because I love how comfortable they are. This pair is no different even though the style is not exactly the same. These "jeans" are not the usual jeggings that are totally fake and look fake (jeans print). They actually look and feel like real jeans but only thinner, softer, and more stretchy. They are pull-on pants with the crossover wrap high waist like yoga pants and flare legs like bell-bottoms. I'm 5'1" and 130lbs and I usually get the shorter 29" inseam so I can wear them with sneakers. These are a little long for me (31" inseam), but I can wear them with boots or other heels. Also, all the pockets are functional and I'm a sucker for pockets!

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

Inseam not 31", comfy, more like leggings than jeans Update: At time of order, sizing included choosing inseam length. It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. A disappointment. The color is so bright and vibrant I love it. These fit everywhere pretty perfect. However, there’s no draw string so the band of the pants don’t stay up every step I take they fall. When I sit or squat down the fall to the point my underwear or butt crack shows. I’m thicker in the hips and butt and I have a smaller waste so having a draw string or a band that fits is a must. I’m 5’7” and 260lbs for reference. I got a 2xl and if I would’ve ordered a smaller size these would’ve been too tight everywhere else. The length is a little shorter than I like too. I’m very disappointed in the overall design and fit of these. As someone who is a very physically demanding medical field these are not it. If you don’t want to be disappointed I wouldn’t buy these.

Perfect boots for rainy days or beach combing! First, the boots arrived faster than promised and were perfectly packaged to survive the rain at my doorstep! I ordered a half size bigger (7) than my shoe size (6.5) so I would have room for a layer or two of warm socks. The fit is roomy but not so much that my foot slides around when I walk. These boots will be perfect for walking the beaches of Northern California where the weather is often damp and chilly. I wanted tall boots to keep lapping waves from splashing inside and these come right up to my knee. I'm only 5'1" with proportionally short legs, so that might not be the case for those with long legs. The sturdy construction and materials look like they will last for many years. The buckles are a little hard to adjust, but I wasn't planning on cinching or loosening each time I pull them on or off. I am very happy with my purchase! *Updated: I saw some negative reviews by people who think their boots weren't genuine Hunter so I included a shot with the official red box and the Hunter stamp on the sole. Mine are the real deal!

Normal size fits perfect I'm confused by the reviews that recommend going a size down. I am between a 7.5 and 8 in all my shoes and ordered an 8 and it fits perfectly. Without socks, yes, it's a bit loose--but I'm not sure why one would not wear socks with boots. The boots are definitely tall so if you're on the shorter side, they may not work for you. I'm 5'8" and the boots come up to just below my knee. (See photos--I took them in shorts so the boot placement in relation to my knees is clear.) They appear authentic and I got them for a great deal. I like the navy blue better than the black (returned a black pair from another seller) and am keeping the navy. Took one star off because they are a bit bulky and fold awkwardly at the ankle when you walk, distorting the shape of the boot (but then again, I'm not sure you can expect anything different from tall, rubber boots). I'm looking forward to trying them out in the fall.

Great Quality! Love the Matte Finish!! Get compliments ALL THE TIME! Fit: I typically wear a 7.5 and I went with a 8 which fit perfectly. I'm 5'2.75 and 110 lbs and the calf opening is perfect. The height of the boots is tall, hit right below my knees. I've had no problems wearing these with jeans, dress pants, no pants etc. Quality: All the seams are really well constructed. The packaging came in the original hunter box and was wrapped very well. Some folks received defective products with odd films on it. I definitely did not have this problem. Brand new, no defects! I've used the rain boots several times in heavy rain and had no issues with leaks or wet socks. Keeps my feet nice and warm and I could care less if I step into a huge puddle! I will say these boots are slightly heavy. I don't think I would walk all day in them. Color: The navy is matte and is a true navy. Not too dark. Pairs really well with pretty much any color rain jacket. Price: I got these for right around 107 which I thought was a good deal for Hunter boots! I've seen several pairs for way more.

Perfect sheer-looking tights for brown girls!! These are exactly what I was hoping for. They are quite warm, comfortable, and stretchy. I live in freezing Ohio, and these provided the warmth and look I was going for. I ordered a medium and I’m about 5’5 150 lbs. I ordered black/brown. The color on the inside looks quite light but once you put them on, they are very brown girl friendly. They look as if I have sheer black tights on and it was perfect.

Anemic Approved! Being anemic I am always on the look for clothes that keeps me warm and comfortable. Usually when winter comes the dresses go away because I couldn't find a legging that was cute AND warm enough for me. The entire night out it felt like I was wearing warm, cozy leggings that offset having all my arms and chest exposed for this body paint and sip event! I am 5'2" 125lbs. I bought the black brown in medium which was kinda loose on me, but I was able to pull them up and they never slipped down. I really appreciated that the skin tone underneath was a good match for my complexion as that is challenging to find. I had no rips or tears and they were easy to hand wash, even withstanding the black light paint that got on them. For the cost (especially if you get them on sale) they were worth every penny and I intend on purchasing a couple more pairs.

Buy them, love them, and convince your friends they need a pair too 😉 Absolute game changer! These look amazing and they are so warm. I have worn them several times now. The fit is amazing. I’m 5’8” and had tried other brands that felt like they weren’t long enough. These fit amazing, are truly high waisted, support your stomach area, and keep you warm. Shoot they had me sweating wearing them in Church on Christmas… but there were a million people there so I can’t blame the tights. When I tell you these go with anything… I mean anything! And other brands I tried had a clear line where the fleece lining stopped so you had to wear socks or boots to cover that area. This is not true with this brand!! Love them- have 2 and will get more!

SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

So soft and perfect for short girls. I’m only 4”11 and it is so hard to find flare leggings for short girls. These are so comfortable and have tummy control. Great Fit! I am so happy with these pants! They fit well, are stretchy, and flattering. I bought 8S and they just touch the floor with no shoes on so they are a tad on longer side than most short length pants but not by too much For reference, I’m 5’2” and 161 lbs (thanks to Xmas treats 🥴), hourglass shape, pretty evenly proportioned w/ weight evenly distributed. Great but lint and fuzz all over after 1st wash First off UA do NOT resell items that have been worn! So gross! I just read that ookie crotch review - what a nightmare! Second, I got two definitely new joggers 🙂 just did my first wash and dry. Lots of lint and fuzz so don’t wash and dry with anything else. The instructions say wash/dry inside out. They also say don't use fabric softener. Perhaps that was my mistake cuz I did. But yeah lots of lint and fuzz and used my lint brush 3x. Pic is the last run through of half a leg. Finally, these are well made, seams and stitching are great. I got a large regular. At a mere 5 feet tall the length is perfect. But I could see some problems if you’re 5’4” and got these. Be much too short. I actually got a pair for my sis who’s 5’3” but returned because I knew they’d be too short. They do shrink, mostly in the waistband. Crossing my fingers they wash up well from here on as I don’t want to have to pull out the lint brush on them again!

Finally, Jeans That Don’t Make Me Look Like a Potato Sack I’m 5’2” and usually buying jeans feels like choosing between tripping over extra fabric or looking like I accidentally borrowed a child’s pair. But these? The “short” length is actually short enough for me—no impromptu denim hemming project required. 🙌 The fabric is a little thicker than what I expected for stretch jeans, but honestly, I’ll take it. They hold everything in place without feeling like I’m wrapped in shrink-wrap. Let’s just say I can bend over without hearing the distant threatening rip sound that some other brands give me. Bonus: they’re well-made, comfy, and flattering. My husband said, “Nice jeans,” which in husband language is basically a standing ovation.

They don’t come in a box, no smell and true thigh highs go a half size or up Let me start of by saying these boots are 🤩 amazing! I read some reviews and my experience is different. They didn’t come in a box 🤔🤨😏😒 I ordered these the same time I ordered another pair of furry boots these were due to come today which is Sunday. They came yesterday a day earlier that’s a win for that company in my book! Any I ordered a size bigger I think. I’m a true 8 but I ordered a 9 (41) and I’m glad. I have fat toes and I tried them on with the thickest sweat socks and my feet felt amazing. They were very easy to put on, the color is beautiful. No smell, they look like are very durable like they will last for years. The sole of the boot reminds me of bear paws 🐾 collection. I was a little disappointed bcuz I thought the fur would would be a lil more kinky. I wasn’t expecting the fur to be kinky straight if that make sense. Other than that, they are true thigh highs and I am sure you will be the only person wearing them where ever you go! I low key wish they had a heel. They are Everything!!! I have to say it again, and the price is well worth it! For ref I’m 5’6.5” and I weigh 151 lbs slightly curvy with muscular tones.

I REALLY like those sweatpants! I am 5'5", 160lbs, (small fupa & not curvy) have 4 pairs. They sit at my waist/belly button & hit right at my ankle. They are warm & comfy durable material. The elastic waistband is strong, has a drawstring, but these thick, long ankle cuffs are awesome to me, a snug fit for a good look. These have been worn & washed a few times each and there is No Pilling, No Color Fading. **I have 1 Varsity Blue, UA is stitched (great, true color), 2 Grey, UA is iron on (true color), 1 Purple, UA stitched on (more of a Pink, still love them). I hope this was to some!

FINALLY!!! I have been searching high and low for something that reminds me of the old cargo pants I used to wear back in the 90s, and these are it. For fit... I am 5' 2", 140lbs, average size 6, with a body somewhere around an athletic build. After some trial and error, I ordered these in an 8, as the 6 was tight on my rear. Once I found my fit in these, the went from meh to great. Pros: The elastic waistband is forgiving for anyone that struggles with bloating/gut issues. The rest of the fabric has some stretch, and I'm able to pull these up and down without unbuttoning. I'd say they're high rise and they don't irritate my sensitive stomach. They can be dressed up or down and work with a variety of tops. They're even good for rigorous hiking. Also. SIX FUNCTIONAL POCKETS! Not those itty-bitty teases they call women's pockets nowadays. We are talking almost on par with men's pockets (but not quite, guess they gotta keep us humble or something). The length for me is good as well. Although I'm vertically challenged, most of my height is in my legs, so I tend to wear a regular inseam. These wash just fine, no fading or bleeding. I can't speak to drying, as I hang dry. Cons: If you are super leggy, these may be too short. If you carry weight in your midsection or rear, size up. They may (?) shrink in the dryer. Overall, I highly recommend these pants. I'm wearing the darker grey in the photos, and I've come back and ordered them in multiple other colors. I basically live in these now. And I like them as much as I do my Le.vi 724s, and that is saying something.

Barrel cut. Not straight cut I’m 5’-8”, 155lbs, usually a size 12. These pants are supposed to be straight cut but they are not. It’s more like a barrel cut with way too much fabric at the hips and generally around the crotch. The first photo is with the extra fabric pulled out. I am not shaped like that. I don’t think anyone is. The fit is weirdly baggy at the upper thighs and more straight cut at the calves. The back side is horrible with extra fabric in all the wrong places. They stretched out to a super baggy fit after working in them for 1 day. Not attractive or flattering.

Some times at work I may have 3 phones, box cutter, markers and a set of keys and it’s no problem with these pants. I bought 2 pair, black and the blue and workmanship on both are excellent. Highly recommend. (no smell)

Comfortable fit, even for my 5'10" height The current style of longer length pants is a welcome trend for 5'10" height, and these pants are no exception. The elastic waistband with drawstring sit just above my natural waist; again, a welcome trend to hide a not-so-trim belly. The wide barrel-style legs come right to my ankles. The vibrant red color is just as the depicted, and the side pockets are a convenient feature. I struggle to describe the fabric, since it isn't the cotton fabric of traditional sweatpants, nor is it polyester; yet, I wouldn't call it a cotton-poly blend. The pants arrived with wrinkles, but those didn't last long. They are machine washable and should be line dried, but I put the new pair in a dryer with a slightly damp towel on low heat, and the wrinkles came out. These sweatpants are very comfortable and I love the way they fit. Since my size varies quite a bit (between 138lbs to 152lbs), I ordered a Large, but could have also gone with a Medium due to their design. My advice is to order your typical size. *If you're tall female who has gibmven up on finding reasonably priced sweatpants, Ihope you give these pants a try!*

Amazing Amazon find I have never worn pants like this and I am in love! I’m 5’3 159 lbs I ordered dark grey XL 27” inseam but didn’t realize they stretch at the waistband therefore are a tad loose. I still loved the fit and added a belt for style. I ordered another color in size L to compare the fit around waist. Although the XL is already extremely comfortable but I do anticipate losing more weight. They are gorgeous!!

Comfortable, soft, true to size These pants are SO comfortable. They feel like a breathable thick legging material that is thick enough to be professional and thin enough to be breathable. I only bought this pair bc I wanted to make sure I like them. Im definitely getting more!
