Not sure but will try.... The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

This is my new favorite lounge set. I wore it 5 days in a row before my husband made a comment about how he hadnt seen me wear anything else in a week. I ordered the pants and matching crew pullover, both fleece lined. Im 50 years old and starting to get regular hot flashes. The inside fleece lining is just a thin layer to soften the texture, but not enough to make me feel overheated. The outside modal fabric is so soft to the touch. Im 5'2" and 140 lbs and I ordered size small for the top and small with a 27 inseam for the pants. Ive washed the set numerous times without any shrinking or fading. Ive noticed some light pilling inside on the fleece fabric on the top/back part of the top. My only complaint would be that the navy color is muted, which makes it look faded. I wouldve preferred a richer, darker navy.

Great lulu dupe I was looking for the “soft and comfy” wide leg dupe pant similar to lulu. Other pants that I had purchased were either too short, or not the exact material I wanted. However these are great! I was looking for more of a wide leg; however I paired these with an oversized sweater and they were perfect. The color was a true cream color which doesn’t look yellow (I feel like other brands always offer a white color option but not many have cream). The pants are a medium thickness (enough to keep you warm) however not too thick where they still have some movement. Lastly, the fabric is the soft lululemon type of material (no sheen). I’m 5’9 and weigh 155-160 depending on the day. I would describe my thighs as thicker/average and I hate when pants are too tight around the thighs, but these weren’t.

Good material, flexible fit. Surprisingly Flattering and High-End Quality! I couldn’t believe how great these jeans are! I’ve always been loyal to high-end denim brands, and these totally exceeded my expectations. Right now I’m in between sizes—I’ve gained some weight but am already on my way back down. I ordered both the size 14 and the 12 in short length. I normally wear a 14 (I’m 5’2” and 180 lbs), and the 14s fit comfortably, but I was shocked to find that the 12s fit too! They were snug, but the material is stretchy, soft, and extremely forgiving—surprisingly comfortable even in the smaller size. Both sizes looked amazing in the mirror. The short length hits just right for me when I’m wearing 3–4 inch heels, which is my go-to look. In these images I’m wearing heels, about 3.5 inches. The jeans graze the floor in a flattering way and have that perfect bootcut silhouette that works beautifully over high-heeled boots. If you look at the product photos, you’ll see they fit like a dream and give that sleek, elongated leg effect. One thing to note: the short length runs a little longer than expected, so I wouldn’t wear them with sandals unless I hem them slightly—but with heels? Perfection. I highly recommend these jeans, especially if you’re curvy and love a high-end look at a great price. I’ll probably keep the size 12 as my motivation jeans, but honestly, both sizes are a win. 👖✨

The G4Free AiryBlend Wide Leg Sweatpants are well made and very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric, it has a nice drape to it that makes the fit very flattering and it doesn’t show every little bump and bulge. The fabric has a very slight shine that elevates the look of these pants. All the seams are neatly finished both inside and out. These sweatpants are a step up from your average sweatpants and to me, they seem more refined and slightly dressier. You could wear these pants anywhere. They are not just for the gym. There is a cinch cord at the waist that works so much better than the usual ties. The cinch cord allows you to quickly and easily adjust the size of the waist and it holds firmly in place. The pants are just the right fit, not too loose or too baggy. I ordered the 31" inseam and the pair I received is actually a 32" inseam. I like that these come in regular and tall sizes because we all know that one size does not fit all.For reference, I am 5’9” and I weigh 195 lbs. I normally wear an XL and I ordered these in an XL. These pants fit me well but the waist does seem a little smaller and tighter than my usual XL sweatpant's waist. But they are still comfortable enough to wear all day. The length for me was perfect and they don’t require any hemming. These sweatpants are well made, look great and feel great.

I got the medium petite, I am about 5'2". I am currently 24 weeks pregnant. I have been struggling to find good maternity scrubs that fit but don't break the bank. I found these because of other reviews talking about using these during pregnancy. These are the best option I have found so far because the waistband goes below the belly, but still stays up. The waistband is stretchy and comfortable. I am hopeful that they will work for the whole pregnancy - fingers crossed. I appreciate the size of all the pockets, although I'm not sure what I would realistically use the waist and pocket for - I think it goes all the way around the waistband, so I would be afraid I would lose anything I put in there. The pants are good quality, the material is lighter-weight which is great because I tend to get hot easily. The fit is good except for the length. Things that would have made it 5 stars for me would be back pockets, shorter pant legs (typically petite works well for me but these were still a little long, even with my shoes).

Moms .. you want these I have been in the healthcare field for 12 years I bought these pants because I’m finally in nursing school and the scrubs that they gave us at the school are terrible they’re just so uncomfortable they are the drawstring unisex scrubs, like the black sheep of all scrubs. I bought these pants and tried them on and in the 12 years that I’ve worked in hospitals these are the MOST COMFORTABLE, MOVABLE, STRETCHY scrub pants I have EVER worn. I have three children, my twins and my baby girl all via C-sections so my scar is very uncomfortable with different pants and I still have a little mom pooch from my last one I hate when scrub pants roll down or when you do find pants that are “high waisted” but are so tight it’s just uncomfortable however these pants stayed in place all 12 hours I wore them and they are extremely stretchy but they stay where they need to be. They definitely are made for the curvy women. I can’t say enough that these pants are the most comfortable pants I have ever worn moving around pulling on patients bending over and squatting down you can move so freely in these pants if I could give these pants 10 stars I would I added pictures to show you how they look and on the pant legs as well. You can see that there’s a drawstring so if you buy them Long they’re definitely long enough to look professional with Dansko‘s or clogs but also if you like joggers you could probably buy them short and pull on the drawstring and they’ll kind of be against your ankle rather than hanging over your shoes (check out my pictures) either way these things are the bomb they are so comfortable I cannot get over how comfortable they are I only ordered one pair for clinical and after wearing them for one day I ended up ordering more and I ended up ordering a bunch for my actual job because I don’t think I’m gonna wear any other pants for work & school! THE ONLY SLIGHT DOWNFALL that some people may find is the material it’s like the polyester material it’s not cotton and I don’t think there’s any cotton except for the waistband I know some people sweat while these pants are good for comfort they might not be the best option if you tend to sweat let’s be honest I’m just gonna say it, if you tend to easily get “swamp booty” then I would recommend wearing 100% cotton panties with these bc honestly the material is so clingy and soft it’s won’t ride up your butt. I’m just keeping it real ladies.All in all I love these pants I’m so glad I ordered definitely 100% worth every penny!!SD: I am 5”10 200Ibs “slim-thick”

A tall girls dream! Height: 5’9 Weight: 130-140ish A DREAM FIT!! I purchased these for a business work conference, wore them for 12 hour days on my feet and the pants were so comfortable! They didn’t over stretch with time, nice high quality fabric… thick enough to hide any underwear lines/cellulite, but cool enough to not overheat you. (Was in Florida) Very cute, and I loved that they are TALL!! As a tall woman I struggle to find pants long enough. If I ever have the need to purchase more work pants- I would buy more!

Not for short girls. I really really really want to love these pants. But they are just not for my body type. I’m about 5’2 120lbs, 34-27-36 with a short torso and long legs. And for me these pants are wayyyy too long. But the worst part is that the torso is really long and I either have to have the waist up to my bra line or have the crotch sagging like the 90’s style Mc. Hammer pants (shown in 3rd to last pic). So I would say that these may be more fitting for those that are curvy and really tall. There were also a few odd strings hanging but over all I feel like the material is good quality and if you are tall and curvy then these will look amazing on you.

Well made lounge pants. These lounge pants are well made. The style looks nice. The material is a 66% polyester/34% cotton blend, that is about a medium thickness. I'm not a fan of the feel of the material. It reminds me a lot of my mom's polyester slacks she wore (and made me wear too!) back in the '70's. This pair of pants is not what I would term as sweatpants. There is no fleece inside. To me they feel like a pair of slacks that got styled to be lounge pants. There is nothing wrong with the quality of these pants. The quality is great. It's just a personal preference for me, because of the way the material feels. I did really like the style/look of them. I ordered them in a size XL, my normal size. The waist fits nicely and is quite comfortable. Plenty of hip room with the bagginess of the legs. However, the length was a little long for my 5' 4" height. They sort of sweep the floor for me. The waistband is wide and elastic with a drawstring for size adjustment as needed. I will recommend these lounge pants as they are great quality for those who like or don't mind the full on polyester feel of the material. They are quite comfortable to wear, though a little long for 5' 4" and shorter if ordering an XL size. There are several colors to choose from. The price of $34.99 (8-31-2025) is a bit on the high end and needs to be lower to make them a good value, in my opinion. I gave them a 4 star rating because they are well made and look nice.

Definitely misjudged size I ordered a size 8 (regular) based off of reviews. After trying them on I should have gone with a 6. The material is very nice and comfortable, not scratchy in any way. Fits well and tall girl approved. I just got these jeans so I might update as I actually start wearing them day to day but so far I like the fit. I am 5’9 174 lbs and I ordered a 14 long. I wear a 12 usually but I’m glad I sized up based on the reviews. The waist does have stretch to it so they’re comfy. The length is perfect for my height, goes to my ankles, not too short or long. I keep trying to wear different silhouettes of jeans to be more current with today’s trends, but my millennial self just seems to look and feel best in skinny jeans. These are definitely reasonably priced as well.

Great for tall and curvy girls!! These pants are AMAZING. The material feels thick, but with some stretch. The waistband is wide enough to not feel too pinchy, and I LOVE how it made my butt look. I’m 5’9, hourglass build with wide hips, a fuller bust, and long legs. I got a size 8 Long, and they fit beautifully. They fall just above the ankle, so if my fellow tall ladies want your ankles covered keep that in mind. They look polished and keep you looking snatched. I would say these fit true to size. I vary between a 6 to a 12 depending on the clothes but usually an 8-10 is where I fit best. I’ll be getting these in more colors! These are great jeans to wear to the office or on a fun night out, and the price point is great for what they are. :) I didn’t notice a smell and I haven’t gotten to wash them yet, but I’ll see how they fare in the washer. Overall though, they get five stars from me. :)

Extremely nice quality, thick and warm These pants are THICK and suuuper soft. Really heavy weight fabric with a smooth exterior and soft fuzzy interior, triple stitched elastic for no rolling, and a nice slightly flared leg. I got the petite size large and they are maybe still an inch too long for me at 4’11”, and the waistband is a tiiiiny bit tight so I probably could have swung an XL for maximum comfort, but overall these are amazing quality and extremely warm and soft and will look great dressed up or down

100% Recommend- Soft Like a Cloud These are soooooo comfy and soft inside. I got the longer length (32” inseam) in XS and they fit perfectly, fitting right on my waist (high-waisted on me). For reference, I am 130lbs and 5’7 and have a shorter torso but longer legs. They are the perfect length to go past my ankles. The side pockets are super comfortable and flattering and the inner pocket lining is made of this silky smooth fabric. The taupe color is more of a muted periwinkle brown which I actually like better. These pants feel really high quality. Something to note though is that the waistband is pretty thick and there is no string to adjust the waist. Make sure to pay attention to the size chart for waist measurements especially. If my experience with these being high-rise is more universal and others also felt these were more high-waisted pants, then it’s all the more reason to make sure to adhere to the size chart for waist size. There are no back pockets but the side pockets are really deep and easily fit a phone and a wallet/ keys. I think the price for these pants is honestly worth it. They feel super high quality and you can tell just based on the seams and stitching alone. I’m honestly keeping an eye out for a matching sweatshirt from them in the same color because I love these so much! 100% recommend.

Compares to Comfrt Brand Bottoms. I am 5'7 146 lbs with a curvy figure. I ordered a medium and the fit perfectly. I compare these to the "comfrt" brand sweats that everyone is wearing. They definitely are comparable with a couple of differences. The first is the sweat material weight. It is slightly and I mean just slightly less thick than the comfrt brand which for me is good since they are not so heavy and hot to wear. It also allows the waistband to be less bulky and sits more comfortably than the comfrt brand. The rise is great! I like that the rise is not too long so you don't have to roll the band down to be comfortable. The sit just above the waistline and are slightly contoured for your hips so less bulk at the hip area. The pockets are nice side slits and are designed not to gap. The pockets are lined with a thinner material so no bulking at the hip area. The length was great for my 5'7 height in medium regulars but they also have petite and tall sizes too. This is the second pair of "G4Free" brand pants I ordered. I also have the thinner drawstring yoga pants which I love and have more on order. This brand is great and the price reflects the quality. If you are sitting on the fence about them just buy them you won't regret it!

Definitely comparable to other name brands. I ordered the taupe in Small and they are perfect. They hit right above the floor with sneakers and drag a tiny bit when I'm barefoot. I'm 5'5", 125 lbs with athletic frame and small hips. These have a relaxed cut but not so baggy that they are unflattering. They're so cozy for lounging at home but have a nice finish that I'm fine wearing out of the house. I've worn them working from home, to physical therapy, and running errands. The pockets are also nice and big so my cell phone fits when I'm chasing my toddler around the house or walking the dog.

Super thick! It’s getting cooler outside, so it’s definitely sweatpants season. I’m 5’6” tall and my measurements are 30 inch waist and 42 inch hip. My inseam is about 32 inches. I ordered a size large and they fit snuggly. The length was perfect. The fleece on the inside is warm and the counting on the outside is very soft. These are exceptionally thick. They’re perfect if you wanna wear a pair of long johns or fleece tights underneath if you live in freezing temperatures. My climate is on the warmer side so I can get away with just wearing these as is. I love the pockets and overall the quality of material. These are super comfortable. They’re a little on the pricey side for a pair of sweats, but I guess we live in times of inflation.

Warm, cozy, and look better than plain sweats. Firstly, review and trust the size chart. I should have ordered a small instead of medium; that’s my fault completely. I’m hoping they’ll shrink a bit after a couple of washes. Besides that, these are soft, warm, thick and flattering. The leg is not too flared, they have just the right width. Quality stitching and fabric. A tiny bit long for me at 5’2”, but they don’t touch the floor, so no hem adjustments will be needed. Generous size pockets. They are definitely sweatpants but with a dressier lounge aesthetic, fine to wear out and about.
Nice fit They feel like great quality jeans. I love how they hug hips and thighs. They fit perfectly. I am 5'2 and prefer the 32 length vs the 30. It was short for me.
Tall girl friendly jeans! I think they were popular lol they fit really well. Love the 36” inseam, tall girl friendly. They wash well! Need more colors!

Pockets broke my heart I am a little on the taller side (5’9”) and have been trying to find jeans that I can wear with heels. It has been a trying time in my life lol. I ordered the 28x36 and found the jeans to be comfortable and long enough to wear with a heel without a platform, although the inseam actually measures 35”. The biggest problem is that the rear pocket placement is really unflattering and makes your butt look like your cheeks are trying to fly off in opposite directions. I can’t get past this so unfortunately I’ll have to return the jeans and continue my search.
Short for tall girls 5’11” 209lbs 14 The pants are made well, seem to fit nice but short without ordering long.

Not the most flattering but pretty cozy My measurement are approximately 41 inch hips, 30.5 inch waist, and pants size 8-10 or 29 depending on the brand. I am kind of in between sizes per their chart and went with large. These don't hug my curves and are somehow a bit loose in the butt but a bit tight across the front hips, causing the pockets to flare. I'm not sure if sizing down would have given me a more flattering fit since they already look tight in the front. I got the 31 inch inseam and at 5'6" find them pooling a bit at the bottom. They're passable for "dress" pants if your office is casual or you're only concerned with meeting the letter of your business casual dress code and not the actual vibe, which is exactly how I picked my work clothes when I had an office job, so I'd wear them to work. Overall I don't find them flattering, but I do think they're very cozy. I'll use them as lounge wear or for my current job in pet care. I laundered in cold water and dried on low heat with no problems.

Nice Quality But Long I'm 5'6", 175# and ordered an XL. These pants are a nice weight -- not too thick. I was concerned because they are described as fleece lined, but it's not a heavy material at all. I love the pockets and think they are of a high quality design and make. I did find them to be extremely long so had to bring them to a seamstress. The fit felt comfortable, but as I look at the rear view, ahem! I would consider them to be winter/fall pants but wouldn't consider them to be particularly warm -- more cozy.

I walk around a very dusty gravel parking lot for my job, and these boots are great because they can just get dirty (and they can easily be rinsed off) and so you don't have to worry about ruining nice shoes. Great pants! I'm in my first year of nursing school, so I decided to try these out. My size varies depending on brand and style, so I got a medium and large to compare. The first two mirror pictures are large, second two medium. I am 5'6-7 and about 165 lbs. on a good day- pretty normal proportions. I also get a bit bloated depending on the time of month, so I took that into consideration as well. The medium fit perfectly- as in it was snug in the right areas and didn't make me feel too constricted. I tried squatting/bending over a few times and there was no movement around the waist or ankles. I hate when my pants fall down throughout the day, so this was definitely a plus. The large was more comfortable in my opinion since I lean more towards baggy clothes in my day-to-day style. However, they fell slightly when I bent/squat and rustled when I walked. They also were not flattering at all. Mediums were the right choice. Both fell right at my ankle bone but of course, the large was baggier. It's just up to preference. Other things to note- Very soft, big pockets, sturdy elastic. However, they could've been a bit stretchier. That's the only downside I've noticed so far. I have not worn these to clinical yet, so we'll see how they hold up.

♥️Cute Red Scrubs! ♥️ Nice scrubs! 5’8”’172 lbs. Large long in the pants, and XL in top. I’m a 38C and broad shoulders. I don’t like tight scrubs, so these sizes fit well on me. Full high waist which I love. These scrubs won’t break your heart! Just get them already!!

Order up 2 sizes if you are plus size I'm 5'3" at 250 lbs and I knew to order up a size. I can wear 1x/2x depending on material and designer. I ordered a 20W petite and a 22W petite. The material was comfortable and the thread count made it very durable. It has the feel of a solid work pant for sure. I love that there are real pockets that are of a good size. However, the 20W did not fit. The 22W did but I had to suck in a bit... Thankfully I am squishy. I recommend anyone who has my body type to order up twice. ( I also bought a shape wear that I was trying on so that's why the one pic has it... News flash, it didn't help)

Great leggings if you can handle the smell... I am 16weeks, bought 2XL, 5'8, 230 pounds and growing. Only reason I'm giving them a 4 star is the god awful smell. My husband said it smelt like bad cigarettes. I think it had a hard chemical smell that gave me a bad headache... but at least it came out in the wash (mostly, not bad now). They are buttery soft, every breathable but still keeps the belly warm. Also pass the husband test. No see-through unless you're touching your toes, but even then he said not unless you are looking directly at your butt trying to see it. I'd say tall girl friendly, and thick girl friendly. My husband said it smelt like bad cigarettes. I think it had a hard chemical smell that gave me a bad headache... but at least it came out in the wash (mostly, not bad now). They are buttery soft, every breathable but still keeps the belly warm. Also pass the husband test. No see-through unless you're touching your toes, but even then he said not unless you are looking directly at your butt trying to see it. I'd say tall girl friendly, and thick girl friendly. They have an extra seem down the leg to give more room for us thick thigh girls. I'll definitely buy again.

So far so good! Update: 6 weeks post c section and I love these more than ever. Feels like a gentle abdominal binder and makes sneezing not hurt - like a little elastic hug lol Let me preface this by saying, I just got this today. Will update if something weird happens in the future 😅 Stats on me in case it helpa anyone else with sizing - im 5ft even. 125 pre pregnancy weight but about 154 right now at 32 weeks pregnant. Usually I wear a size 6 pants. I ordered the small, knowing a wanted something that would give me a little more support, and theyre snug in a supportive way without being uncomfortable. I love it, feels like I'm being held in lol. Every time I sneeze I have to grip my belly or it feels like my abs are going to burst open (yay diastasis recti!) but when I have these on, I don't need to! I imagine they'll also be nice for making me feel held-in after my scheduled c section. Yes, they come nearly to my boobs but I personally prefer that

Super soft, true to size Actually very cute, and extremely soft. For reference I am 5’3”, 130lbs, small fits perfect. Need to roll waist up once to mae length right, but I like doing that anyways. Good value for the money!

The best scrubs! I have tried Figs, Mandala, Greys Anatomy, the list goes on… and none of them compare to these. Those brands fit my body (5’4” and 107 lbs) like a trash bag and I was feeling discouraged that there was a brand out there for me. I took a chance on Med Couture and I’m really glad I did. Stylish Rain Boots That Actually Feel Good to Wear I’m in love with these boots! I’ve been using them for about a month now, and as a full-time RV traveler they’ve quickly become essential. They’re flexible, comfortable, and unlike most rain boots, they don’t flare out awkwardly at the calf. On me (5'2") they fit like tall boots and honestly look super stylish, not clunky at all. The quality is excellent, clearly made to last, and they feel much lighter than traditional rubber boots without sacrificing durability. Cleaning is a breeze, and I really appreciate the thoughtful details like the pull tab and reflective strip. At a fair price, these are a no-brainer if you want rain boots that combine fashion with function. Highly recommend!

Stylish boots and lifetime warranty These rain boots come in separate cloth bags within a plastic bag. The rubber of the boot itself is thick and looks like it will be good at keeping water out. I am 5'3" (160cm) and the length of the boot is about halfway up to my knee. This would be a good length to tuck pants or tights into. I wear a size 6.5 (37) shoe so I went up a size to 7 (38). My toes at this size do not touch the front of the boot when I put them on. It gives enough room so I can wear thick socks to keep warm.The boots also come with a lifetime warranty which is incredibly impressive. There is a card in the bag that has a QR code to scan. Once you scan it you can sign up for the lifetime warranty for the boots. This is a really nice feature, since it isn't winter yet, so I wouldn't know if they worked well or not.I will update my review once the winter season starts and I can try them out in the rain.

Brown boots I love that the heel isn’t too high and the calf actually fits me! Usually there is too much space around my calf and I wanted a boot that looks slimming! I also like where these hit below my knee (I’m 5’3) and they are fairly comfortable! My only concern is how long they will hold up, they are a pretty thin material and definitely doesn’t give the real leather look or feel

Faux PU leather boots fit well and are comfortable for long walks. I ordered these Women’s Knee High Boots Round Toe Chunky Heel Faux Leather Tall Riding Boots with Side Zipper for fall; I thought they would look great under my period costumes, especially my Victorian era riding habit. These boots are made from faux PU leather. The faux leather is flexible and comfortable to wear, but it is a bit shinier than I would prefer. I’m hoping with time these boots will dull slightly, but the very shiny appearance actually detracts from the look to me; they’re so shiny that they look a bit fake. I ordered these in a size 9.5; I usually wear a size 9, but I wanted a bit of extra room since I knew I would wear these in fall and winter. I wanted to be sure I had room for thicker tights or socks. I did buy these mostly for costume purposes, but I knew I’d wear them as regular fashion boots, too. I live in NYC; in winter, when it gets really cold, sometimes I layer thick socks over tights for extra warmth. The 9.5 is just what I wanted; they’re comfortable, with enough room for me to layer tights and even thick wooly socks. The boots have a stacked heel that’s about 3 1/4” tall; it’s sturdy and easy to walk on. The shaft circumference is slightly over 16”; this is bit bigger than I needed, but it allows me to layer tights or even leggings under the boots. My calf muscles are developed from years of fencing and these boots fit me fine with room to spare. I like the rounded toe; both the aesthetic and the fit. So many shoes and boots lately are super log and think even pointy. These boots have a nice, wide toe box; they’re comfortable for long term walking on city streets. The one thing I don’t love is the shiny quality, but since I got these to wear under the skirts of a period riding habit, it actually works out fine. The heel is a bit tall for accuracy, but I’m short (5’2”) and all you really see are the toes peeking out; these are just what I needed for my costume. These come in a square black pasteboard box; suitable for storage or gifting. The boots each have a thin fabric “baggie” wrap to protect them when they aren’t being worn. These also look great with tights and a skirt; they’re perfect for fall and the coming winter. While the heels are too high for me to wear these riding (and they’re a fashion boots, not made for equestrian use) they look great with my jodhpurs and a jacket. I like getting double use from these; they’re not just a costume piece for me. A nice addition to both my costume and personal wardrobe.

Gorgeous, comfy, and well made These boots are beautiful! They’re very clearly not real leather, but I wasn’t expecting them to be, and they’re made really nicely, with neat stitching, clean edges, no visible glue, high quality matching zipper, and unique styling on the heel. The interior is thinly lined in foam so they are very soft and also warm, and the fit is very true to size. I ordered 6 and it’s perfect, honestly possibly too snug of you like to wear thick socks in winter like me. The point is not too pointy and doesn’t squeeze the toes horribly, and the shaft comes up to just under my knee at 4’11”. The thick triangle heel is very stable and comfortable, and the sole is nonslip. My only issue is that the boot shaft is rather tight and doesn’t comfortable zip at the top of my calf. If I force it it will close but it’s uncomfortable at the top. If you have wider calves keep that in mind. Overall very lovely and nice quality faux leather boots for the price, I’m excited to wear them this fall and winter!

Attract pet hair like no one’s business Fit wise these are OK, maybe a little bit small and a little long in the leg. I’m 5’6” and about 200# and an apple. I bought a size 14 regular. I tend to carry a lot of my weight in my belly. The rise on these are just a little bit short for me. They are hitting right at my hip bones and giving me a total muffin top. There’s a decent amount of stretch in the legs, but not really in the waistband. I just took these out of the package and I know I have three dogs, but the bottoms of the pants have attracted like all the pet hair in the house in about 30 seconds. Too many little things for me to keep them so back they go. I will say they make my butt look good though.

Comfy but short I’m 5’8” 140 lbs. these pants are high waisted, and rest a little above my belly button. When I wear it like that, the hem rests above my ankles, which is a little short. I’m pretty sure it’s synthetic, but it still feels breathable and would probably be comfortable in a hot climate. I got these to be comfortable lounge pants, potential dress up pants for a work function, but also work for if I’m stretching or doing light exercise. I’m probably still gonna wear these pants even though they’re a little short.

Ordering half size up As other reviews previously stated, yes the calf size is tight upon arrival. Just like any other riding boot you have to wear them and break them in. I’ve been using my boots 4 weeks now prior to writing this review. The boots slip on without any hesitation, zipper goes up fine and no excessive compression near the calves. I’ve rinsed my boots every time with the hose. No water has leaked in. Overall great boot. I am standing at 5’5, 155 pounds.

Perfect Fall weather pants I love these pants! I ordered them because I needed pants for hiking in the fall and cooler weather. A friend of mine owns a pair similar to this that she has let me borrow, and I really liked them. When I ordered these, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but I’m so glad I took a chance. The size I ordered was medium in the 27.5 length. I currently weigh 132 pounds and I am 5’2”. These pants are a perfect fit for me. A small would’ve been too tight through the thighs and rear end, but I am bottom heavy and have to consider my shape. These pants wick water nicely, but don’t feel plastic or weird. They feel really good and have a fleece liner. They are cozy, warm, but not hot. They are not overly insulated, they are not too thick, they bend and flex well, and don’t look bulky. I think they are flattering. These pants do not have back pockets, but they have nice deep front pockets. I can easily put my phone, keys, etc, in these pockets without a problem. The waistband is wide and feels good. It’s also an elastic waistband with and adjustable string. I feel like the only reason anyone would not like these pants is if they are expecting them to be super warm and insulated. These would not be good for really cold, snowy weather. They would be a good layer for really cold, snowy weather, but on their own, they are not intended for that. If you need these to stay warm and cozy in crisp, cool weather, then these are exactly what you need. I can see myself wearing these to outdoor sporting events, hiking, or just in general when I’m going to be outside a lot during cooler weather. They are so comfortable and would look good with a T-shirt, sweatshirt, or whatever. I would not hesitate to recommend these, and the price is quite reasonable.

Warm, Soft, and Great for Pet Owners—But Check Your Fit! These G Gradual fleece lined pants are high-quality, super warm, and the fabric is a dream for dog owners, pet hair just slides right off. At 5'3" and 160 lbs, I tried the medium in the 27.5" length. The petite length was just right, but I do wish there was a touch more room overall; the fit is a tad snug. If you’re between sizes like me, you might find yourself balancing between a better fit in the waist or the perfect length. The pockets are thoughtful, including a small inner one for keys, which is handy for walking. The fleece lining offers cozy warmth without bulk, making these an excellent choice for chilly days. Just keep in mind: the side pockets can stick out a bit if the fit is snug, so consider sizing up or pairing with longer tops for the best look and comfort.

Incredible price, tts, color -- perfect for larger calves. maybe a better zipper but enough room Beautiful boots that will serve you for many years to come; true to size and particularly if you have larger calves (5 7" 161 lbs but healthy calves), they are supremely roomy. I'm medium foot wide so initially the size 10 was slightly snug on first try however I can tell they will stretch out for my foot. Color is spot on; style is timeless and they are an incredible value for the money. A side note is whether the zipper (medium grade) will hold up however given the give and just right roomy calf room, it probably won't be an issue! At this price point, this is a super comfortable stylish win!

Buy a size down… The waist section runs a bit large Good material, not that stretchy, not that dick. But they are comfortable. They’re very flattering to the curves. Especially the posterior curves. So if you’ve got a little bit in the trunk, and you’ve got a nice round shape to it, and you’ve got some round curvy hips, these are the jeans for you. But I would caution you to check their sizes, and maybe be considering buying a size down as these ran a little bit big. If you are short, you’ll need to hem the bottom of these jeans as they’re a little bit long, but if you’re in the tall range like a 5’7 Ish, These jeans would fit perfect on you. The print is nice, but it’s not as pronounced as it looks in the photos. Overall, this is a good jean. If I could, I would get 4 1/2 stars.

Great for taller full figure women- heavier like work jeans I think I am more of a full figure- I have a butt and hips with a smaller waist. I bought size 18- my normal size these fit perfectly. The denim is more like 90s denim but with some stretch and they are high waisted with a boot cut/flare. They give Y2K vibes. If you are full figured and like high waist boot cut these are for you! I own a farm and really struggle to find a denim that I can work in and still be comfortable- these are perfect. Thicker denim offers more protection, these are comfy for riding or working. They also look nice enough to wear out. They are a great multi use jeans. These are made for taller women, I am 5’7” so not to tall but if I am not in my work boots they drag, I have a 31” inseam ( longer legs with a short torso) if you are taller with hips/ butt these are for you. I can see why shorter women do not like them, or women with small butts find them uncomfortable. If you have a pan fold and a butt these are great- order your true size, the stretch does loosen after a few hours of wearing so if you are in between sizes order down, if you are a smaller figure you will also probably want to order down a size. These are the best jeans I have found for my current size in a long time. My new favorite.

Buy the leggins! I'm a runner, do yoga, lift & gym rat. I have 6 pairs of HeyNuts leggings. These are a 10/10. I will NEVER buy the expensive L brand ever again. Love the fabric but not the pocket in the waist band. I really like this brand bc I’m 5’9 and the pants reach all the way to my ankles. They are super soft and feel like lulu lemon. Amazing lulu dupe! Love!!! I ordered an extra small, dark olive the length 28 inches. I’m 5’6”, lbs. 114, waist 25”, thin legs Xs fits great to my ankles. Better than Crzyoga.

Shapeless and long. It’s fine to go under a hoodie or something like that. It doesn’t lay in a flattering way, it shapeless, overall runs big and a bit too long. I am 5’6” and weigh 158 lbs and got the medium. Maybe a small would have been better. I am retuning it.

Not a flowy beach pant. I am 5’ 5” tall, weigh 122 pounds, waist is 27 inches, hips are 36 inches. I purchased these SEMARO pants in a Small, White color. These pants were more see through than the other pants I tried and the material seemed stiffer, not as flowy as I was looking for and more of a straight-leg pant. They fit my waist and hips well but the length was above my ankle, which I don't care for. The quality seemed good but I didn't care for the material much. Just not what I was looking for and I will be returning.

2025 version is SYNTHETIC! Disgusting! Don't buy! I ordered these last year (2024) and they were fantastic! Ordered a pair of beige ones Aug 2025, and now the fabric is SYNTHETIC! Gross! It is now a mixture of nylon, rayon, (80%), cotton and spandex. They took a good product and destroyed it. These are going straight back. Review from Aug 2024: These are 80% cotton and 20% flax. Really thin which is good for hot weather! It seems like they will hold up. I ordered the dark blue first then the khaki and white. With the khaki and white you need to wear lighter colored underwear but they are not super translucent. Very acceptable in my opinion (I hate see through stuff). I first bought XL but they were way too big. The L fits better... so would say they run big. I'm tall (5'9") and the dark blue ones fit perfect in length! (I rarely find pants long enough). However, the white and khaki ones are almost 1 inch shorter!! Such a disappointment! I will still keep them and just scoot them down lower on my hips, but that discovery was a bit of a disappointment. Before I ordered the white and khaki colors, I washed the blue ones on hot and threw them in the dryer (warm) to see if they would sitting shrink in length - they did not, so that is good. Seems like they must have pre-shrunk the fabric before the pants were sewn. (All manufacturers should do this in my opinion). Let's hope the white and khaki ones don't shrink. (Then they will definitely be too short.)

SIZE UP I have such strong mixed feelings about these pants. I'm 5'8", about 150 pounds, pear shaped, and all legs. I wear size 6 pants and I'm almost always a very solid medium. I work at an outdoor school where the temperatures are in the 20-40s(F) for months. I don't need to win any fashion awards, my main goal is warmth and comfort. I LOVE that these pants come in multiple lengths because that's incredibly hard to find for sweatpants and my legs are long and I need my ankles covered because it's cold. I took the advice of everyone and sized up and bought a large in 34in the first time (black pants in pictures). The waistband, even sitting on my natural waist, is still incredibly tight. You can see in the pictures where it's making my tummy squish out the top. I love the way the rest of the pant fits and the length. I decided to order a second pair, this time an XL in the same 34in length (grey pants in pictures). This waistband fits a lot more comfortably and I can wear it lower on my hips which I find more comfortable, but they are so baggy everywhere else. They are also at least 2 inches longer than the black pair labeled the same length, which I find weird. Another thing I'm not a fan of is the line across the top of the butt. I'm not sure its purpose because it's thick, unflattering, and I can feel it through the pants if I'm not wearing a base layer. Despite the flaws, they are thick and warm so I'm keeping both.

user_comment: Agree with all the tall girls . . LOVE them As another reviewer said, finally sweats that are long enough and don't come from the men's department. They are plenty long and don't have extra room for a package I don't have. I am 5'11", 170 lbs. with an athletic build. I got an XL, with the 34 in. inseam. I meant to order the 36 in. inseam as much of my height comes from my legs but as you can see these are plenty long. The pictures were taken after the sweats had been washed and dried. Although the tag says not to put in the dryer, I did and they came out fine. I tend to wear my pants down on my hips a little probably from a lifetime of not having pants otherwise long enough and you can see that the drawstring remains in the original position so they fit very comfortably around the waist. I didn't know what to expect with all of the reviews having contradictory information on sizing. They are super soft and kept me warm walking outdoors in freezing temps (that's not hyperbole the temperatures were below freezing and my legs didn't even get chilled after walking nearly a mile). My dog who oversaw the making of this review thinks they look really comfortable and will be great for many long walks.

Comfy, cute, but super thin fabric! Adorable print...on my undies!! As noted by other reviewers, this is such an adorable dress, but the fabric is way too thin! I tried it on with floral underwear and could see the full print. Even if I wear beige under garments, the pockets show thru, again highlighting just how thin the fabric is. I ordered the white floral to layer with my denim jacket, but I'll have to use it as a sun dress at home. Perhaps a print in the darker colors would be more forgiving. On the plus side, I do love the fit and flare cut which is perfect on my 5' 5" 140 lb frame. The fabric is light without the cheap clingy feel of some poly blends. The seams are well sewn and I really like the pleats across the bodice for added interest. Overall, this is a cute, comfy dress at a nice price. I would just shy away from the white...

Perfect dress for travel! I travel out of the country quite often as a a volunteer for different organizations and perform various tasks from cleaning out dog pens for an animal rescue to teaching English language classes for a remote village, so wardrobe choice is essential for these trips! But regardless of the task, location or weather, there is always a need for a nice, simple, lightweight, wash and wear type dress and I am pleased to say this item fits the bill. As far as size, I am 5’-5”, 135 lbs, 34B/C and the small fit perfectly. It has a high rounded neckline and flutter sleeves, as well as 3 lines of pleats across the bust that give the garment a bit of flair. The dress curves in to form a defined waist before it cuts back out to a flowy A-line that ends mid-calf. All the seams appear to be tight without loose threads and is overall a well made garment. The material is a blend of lightweight 95% poly and 5% spandex which gives it a bit of stretch to be able to go over the head and avoid buttons or zippers, so it’s easy on/easy off and is wrinkle free for the most part. The pattern is on the front side of the fabric only with the backside being plain white. I chose a darker color in case the material was see through and, from what I’ve seen in other reviews, it was a good choice. I am extremely happy with the dress and it will be the perfect lightweight “nice” dress for almost any occasion while traveling.

Very comfortable, washer friendly, wrinkle resistant I am so happy with this dress. It is the very popular 95% polyester 5% spandex blend. This fabric feels soft, it flows well, and it has enough stretch be comfortable. It is well-made with no weak seams or loose threads. It has side pockets which I find a real plus. I am 5' 2" and the length is about mid-calf for me. The a-line style is comfortable and forgiving in all the right places. I really appreciate the horizontal tucks across the bodice that elevate the look to something more fashionable than a basic tee-shirt dress. I chose the 01 blue floral pattern which was my favorite of many color options. I think it will look very smart paired with a white linen jacket this spring. You could even add a belt to change up the look. I am sure this will be one of my favorites this spring and summer.

Almost didn’t order this - SO GLAD I DID!!! Funny story - I almost don’t even order this because I thought for this price point it was going to be poor material, wouldn’t be flattering, and would be cheaply made. I am so glad I took a chance!!! Not only is this better than I expected, I plan to buy more in other colors/patterns! The fit of this dress is amazing. It is true to size (I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 169# 38C body) and I feel like I am quite comfortable in it. The length is really nice. The detail on the chest really adds a nice touch that prevents this from just being a t-shirt dress. I am actually already planning to put a nice jacket over this and wear it to a work event coming up! I travel nearly weekly for work, so the fact that this also was not at all wrinkled when I opened it is huge. I always am on the lookout for things that lack and travel well. The color and the pattern were actually better than I expected based on the photo in the advertisement. I seriously could not be happier with it!

Pregnancy pant staple! I’d stay pregnant forever if it meant I could wear these pants every day. They’re very flattering, extremely comfortable and easy to dress up or down. The only downside is they don’t come in more colors! I’m 5’4” and ordered the regular length and it’s perfect

Cute boot but…. I’m usually a 9 in boots but I got a 8.5. It still fit but could be a bit snug if your not use to fitted shoes. The heel was a perfect hight as I’m 5’4 very plump with short legs, I hate walking in heels lol. Only con- the fur is very cheap (like a stuffed animal from the .99 cent store) & came slightly madden but that can be brushed out. Nonetheless, I needed a quick boot and it did the job.

Not for shorter individuals or my body type! I had high hopes for this dress, but unfortunately it didn’t work for me. I often struggle to find dresses that aren’t too long, as I’m only 5’1” and weigh 185 pounds. On me, the dress felt unflattering and frumpy. I asked my sister to try it on instead—she’s the one shown in the photos. She is 5’4” and weighs roughly 170 pounds. Even on her, the dress still feels quite long, which leads me to believe it isn’t well-suited for shorter individuals. Overall, I didn’t find the dress flattering for my body type. For reference, this dress is an XL, while I usually wear a 2XL.

Super Airy and Comfortable but Fit Was a Little Off These pants are really nice looking. The wine color is even prettier in person and the fabric is so lightweight and comfortable. They are perfect for travel or lounging at home since they are almost unbelievably airy. The front seam makes my legs look longer and I love that it has pockets. I am 5’6” and 145 lbs and got the Medium with the 29” inseam. The fit confused me a bit because the waistband was not snug enough, and the pants felt a little baggy and long. I honestly can’t tell if a shorter inseam would fix it or if the slightly loose waistband was the main issue. The chart suggested 27” for my height, and I am wondering now if I probably should have chosen that. They are beautiful and super comfy. I just wish the fit was a bit better on me.

Soft, light, stylish, and LONG! Actually had a hard time getting a picture of these because I tried them on as soon as they arrived and then wore them around the house for like 3 days straight! Being 5'8" I got the 31" inseam and wow... I'm not used to having a bit of pants pooled around the ankles! I've already worn these casually around the house, out on a weekend, and even to the gym once! The material is like REI sport apparel quality... very lightweight and flowy, with all directions stretch, and the length is perfect. Super flattering. With the stylish pleat down the front, I may even be able to style these for office wear! Easy to wash. Dries super fast for hand washing during travel, and packs/unpacks with NO wrinkles. Basically, the perfect all around pants.

Thin material but flattering fit Very comfortable, I am about 5 foot seven and 140 pounds wearing a size medium and the fit is great. however, the material is not the best quality. The stitching doesn’t seem very strong and unfortunately does not have pockets. The chest material is quite thin so you need to wear a bra with it. Wish it had built-in padding because the back is quite low, making it difficult to find a bra that fits with it. Overall, the jumpsuit is cute and flattering for the price.

The softest modal sweatpants, surprisingly short I looooved these modal sweatpants from G4Free. The fabric is dreamy soft, like butter. The legs are straight but slightly wide. Slant pockets. The ribbed waistband is adjustable with a drawstring. These pants had everything I could have dreamed about except length. I am 6 feet tall and my inseam is 33-34 inches and the tall version of these pants have a 31 inch inseam, which in my opinion is not considered a size “tall”. So tall ladies beware. On me, these pants are ankle pants at best. Length disappointment aside, they are so well made and the fabric so luxurious. If you don’t have really long legs, you will be pleased with these sweatpants from G4Free. If they offered a 33 inch inseam, I’d be right on it.

Amazing Pants! But Lengths Vary. Bought 5 Pairs PROS: The pants drape beautifully and dont wrinkle! I bought 5 pairs different colors. They're heavy enough to hold their shape and cover my bulges (unlike other wide pleated pants), and they're light enough to wear in spring, summer, or fall. Second, they're so comfortable! I could live in them. Third, they dress up or down. I think they would enhance any body type. I ordered one size up because I didnt want any "pull" across heavy areas, and these delivered! CONS: First thing: I had to hem all 5 pairs (these pics are after hemming). For reference, I'm 5'6" but wear 2-3" heels or platform sneekers. The Burgundy and Light Pink pants were 3" too long; Green and Beige were 2" too long; Black were 1" too long. But easy to hem. Second, these are all 100% synthetic fabric, no cotton. But they tumble dry or dry clean or line dry equally nicely (the tag says no tumbling dry but it's no problem) and no wrinkles--or if you get a couple, theyre easy to steam. Last but not least, they're very high waisted, but can ride lower when hemmed, as pictured. Overall, fantastic purchase! A little pricey but worth it. :) Great for mix and match!

Great pants! Very nice fit at the waist and flowy wide leg. Good weight to them, almost like you'd expect from a suede material. I'm 5'7" and the length hits the bottom of my shoe. Perfection.

Dress up Dress down. Just buy them 5'6" 115-118 lbs. (depending on the day) ordered a size 4 (normal size). These pants are made for us long legged ladies. I love when I can find a pair of pants that hits the floor or even the top of any of my shoes, flats or heels, which you can wear both with these pants. They are high waisted, as in right at the belly button (I have very long legs with a short torso), slight pleat in the front (great for hiding the perimenopause pooch), elastic waist band in the back. I own them in five different colors, grey, black, khaki, green and oatmeal. They can be dressed upped for the office or dressed down for casual were (yes they are that comfy to wear). I love the cargo, baggy look of these pants. I did take one star off, because the sizing/length is not consistant. I actually had to have the oatmeal colored ones hemmed by 1" because they were actually too long (surprise)! I am excited to order more.

Stop looking and buy These are so good! The quality fit and style are on trend to dress up or down and can even be worn with high boots underneath. Each time I wear them I get compliments. I am between a small and a medium (5’7, 128) and went with the medium for a more baggy fit. They washed nicely although I do hang them up to dry. They are super comfy. Now I want to order the olive green, because they are that good. Highly recommend.

The grey color is definitely cut differently I love these pants for the price. They are lightweight and comfy. For reference I am 5’8”, 135 lbs. I typically wear a size 6. Sometimes a 4. I ordered the 6 and they are perfect in the waist. The length is absolutely perfect in the other 3 colors that I ordered, the grey pair however is slightly more snug in the waist and significantly shorter.

4 stars but returning due to size My sizing basics, to start: I am apple/heart-shaped (broad shoulders) Height 5'6" (170 cm) Weight 125 lbs (about 9 st.) Breast 36" (B-cup) (91 cm) Waist 30" (76 cm) Hips 36" (94 cm) I bought two variations: Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean, Madison wash, 6 average and Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Classic High Rise Tapered Jean, Portland Wash, 4 Average They were both too large for me. I could pull both of them up over my hips while they were zipped and buttoned, so that means wayyy too big. I'm not remarkably sized in any area except my shoulders, so I was a bit shocked at how large these were on me. The cut was tapered, supposedly, but still really wide at the calves. Size 4 picture attached. I didn't take a photo of the 6 before returning. The material wasn't terribly heavy, but lightweight denim due to the stretch capability (which I hate but can hardly ever find any that don't have elastane or similar materials). Stitching seemed well made.

Fit great and super comfortable I really like these jeans. Great fit and they’re very comfortable. I’m going to share my stats because it really helps me when other women share when I am trying to find my size online. I don’t understand why all women won’t just share this to help others out but I hope this can help someone find their true fit. I am just a hair over 5’5 and this morning the scale read 175lbs. My weight DOES fluctuate but I could see these fitting well up to 180ish or down to 160ish pounds as well. I got a size large and they are perfect for my body right now

Comfy cute Ladies if u have no butt these aren’t for u. Tired of reading reviews complaining about too much room in crotch etc.. I am 5’3” 125lbs I have a 39” butt which is apparently larger than average ( buy old fashion industry standards) for a 27” waisted woman .. amazon recommended medium but ignore that.. Small fits as expected usually a size 3 junior.. 2/4 women depending on stretch. These aren’t “amazing “ BUT comfy fit , hides my celly.. if u know what I mean and cute enough they compare to pennys size 3 Ana approach jeggings so taking star away for average generic pockets and price should be closer to $25 imo.. If u want to look like the selling picture got to wear heels, hike up as far as you can.. and get someone to take a picture of your butt at butt height angle lol…
Good length for Petites Love the color, stretch and the way it accentuates my curves. I am 5 feet and 157 lbs. Fabric is light denim, which I prefer. I’ll be buying other colors

Top quality and great details I am not a member of the medical community, but I have been replacing some of my leggings with scrubs because of the comfort. And these by Med Couture are my favorites. They are an updated straight legged style. The details on these scrubs really dress them up.On the practical side. They are fairly lightweight, but I am not cold in them. These scrubs have 3% spandex so there is good stretch and range of motion. I was thrilled to be able to order them in a petite length. I am 5 feet tall, 110 pounds and the split hem skims perfectly over the top of my shoes. The small fit true to size. I ordered them in the color pewter and love this dark shade because it compliments many of my tops. There are six pockets, with the front ones being roomy enough to handle a cell phone. I really like the double side seams, the split hem and ribbed fabric on front pocket. These special touches make these scrubs a cut above the rest for me! I want more and highly recommend!

I couldn’t be happier. Awesome! Truly amazing — Amazon pants have never fit me right, but I kept trying, and today I finally found the pair. These are the best pants — they look great, even when I’m bloated, and you’d never know it! After a major weight gain, nothing seemed to look right… but these do! For reference, I’m 5’7” and about 170 lbs.

Soft fabric with Pockets These are very comfortable to wear and the pockets are deep and soft on the inside. They hug to your middle and behind but are loose fitting on the legs, like dress pants would be. The waist is comfortable, and not as tight as some other yoga pants I own. The fabric has stretch to it. It is true to sizing. Only note is the length of the pants. I chose the 31" inseam for up to 5'8" and the pants are a little long. I think with shoes or boots they will be fine. For ref. I am 5'7" 145# and the Med fit me perfectly.

Gorgeous! TTS! Beautiful embroidery, half zipper, chunky heel, non slip. A real winner. Oh. My. Gosh! These are gorgeous boots! First off let me tell you that I’m NOT a cowboy boot girl but I am a boot and shoe lover and I own dozens of each and when I saw these beauties on Amazon I had to try them. Well, these are so wonderful that I’m now a cowgirl boot girl! Let me tell you about why I fell for these upon trying them on and walking in them: - they’re of a thick suede like material that’s sturdy yet soft and flexible. - the inner fabric is soft and easy against my skin. - their cushiony insole is Oh-So comfortable so I can wear them all day with no achy feet. - the chunky block heel is super cool! - the outsole is non slip making them safe for winter wear. - the half zipper makes them super easy to slip on and off. - the beautiful embroidered design is strikingly attractive and eye catching. - they come in multiple gorgeous, feminine colors so I’ll get a few more pair to go with different outfits. I chose the brown and this color is more of a dark tan that’s extremely appealing and that goes with everything. - they fit true to size and the footbed is wide enough that you don’t need to size up to accommodate the softly pointed toe. Note in Sizing: don’t size up. I normally wear 7.5 or 8 in shoes and boots and I sized up to an 8.5 because of the pointed toe but they’re roomy on me so I didn’t need to size up the 8 would fit much better. I’ll just put a heel pad in these ones so they’ll fit better. Note: If there’s one, and only one, thing that is off about these boots it’s their shaft height, it’s very tall. I’m 5’ 4”and these boots come all the way up to cover my knee. Since they have a curve at front and back I can bend my knees comfortably while wearing them but I’d much prefer them to hit at the bottom of my knee. Since these have so many other really great benefits and since I can easily bend my knees in them I’ll deal with the height.

True inseam, but waist runs large – returned due to sizing I ordered these pants following the size chart and still ended up returning them due to fit. My waist measures 35 inches and my hips 41 inches, so I ordered XL with a 27-inch inseam (I’m 5’5”). The inseam length was perfect for my height. However, the waist ran large. Laid flat, the waist measures 17.4 inches, which resulted in a loose fit at the waist, as shown in my video. In the video I misspoke about my height 😅 but the measurements shown are accurate. I really liked the blue color, and the modal fabric feels very soft against the skin. The fabric type matches exactly what’s described in the product listing. It also has two side pockets, which make it easy to style either casually or in a more polished, dressier way. I returned them due to the waist fit, not the quality. I will definitely reorder them in one or two sizes down, as the length and fabric worked well for me. CONCLUSION Great fabric, color, and versatility, but the waist runs large. I recommend checking measurements carefully before ordering. I hope this review helps you decide.

Nice set! I love these pants/these sets, just didn’t love the Khaki color on me. I’m 5’0 115lbs (smaller waist, hold my weight in my legs and hips) and the XS 27inch inseam was perfect. I’m also wearing the shirt in an XS. I returned and am going to order a different color. But the quality is incredible and so soft!

Great Fit, Nice Color – Just Wish “Short” Was Shorter! These pants are super flattering and comfortable for everyday wear or work. The Summer Tan color is a beautiful neutral that’s easy to pair with almost any top. The fabric feels sturdy but has a nice stretch, so they move with you without losing their shape throughout the day. I ordered a 14 Short, and while they fit great in the waist and hips, they’re still just a bit long for my height (I’m around 5’0”). I’ll probably get them hemmed ( they are tucked under in the photo) for a perfect length, but I still really like the slim, tailored look. They give that “put-together” vibe without feeling stiff or uncomfortable. If you’re in between lengths or on the shorter side like me, just be aware you might need a slight adjustment. Still a great buy overall—I’d definitely recommend them!

Super comfortable on stomach These are my favorite pants. They are warm, wide-legged, and super comfortable. They are stylish too because wide pants are trending. I got these a few months ago when they were $31 but I noticed the price went up. I would say it was worth the $30. I might buy another if there is another sale. After washing, the quality is still the same! For reference I’m 5’1 and ~110 pounds! They are perfect length. Good for ~50 F weather!

5'3, 120lbs, waist 26', hip 37', ordered khaki, size small with 31' inseam. They have a beautiful, shapely fit without being too tight. I love the khaki color and because they are fleece lined, they are not see-through. Edit: After loving the khaki colored pants, I ordered them in black. Very disappointing. They came with an elastic band sewn into the top of the waist band, which is a non-negotiable for me. I believe pants that are expertly tailored do not need elastic bands around the stomach. Also, the black ones came a full inch shorter than the khaki ones despite ordering the 31' inseam. The khaki pants' label has the 31' inseam length printed on the label, the black pants' label doesnt even list the inseam length. I hope the company goes back to the waistband-free version and gets their inseams figured out. To conclude, the khaki colored pants are all I've been wearing this winter. I have many items by G4Free, they are a 10/10 brand, but the black version of these pants did not work and needed to be returned.

29" are too short for 5'7", 32" are a bit long I am 5'7", and the 29" (blue) are a bit short for me, at least to wear with sneakers. The 32" alternative (wine red in the picture) were initually too long, and I ended up folding the bottoms before the first wash. For my legs, 30-31" would be ideal. If you plan to wear these pants for yoga practice, then the 29" is good length. I think after a warm wash the 32" wine pants became OK, still a tiny bit long, but don't require folding anymore and don't sweep the ground, just touch it in sneakers. The pants are pretty thick and a bit compressing, comparing to a lighter version I have from another brand. Love the shape of the front, and deep side pockets. The wine color works great with blue (bright, light and sky), or pink/light purple.

Nice, cool pants but longer than advertised My husband and I have some upcoming travel plans that will take us to some areas that will have temps in the upper 90’s… too hot! I’ve been looking for clothing that is not only cool but lightweight and wrinkle-resistant. These pants are perfect!!I’m a former ICU/ER nurse and had to wear scrubs daily at work. They were always comfortable, but it never occurred to me, now that I’m retired, to purchase scrubs for daily wear. These were really a revelation for me! Very neat, no ironing required, lightweight, and multiple pockets, what more could a girl ask for?These have an elastic waist that is very forgiving, as well as a drawstring for reinforcement. The fabric is not see through, but is rather thin, so although there are multiple pockets, I wouldn’t try to stuff them with bulky items. A really nice detail is the small slit in the outer seam at the bottom edges, it really makes the pants look nice. These are available in petite, regular and tall. I have a 32” inseam, so ordered the Tall size. Although the “Tall” states it is 32.5” inseam but measures 33.25”. I have no problem hemming them up an inch but just an FYI. I’m just delighted that I can find pants long enough for me.I am thrilled to have found these but must still deduct one star for the length.I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

Great fit; comfortable house pants I don’t use scrubs at work but I used to and remember how comfortable they were, so I ordered this for casual pants. They are perfect for wearing around the house and then going outside or out for a quick errand without having to change. The color is pretty and they are nicely fitted. They are not really relaxed-fit pants but not tight either.I am 5”7” and about 130 pounds and the small size fit me just right. If I weighed a bit more, a medium would probably be more comfortable.Easy care! They did great in the washer and dryer.

4.5 Stars Comfortable and the perfect amount of tall I am 5'4" and the boots reach right at the knee which is exactly what I was looking for. The only reason I gave it 4.5 is because they run slightly big. I ordered my regular size (9) and they fit big in the foot area, but perfect around the calves area. I ordered half a size smaller 8.5 and they were perfect in the foot area, but they did not zip up in the calves area. I ended up keeping the size 9 because the width in the calves area is a few millimeters biggers allowing me to zip them up . I included pictures of the differences between my regular size of boots and these.

Comfy and attractive, but light khaki highlights lumps and bumps Overall, these are great pants (and attractive enough that they definitely don’t need to be relegated to lounge pants)! They are very comfortable and the cut is flattering. At 5’2” and 110lbs, the 28” inseam fits perfectly. Having said that, I do have some lumps and bumps in my upper thighs that unfortunately the material follows perfectly, highlighting them and making me feel self conscious (however, a nice pair of slip shorts solves this problem). I think a darker color would prevent this and the pants are so flattering otherwise I plan to order a darker color to try it!

Great fashion/costume boot This is a great pair of boots to wear as part of a costume or for fashion. They are made of faux leather so I’m not sure how confident I am that they could be used for riding as stated in the listing without damaging the finish. It will be interesting to see how the finish on the heel holds up as well. I can’t tell if it’s a solid piece or a veneer but it’s some kind of plastic/resin. As a bonus, they come in a really nice box if you want to give them to somebody. That being said, these are truly knee high boots. I am 5’7” tall and am more torso than leg. I usually have to buy petite jeans. These boots go all the way up to my knees. I also have large wide feet. I got a size 11 and was skeptical when I saw how narrow the toe was. But my feet went in easily and the boots were comfortable. The heel was also higher than I normally wear, but it did not put too much pressure on my toes. I could definitely wear these going out, but I don’t think I could survive a whole day in them. The boots are comfortable and lightweight so it is easy to move around. The stitching for the design, inside of the boot, and around the soul are all good quality. My only real issue is that even though I have the boots pulled up all the way, there is still some sagging between my ankle in the middle of my calf. The top of the boots are snug around the top of my calves. I will post a couple of pictures to show what I’m talking about. I did fill them with bubble wrap to try and smooth out those spots while they sit in my closet. Otherwise I like the style and cut of the boots. Price wise I think these are a good value for what you’re getting. I had an old pair of knee-high boots that I had for over a decade and they finally bit the dust so I was happy to come upon these.

Stylish & Comfortable Value: 8/10 Quality: 10/10 Material: man-made Color: dark brown Size: I ordered up a 1/2 size for socks, perfect fit Step into style with these man-made, dark brown cowboy boots. They're like the trusty steed of footwear—stylish, comfortable, and always ready for an adventure! The block heel and slightly cushioned insole ensure all-day comfort. And let’s talk about synthetic material: easy to clean and maintain, these boots laugh in the face of mud puddles and rainstorms. Slipped them on with ease, though they might be snug for those with athletic calves. Standing at 5’2”, I found these boots hit just below my knees—making them my go-to rain boots with a twist. Are they worth the splurge? Time will tell, but they're a great leather alternative for bad weather! I’m eyeing the pink version of these bad weather beauties.

label says petite Update: i returned them. kept falling down. not sure what "7/8 petite" means on the label of the pic i provided. But they were not made for my tall body. Update: i have only washed them, air dried. i want to say that if you're taller than 5'9, i would advise against the 27 in inseam. the crotch keeps falling down between my thighs. i will get the shorter inseam ones next time. i thought i needed the longer length, but honestly, the more i move, the more they pull down. i understand that may be due to the size so i will get an xxl next. I am 5'9, 197lbs. I have long legs and a short torso. My waist size is 38 in, my hips are 45 in. I bought the XL 27 in inseam one in black. Not see through. Opaque. I am super happy that they are not thin as skin like some other brands out there. I love that they are actual leggings, instead of colored nylons. They don't compress the fat around my knees, but there is a slight compression around my middle. They come up past my bellybutton and still reach my ankles. They don't unfold at the waist when i sit. I don't work out i just wanted a nice pair of leggings. The pockets are slim fitted to the thighs so no baggy-ness. They fit me very nice. IF i had went by their size chart, i would've got an xxl. But i decided to go down a size for the compression, and they are very comfortable and i am happy i did. i am not a paid influencer and i didn't receive these as a promotion. Just a real 46 yr old mom of 3. :)

Actually fit My stats: almost 70y/o, 5’4”, 112lbs, waist 27”, hips 38”, inseam 29”. I bought size 6 with a 30” inseam. These fit my body type well. The fabric is not very thick like jeans with a higher % of cotton. I’m assuming like all jeans with a synthetic/cotton fabric that they will stretch out as the day progresses. I will wear an elastic belt to keep them fitting as this occurs. They are standard height waist, not high waisted as described. The waistband comes even with my hips. I haven’t washed them yet but I think they are very nice. If they shrink, it’s nothing that a few squats won’t fix by stretching them out. I am happy with my purchase.

My Go To Jeans, Very Pleased! Read All - I am very happy with how these jean fit. I was not sure what size to order, as my weight has been fluctuating. I usually were a size 12 in most pants, I weight 164 and I am 5'5". When I looked at the size chart and checked with another pair of wranglers I already own and still fit at size10W, I went with the 10W 30L. This is my first pair of Wrangler jeans, and I decided to try them because of the reviews and as they were a bargain. I had been fairly confident they would fit after examining their sizing, but was still surprised to feel for myself that they indeed fit like a glove. I'm 165cm, weigh about 70kg, and with a 30-31" waist and 41-42" hips. Many of the jeans I own (mostly Levi's, a few pairs of Uniqlo) fit one or the other. With stretchy material the fit is better, but I always feel that the fit is slightly off. These Wranglers wowed me after I tried them on. They are a true high waist, which helps to disguise my midsection, and are snug but comfortable in the hips, even when sitting down. I've ordered three more pairs in the same measurements as they were all on sale, and look forward to receiving them. I'll probably try more Wranglers in future as well due to how well this fits, and how much I like the way I look when I'm in them.

Sizing is interesting Sizing on these was really difficult. It took a few tries to figure it out so hopefully I can help someone. After reading about how raw denim works, it is best to choose a size that is slightly tight along your body.Because as you wear them, it will conform to your body.And stretch about a half an inch. So keep that in mind when you are looking at the size chart.You may want to go smaller I am 3 month postpartum looking for pants, leaving me somewhat squishy in the middle leaving me at a 31 waist and 42 hip. When I tried the size 11 to my measurements they where waaay too big. I tried the 9, still not good. Left crotch saggy. So I decided to skip the seven and went to the 5. I like the fit in the legs and honestly I think I will get them to fit great after constant wears, just a lil tight in the waist but I think if I get them wet amd wear them around I can fix that. I think the moral of the story is I woild size down 1 or even 2 sizes if you like your pants to fit tighter and not have a baggy crotch. This is coming from someone tall without alot of booty so If you are more curvy you may have a different experience but if u look like me size down. In the end u really like them and I think they will last me a long time

Not the color I ordered. Update please read I like the way these jeans fit they are made of cotton and they do not have any stretch. They are like the kind I wore back in the day. They are made to last.. But I ordered the lite color and obviously I was sent the wrong color these are much darker. I really wanted the lighter color for an event I was going to but now it’s too late. I’m not sure if I want to send them back or not because these kind of pants are hard to find. Plus who knows if I would get the color I want even though I couldn’t get them in time when I needed them. I took too stars off because I was sent the wrong color. Other than that I really like the jeans. For reference I’m about 5”9 and weigh 130 pounds I ordered the size 3-36 and they fit perfectly. Update… I decided to keep the one that they sent me that was the Wong color. I tried again and I got the right color this time 😁😁 but these don’t fit the same as the darker ones. Comfortable, thin, breathable These are super comfy, stretchy, light weight, and breathable. Wish these had back pockets, but do like that side front pockets are a good size and have zippers. The fabric is thin and will reveal the shape of whatever underwear you are wearing.
