Joes jeans look for the fraction of the price! Ok so Amazon knocked it out of the park with these, truly, and I’m SO confused by all the bad reviews! 🤷‍♀️ I know all bodies are different but for me these are PERFECTION. 🙌🏻🙌🏻 They totally look and fit exactly like a pair of Joes jeans boyfriend cut I used to have and LOVED. But I ended up giving them away after I lost weight and they became too big. I’m a Torrid size 2 on top, and apple shaped. I wear a 33/16 in Abercrombie and Levi’s, sometimes a 14 if they have stretch. I ordered a 16 in these and they’re beyond perfect. Slimming through the top half and the legs are perfectly tapered and loose, but not too loose. My legs legit look a mile long in these! 😲😍 I don’t even normally like high waist jeans due to being very short waisted, but the high rise on these just ends up being uber flattering and slimming. I’m 5’6 and they’re about an inch long on me, but nothing a low heel can’t fix. The denim itself is pretty stretchy… stretchier than Abercrombies imo, and MUCH softer. The ‘medium blue vintage’ wash that I got is one that goes with everything in your closet. I LOVE, love these. I own Agoldes, Good American, Buckle Black to name a few and these are better than all of them, hands down. I wish my pics of these did them justice but my full length mirror broke, so all I can do is crappy bathroom pics, sorry! 🤣🤦‍♀️😬 You NEED these jeans!

Good Leg Shape / Heavy & Uncomfortable / Waist Gap Re: Medium Blue Vintage & Black (14 Regular, Low Stretch) Review keeps not posting -- guessing too long? -- trimming and retrying: After reading Jennifer Romolini's glowing reviews, I had high hopes for these jeans. Unfortunately, both pairs fell short of expectations. As a fan of vintage & contemporary rigid denim, and a sometime wearer of semi-stretch denim, I found the fabrication of these jeans perplexing. They're somehow stiff, *heavy*, and uncomfortable, while also being slightly stretchy, and exceedingly quick to bag out -- even during a brief try-on. The straight leg shape is good. However, on both pairs, the waist is unusually high, especially in back. (I'm 5'11" with a long torso, so to have a waistband hit so high is unexpected.) The blue pair was so unflattering overall -- creating a bizarrely matronly figure / butt shape (even worse than the one which exists) -- that I immediately removed them without taking a photo. They were HORRID!! The black jeans looked decent from the front, if bulky; and because they're black, weren't so awful from the rear view (though they felt stiff and immobile, while somehow being quick to sag). Because the legs had the straight, full-length silhouette I'm seeking, I considered keeping the black pair -- but in addition to their stiff uncomfortableness, the waist gapped *too much* in back -- and with the odd fabrication and low price, aren't worth the trouble of having altered. Everyone's taste and body type differ, and perhaps these jeans will work well for others -- but please see attached photo for the waist-gap situation on me. (My specs: 5'11", approx. 186 pounds, long torso / shorter legs, hourglass figure.)

Not medium stretch. Buy if you have small waist with curves Never order jeans based on just whatever you normally wear. Read the size chart but then read the reviews on sizing. I am 5'6 and weigh 145lbs. In levi signature jeans, I'm a size 4/27 because they run large and are stretchy. In typical levis, lee, american eagle and wrangler, I am a size 28-29 depending on style and stretch. I ordered a size 8 in these pants after reviewing the chart. The Amazon Essential pants I own for work run too big but a few comments on these jeans said they ran small so I just picked the higher end. The fit is confusing. The waist runs a tad small while the rest fits me fine (thick thighs). If you have a smaller waist but are curvy in the hips and rear, these might be perfect for you. These are in no one "medium stretch" as stated. They stretch out throughout the day after wearing them, just like traditional non-stretch jeans. I prefer jeans that have a tad more stretch so they hold their shape and are comfortable to sit in. I will say, they came out of the package very stiff and are a tad softer and more comfortable after washing. They do not look terrible on me but they are not my favorite or most comfortable. Again, buy these based on the size chart and if you have a slimmer waist with a curvy figure and you will probably look fantastic.

user_comment: Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Super comfortable and long enough!! Absolutely love these! The material is super soft, comfortable, and breathable. I was slightly worried they'd rip because they felt a bit thin but I did yard work in them and even after getting snagged by our farm fence and tree branches multiple times there are no holes and anything. Plus pet hair doesn't really stick to them and the small amount that does easily brushes off. Also the length is AMAZING! I'm 5'10 with a 30in inseam and 160lb. I ordered the Large- 31 inseam. I always struggle with finding pants/bottoms long enough but these are perfect! Especially since I don't like my pants to stop at my ankles and like them to be longer. As shown in the picture, they go over my heel, which is how I like to wear my pants. My only negative thing would be there are not pockets but that really isn't that big of a deal. Definitely will be buying more!

TALL!! 😍 Only leggings I used to be able to find in tall is UA... we all know the price of them 😡 Found these and perfect! Ordered 31" Also tried 28" since there are more colors. 31" fits much for me 5'9 135 M I HATE short leggings If I want Capri or 7/8 then I will order that ( but really, for me if I want that length I can just order whatever brand cause they are too short for full length) Wish the tall came in more colors, ESPECIALLY teal. E-A-G-L-E-S!! 🏈 We NEED them The 28 will have to work with eagles socks to offset length. 28 vs 31 M 31 fits with much strong compression and better length 28 is a slightly looser compression (not loose, just not as snug as the 31 for some reason) Both sizes fit fine, I just feel much more comfortable in 31. I prefer full-full length all the way to my ankles. I find myself tugging at legs a bit on 28 to give myself more inseam length, more full room in seat. When I have the leg length of 28 where I want them they are shorter than perfect comfort in seat/ crotch The 31 are 100 % totally perfect. EXCEPT lack of color options 😔 They are also more snug which is great for compression, not as great for loose skin that doesn't "tuck in" lol So... I just ordered size L in the 31 to see the difference. Also ordered the yoga in tall both M and L Will update with comparisons

By far my favorite Amazon leggings. Tall girl friendly and are very flattering on the waist. I’m 5’11 and they are almost too long on me

Tall? Have EDS or POTS? Read THIS review! I don’t often take the time to write a review on anything. I’m a 53-year-old, 6'2" tall female (assigned at birth) who is all legs (37" inseam). I weigh 210lbs and am curvy with a big booty. The 31" long leggings actually go ALL THE WAY DOWN to the bottom of my ankles, and even have a little extra. I wear an XL. They give a nice, firm hug, but are so soft and comfortable. I’m medically supposed to wear compression from my high-waist to my ankles due to chronic illness (POTS, EDS, and 5 other incurable autoimmune diseases). I personally layer a size L under my usual XL to add additional compression. I’m also autistic and have ADHD. I don’t do compression wear well AT ALL! These leggings aren’t causing any sensory issues for me whatsoever.

Runs a bit small. 5’6” 200# and an apple. I carry most of my weight in my belly. OK, so now that we got that out of the way, onto the pants. I really like these. I bought them in a size 16 medium because I find that Lee pants always run a little small and since they’re for work. I really did not want them to be snug. I got the black and white thin plaid pattern and I really like it. The rise on these is good. It’s a fairly high-rise, but it’s not super high-rise. The waistband is a bit big on me but I’d rather that then have it dig in. The length is perfect for me, but if you’re any shorter than 5’6” you may want to order the shorter size unless you want your pants really long for heels or something. The back pockets are sewn shut, but you can open them up, and the front pockets are pretty deep. Material has a good thickness and has a bit of stretch to it. Everything seems to be pretty good quality and I love the price at $18. These will be great work pants, they might be a little heavy for the summer, but we’ll have to wait and see. Between the fit the fabric and the price. I definitely recommend these.

Fits Amazing! I love them ! I got a size 16 Long and Im 5'9

Comfy dress pants! These are nice pants! I got a size 12 regular and I'm 5'3. I didn't want to get the short size because I like my pants longer but I will likely hem them an inch or two so they are more comfortable with and without heels. They are super stretchy so I could've even gone a size down and with the short size option, I just wanted wiggle room. I didn't have the sticker on the leg so there was no damage to the pants. They also do have back pockets!!! You just need to remove the small strip of stitching. The front pockets can fit my fist. They are mid rise and pass the squat test lol. Once I give these a try and see how long they hold up I'll probably get more colors!

Great pants but only fit certain body types. These pants are lovely, great fabric and VERY well made. For my body type, they don’t work. There is nothing wrong with them at all and I encourage people to purchase these if they sound like a good fit. I am only trying to help people in their purchasing/buying debacle. I am 5’6, 130 lbs. I bought a size 4. They fit just fine but the waist was a bit snug (not tight, but snug). The legs were large/unprofessional/baggy on me. But the length was excessive. If I was wearing a 3” heel, fine. But anything shorter than that, they looked like warehouse pants. I attached pic to give an idea. I kept these because I actually like the baggy style when not at work. However, I was searching for a casual business pant and these didn’t quite fit the requirement. Oh, another thing worth mentioning…. They are kinda tight in the butt crack area. So if you sit all day, you probably won’t be able to sit all day.

Comfy, dressy pants. New fav. Ok, I still don’t know what cut these are but I would say somewhere between bootcut and like a straight wide leg? They are fitted through the top, straight and then straight to the bottom with the a wide leg opening from the knee down. But ad pictured in the product photos, these are not like the flowy wide leg pants a palazzo style. I got the Heather gray color and they look very nice and classy. Definitely going to be my dressier work days go-to. I wore these and dressed them up with the heels, and the black top/blazer you can kind of see pictured to work one day and my husband was like oh you look extra professional today!The main highlight: They are comfy as heck! The stretch is just right, and pretty much true to size, with some caveats. I’m 5’4” and about 170ish, thick thighs, some butt, not a lot of hips, then some pudgy stomach stuff takes over the waistline. I normally wear a 10 regular and purchased that size. At first, I was a little worried they would be tight and that it would put strain on the fly/button but once they were past my thighs and all the way up I could tell they would be fine by the stretch in them. I was very pleased with how they hugged my butt and actually made it look more like the cute booty I used to have in my 20s! 😂 I tried to take pics of back but, girl, they couldn’t do it justice. lol Anyway, the only con would be if you are short, these absolutely run long. I don’t mind that, I will be wearing these with heels and I like my pants on the longer side, so not a problem for me. I did add a pic of the length in a pair of heeled boots, probably about 3” heels so you can see how they hit.The material weight is a mid weight. Might be ok in summer with AC in office setting, maybe a little warm. Not heavy though I’ll be wearing these through Michigan winters just running from my car to the office. It’s not like I’m going doing anything active in them. Perfect for fall/spring.I have only worn them with belts. I just feel like it pulls the outfit together better. So not sure if there would be the back gap issue or slipping but they feel like they’re secure all around without a belt.All pockets are real, just bit smaller like you’d expect on dressy pants. They have been very wrinkle resistant so far, even a bit pet hair resistant. I’m hoping the black material is the same because I just ordered these again in black. (Fingers crossed they’re just as good)I do still prefer everyday casual wide, flowy work pants, but these are absolutely up there with my new top fav pants.

These pants are really very comfortable. I’m probably about 200 pounds right now, 5’ 6” and a bit of an apple. I tend to carry most of my weight in my belly area. The scrub pants fit me, shrink little bit after wash PROS: Mid rise, very moderate flare legs, not wide. The cargo pockets with a unique stretch Red knit interior pocket, I place my cell phone inside it. The side vents legs easy to size up to the right inseam. It's a true size for me, especially the adjustable waist.CONS: The front two pockets could be deeper, because they shrink little after washed. Still I can put my whole palm inside. Material 55% Cotton, 45% Polyester, good and not thin. It would be even better if there is a little % Spandex, to allow some stretchable on pants and the drawstring. plus less wrinkle after dryer. I normally took out cloths after dryer and hang it up while it's Warm to avoid iron.Wash it before size : I was thinking to size the length, but it shrink after washed and no need to.True size: I ordered Medium petite, it was a little loose and fit just right after washed. You may think it's little huge but it's not after you wash it. I am 5'3"Overall I like it. I returned two other pairs and keep this one. The mid rise and adjustable waist is my most favorite. Plus very mild flare legs, not wide.

Vet techs: these REALLY DO repel hair! I normally wear dickies signature but really wanted to try out a pair of joggers when I got new scrubs for a new new job. I was skeptical of all the reviews from nurses that said how well they repel their pets hair because one pet is a lot different than the dozens of patients I see in a day. But they really do keep all the hair off! I didn't reach for a lint roller or latex glove all day. They fit as expected- I'm a tiny person with a 24in waist and the xs was just a touch big, but I have this problem with all brands of scrubs. Washed with hot water and dried on high and they shrunk up to a more comfortable fit without losing shape or color.

Wardrobe staple I've been buying ribbed tanks from Old Navy for years. They're perfect for summer and layering. The rib knit is very stretchy and comfortable. I'm 5'9" with a long torso, and appreciate that the Tall length has plenty of length to tuck in without worrying about it coming untucked. I decided to get this tank from Gap to see if it is any better quality than the Old Navy ones. It turns out that the fabric content, texture, and finishings are all the same between the two tanks. The only notable difference is that the back of the Gap tank is cut quite a bit higher, and the arm holes are cut just a tiny bit lower. (The Old Navy tank in the photos is lighter because I've had it since 2017.) The Gap tank curves in slightly along the waist, whereas the Old Navy tank is cut straight down. If you're wearing these fitted, it doesn't really make a difference because of the stretch. Unless you have a strong preference about the back neckline, go with whichever one has a better price, because they're largely the same.

These knee-length boots are surprisingly comfortable and easy to walk in. The toe is pointed, but the boots are not uncomfortable on my feet at all, not even on the toes. The heel is fairly big, but because the width of the heel is wide, they are stable and easy to walk in. The sole has some padding, and I found them comfortable to both walk and stand. They fit true to size for a medium-width foot. I am short, 5ft 2" tall, and they hit me right at the knees, so they are truly tall boots! I currently weigh 177 lbs and wear a size 14 in pants, and the boots fit over my calves comfortably. This is a big deal because in the past, I have had issues with long boots fitting my legs, so these are truly a nice, wide width to fit women with larger legs. The quality of these boots is really nice. I chose the suede style in black, and they look and feel expensive. These are nice-looking boots that will work well with skinny jeans, skirts, and leggings. Recommended!

Beautiful These are such pretty lightweight pants! The material is flowy and flattering, but you may need shapewear because it's also a little thin. The waistband is just snug enough on the 3x to fit my waist flat and be flattering. These are more like a spring/summer weight, and I love all the colors they come in. These will work for everyday wear and you can totally dress them up easily. Great wardrobe staple and great value!

READ THE SIZE CHARTS - don't think you know - if you order correctly, I think you'll love these. At 61 years old, 5'4", and 115 pounds, I have never once in my entire life been described as "tall." I'm a size 2 and have been buying "regular" length pants for 5 decades without incident. So when I saw these fleece-lined pants came in short, regular, and tall options, I confidently ordered Small-Regular (should have been Medium-Tall) At 5'4" I needed the tall length. I'm still processing this revelation. When I put them on, the front pockets flared out instead of lying flat - which tells me I needed to size up. Looking back at the actual size chart (which I should have done FIRST), I realize they provided the correct information about their product, I just needed to LOOK. But here's the thing: despite my sizing failure, I can tell these are quality pants. At the time of this listing the price is $39.99, and for that you're getting something genuinely nice. The fabric is 90% polyester and 10% elastane with a fleece lining that's different from typical fuzzy fleece - it's flatter, shorter, and feels substantial. I uploaded a photo so you can see the texture. This is the kind of fleece that looks like it'll survive countless wash cycles without pilling into those annoying little fabric balls that make everything look cheap. It feels durable and well-made. My advice: These pants are legitimately good quality. But LOOK AT THE SIZE CHART. Don't assume your normal size will work. Don't assume your height category is what you think it is. Actually measure and compare. If they fit properly, I genuinely think I would have loved these. The fabric quality alone makes them worth trying again with the right measurements. I hope this review has been for you!

Get a little tummy control in trendy, soft, lightweight stretch denim When I first opened these, I thought "Oh no, how did we get the size THAT wrong?" I double and triple checked everything, and yes, they are the size they're supposed to be. Well, they stretch, so try them on. I was skeptical as I pulled them up all the way and . . they packed in a lot of my overweight, slimming me, and ever so comfortably. Sold. But so long! It's the trend now, to have your pants puddle around your feet. I'll probably hem them up a bit but in the meantime, they look great with my two-inch heel mules. They come out of the wash beautifully. They're much lighter than regular denim, but they're still substantial. And they're soft. Back to the size; I ordered Large based on Amazon's recommendation and didn't look at the size chart, being in a hurry. As it turned out, the Large really is a bit small, though I wear it comfortably. I'm 5/5" and 160 pounds. so cheap looking lol First of all, I want to say I LOVE love love gym people as a brand. I get a bunch of their stuff for exercising and lounging and its fantastic quality. This is also fantastic quality and well-made. I had been hoping the pants would be better looking, but alas, they look like leggings painted with a jean pattern. That being said, they also FEEL exatly like leggings, which is a win in my book (even though they're fairly ugly lol). They also look EXACTLY like they do in the photo online, so if you're looking for jeggings that look somewhat like jeans, these aren't it. That's fine in my book, becuase it's correct advertising. I'd probably wear these more if they were more jean-looking, but that doesn't mean they're not perfect for someone else. For my needs, these will be used for things like long flights/airports when I still want to look a little more polished than sweatpants, and/or for when I have a long top that will cover the waist. I ordered a size medium based off the size chart (which says size 8-10 or a waist for 32-35 inches), and it fits me perfectly, even at the end of the day. The pants are very stretchy. I would suggest ordering based off your normal numeric size as opposed to the waist inches, since I'd say this is more comfortable for a 30-32 inch waist than 32-35 inch. TLDR: very comfy, very stretchy, and very accurate to the photos (for good or for bad)

These pants are very soft. Not too thick or thin. If you’re hot natured you can likely wear these outside in 30-50 degree weather. If you’re cold natured maybe 50-60 degrees. I am 5’2”, 115 lbs. The first pictures you see are the pants on me before washing. As you can see, they were just slightly bunched at the ankle. After washing in warm water and drying on cottons, they shrunk just a little bit as you can see from the pictures on the left. Both lengths are fine with me. They are a medium blue color with a draw string and cuffs at the ankles. I got these on sale for $10 which I think was a steal! I am very impressed with the quality and have worn them several times already for walking outdoors and once to the gym for lifting. I highly recommend and plan to get another pair soon.

Waist/width: XS: 14", S: 15", M: 17", L: 18" - Shrinks in dryer Measurements for XS in inches: -Waist/width for XS: 14”-14.5” Measurements for S/Small in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10.5” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5" -Inseam for S: 28" -Overall length for S: 38" Measurements for S/Small in inches (AFTER laundry): -Ankle for S: 4.5" -Front rise for S: 10” -Waist/width for S: 15"-15.5” -Inseam for S: 26" -Overall length for S: 35" Measurements for M/Medium in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Ankle for M: 5" -Front rise for M: 10.5” -Waist/width for M: 17"-17.5" -Inseam for M: 28.5" -Overall length for M: 39" Measurements for L/Large in inches (BEFORE laundry): -Front rise for L: 11" -Waist/width for L: 18"-18.5" -Inseam for L: 27" -Overall length for L: 38" ----------- **Original Review**: If I was going for mid to high-rise then I would pick XS. The overall size fits my body better (4’11”, 95 lbs). However, I prefer a loose fit since I have a sensitive (pudgy) belly so I kept size Small. It looks kind of baggy on me but that’s ok. I rather breathe easily. I hide that area with a big t-shirt. I prefer all my pants/jeans/panties to be low-waist, which is hard to find as we are no longer in the low-rise trend. Size S sits about 2 inches below my belly button while XS sits right below my belly button. I think these joggers are better suited for apple shaped bodies since it’s wide at the top (waist) and slimmer/tighter in the knees and calves. Works for me! Too many pants these days are narrow in the waist so I was relieved and surprised that this wasn’t. The inner fleece sheds as I wear it throughout the day. I find fuzz and lint on the floor and stuck around the ankles. It’s not so bad. I have experienced much worse. It also pills a little on the outside. Pockets are decent. Not too shallow, but not so deep either. I would say this jogger is a medium weight/thickness. I washed them in cold water and dried in low heat. They did not shrink (read update/edit below). I bought them on sale so I’m not mad at the imperfections. Overall, I am satisfied with the jogger. Lots of prints/patterns and colors to choose from. ----------- **UPDATE/EDIT (Oct 2022)**: I recently bought another jogger and when I tried it on new (before washing/drying) I noticed it's bigger than my old joggers. Initially I thought they redesigned or updated the model, but nope they shrink. It’s actually ok with me since I am petite. It fits me better after laundry. Before drying: it had a longer inseam (+2"), longer front rise (+0.5"), longer overall length (+3"), and wider/looser throughout the thighs and legs. I recommend using cold water and low heat or line dry. I also want to point out that the Charcoal Heather came with a dark gray drawstring, which looks better in my opinion. The model is wearing a white colored drawstring. ---------------- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I noticed that the cheetah and zebra joggers have a wider/looser waist (about 1 inch) compared to the solid colors. I don't see those animal printed joggers listed anymore though. They must have been discontinued. Now it makes me wonder which joggers did I use to measure. This is frustrating when they do not design or manufacture it consistently and caused me to give out confusing measurements. I apologize. ——————- *UPDATE/EDIT* (Nov 2024): I just bought two more colors and they’re wider than usual in the waist. Their lack of quality control is driving me crazy. Are they attaching wrong size tags on the joggers or changed manufacturers? I had the same experience with their other thinner joggers without drawstring. I love the joggers though. Just need to figure out the right size!

Review of size 6 versus size 8 I originally ordered this Lee Lux Comforte in the Navy in size 6. Amazon insisted 6 was the size for me. According to the size chart, it was close. I’m 5‘6“ tall. I weigh 135 to 140, waist is 29. hips are 38. I am also a little bit long-waisted. The waist of the 6 was OK. It was firm with not much give. However, the 6 was quite snug through the hips and thighs, and it made the pockets gap. I opted to return the 6 because these will primarily be for work and they were just slightly too small everywhere. I ordered the same slacks in size 8. The waist is still OK in the 8 a little bigger and a little looser, but I’ll still be able to wear them after Thanksgiving dinner. There is more room in the hips, but they still hug the top of the hip nicely before they fall to the wide leg. The leg in the 8 is loose at on my thighs, but the 6 was tight on my thighs. They are a bit longer. Unfortunately, they still make a funny wrinkle at the groin. This happens to me sometimes because I am slightly long-waisted. Because these are on sale for $16 and I really need navy slacks, I’m going to keep the 8. For anyone with a waist over 29, or hips more than 36, or generous thighs, or a long-waist, I think the 8 is a better choice. The fabric is not thin. It is a nice weight and it will be good for work. It might be warm on hot days.

So Close to Tall Perfection It Hurts (But I Still Bought Two) Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

Other than that they are fantastic they have held up really well and I love how thick the fabric is but they are so soft and comfortable it feels like I'm wearing pajamas almost. PERFECT!!! I don’t even know where to start. After searching HIGH AND LOW, ordering nearly 10 different pants, multiple trips to UPS to return, I found the ONE. I am 5’7, ~158lbs. These fit like a glove. They are so soft, flattering, and so so so comfortable. The stretch is *chefs kiss*. I had originally ordered the long size & two pockets option but it actually turned out to be too long and the front was sewn weird, so I reordered the regular with 4 pockets and it’s perfect for me (flats girly) and the front pockets are perfect. These feel perfect for work. They’re simple but still look classy and they’re not heavy or weigh you down. Feels like a second skin. Although pricier than I’m used to, I’d have to say it’s worth it, absolutely no regrets and I will be ordering more colors once I wash these and see how they hold up. Will update if something goes awry lol!

I absolutely LOVE these pants! These are perfect for an office that requires business casual attire if you really don’t enjoy wearing stuffy and uncomfortable dress pants! My favorite features are the small details that make them truly appear like business pants: the belt loops, the pockets, the non-functioning button on the front, and the faux zipper. It really does feel like I get to wear yoga pants to work without being under-dressed. The fabric is stretchy, comfortable, reasonably thick (you can’t see through them), and the waistband is not too tight at all (no pinching)! I love that Yogipace offers different inseam lengths. Being a shorter woman, I get tired of shopping for hard-to-find petite lengths or hemming regular length pants. Ordering the inseam length I needed really saved me from the hassle. Overall, these pants are pretty great quality for the price. I will definitely be purchasing more of these! My measurements are as follows: 34.5” waist, 42” hip, 23.5” thigh, and my height is 5’2”. I would say these pants fit true to size. For reference, I bought the Medium size with the 29” inseam. Without my ankle boots on, the hem of the pants sits right on top of my foot. They don’t bunch at the bottom, so I could easily wear the pants with ballet flats or loafers as well.

Good but not great For reference, I ordered an XL/31" inseam based on the sizing chart - I can't confirm if they're actually XL since the tag looks like it was chewed away or something. The pants are very comfortable and fit well, they are just not made that well. As others have noted, they're covered in lint/hair, so keep a lint roller handy. I loved that this has front and back pockets, but the front pockets weren't sewn properly so there are holes in them. I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

Definitely a bell bottom These jeans fit me perfectly, and I have always loved a button fly jean. They are soft and comfortable, and the long length drapes my foot but doesn't drag the floor. The leg opening covers my entire size 11 foot, and the pant legs flap against each other when I walk. Alterations would still worth the cost to me, given that these were not expensive to begin with, and I love the fit otherwise.

Tall Curvy Girl Approved! Finally, a nice quality cargo pant for tall/curvy women! If you have been looking for a good cargo pant, look no further! The high waist sits just above my belly button, and the inseam is a great length for me- I am 5’9”, regular inseam 31-32, ordered a size 14 in black. The material feels sturdy while not being too heavy. I wanted something mostly made out of cotton for durability, so this pant was a great choice for the price. Other cargo pants just did not have all the criteria I was looking for, but this one did! The pockets are all functional, and they lay flat without sticking out awkwardly. The stitching is neatly done and seems to be good quality. I like the straight leg silhouette, it’s flattering on a fuller figure. You can easily tuck a shirt in if you wear these pants for work. I am wearing a belt in these photos, although it’s not necessary to wear a belt with these pants. They fit true to size and are comfortable. They do pick up animal hair pretty easily, but I can live with that. If they hold up good after washing them, I will definitely order in some more colors.

Very comfortable waist, good length but cotton a bit stiff At first I didn’t like the look of these out of the bag, with the half-elastic waistband and slightly stiff cotton. However, on? They look cute and they feel great. I’m 5’5” and the length is perfect. Without shoes they skim the floor and with shoes they fall just over the laces. I tried them on after I washed them, to see if they shrink. I used cold water but imagine they could/will shrink over time so I air dry (since right now the fit is perfect.) I normally wear a medium top and a 6 or 8 bottom. I ordered the 6 and they fit just right. Pockets are placed well and are actually useful (not just for show.) My iPhone fits easily in the two larger front/side pockets. Back pockets are a bit smaller than typical jeans’. The waist is very comfortable and from the waistband down to the tummy, it’s slightly fitted and supportive. From there down it’s loose and wide legged—overall very flattering, even for my height. My only complaint is that it has a slight sheen to it and the cotton isn’t soft, it’s stiff and can make a bit of noise while I’m walking. Just wish it was softer and a little thicker. But since it’s thinner, it makes for a great pant for Spring or mild days.

Decent fabric, true to size. I’m 5’6 and about 120lbs, 26” waist and 36” hips, I have a 28” inseam. I’m normally a size 2 but ordered the recommended size 4. These pants are a little looser on my waist then I like, I probably could’ve gone with a size 2. This fabric is kind of a thinner slightly stretchy twill. It feels fairly durable but also lighter weight. The cargo pockets have snaps, The rear pockets have buttons. The backside of the waistband has elastic, which kind of makes me feel like a four year old. The legs are plenty long. The stitching is mediocre at best, but good enough. They’ll work fine for being work pants as I intended it to be.

Height: 5’2, weight 110 lbs, waist 27.5”, Hips 35”, inseam 29, ordered XS, 29 inseam, color Navy Blue. Let me start by saying that the main reason for the 1-star rating is the condition in which I received these pants—they were FULL of dog or cat hair. While I do love animals, when I pay for a "new" item, the least I expect is for the item to be clean. I understand that some people with pets may try on items and return them, but I believe it's the company's responsibility to inspect returned items before sending them to another buyer. The condition I received these pants in is simply unacceptable. Putting that aside, I'd have given these pants 3 stars. Fabric: I have purchased 5 different colors of pants from this same brand, but the ones with bands around the waist area. The fabric is very comfortable and flattering, and I wear them almost every day for my small business. The fabric of these ones is the same. Size: I'm 5'1" and weigh 110 lbs. I ordered an XS with a 29 inseam, the same size I ordered in the other pants. The waist area is a bit tight, but not too bad. Considering only the waist, I might have ordered a size S instead. However, the legs are noticeably wider than the other pairs I own, about 1 to 1.5 inches wider, which I don't really like. The 29-inch length is perfect, as it's the same length as the other pants, and it looks equally good. Color: The navy blue color is very pretty. In general, I like this brand because the pants are comfortable and can be dressed up or down. However, I'm extremely disappointed with receiving pants covered in hair and not being entirely satisfied with the style. I'm not sure if I would specifically recommend these pants to friends or family.

AMAZING.. if you can handle a skinny elastic digging into their waist These are so amazing and great quality- I want to keep them so bad, but the tiny elastic waistband (thickness of a hair tie) at the very top is the most annoying thing in the world. Because of the high waist, the top band hits just below the ribs, causing an unnecessary fitted feeling in a sensitive area, and I’m reminded of it every time I breathe. For some bodies it may serve a purpose, but these fit absolutely perfectly without it, and the drawstring is there to control how tight of a fit you want. If they were to remove that band or redesign, I would buy them in every color and wear every day during this New England winter! I tried a few size/length combos but the 28” Small worked best for me (5’6, 130 lbs).

Look and feel great These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

Great fit, comfortable and flattering These pants have been my go-to for years. I have to say I think they run slightly large, as my measurements are an inch or two over the size listing and the pants are comfortably loose. I just bought a 16 and love the way they fit, but I definitely could have opted for the 14 if I wanted a more form-fitting look. I’m 5’3” and prefer to shorten the length about 1 inch, but I have worn them with the 29” short inseam as is, and they look okay that way too (just pool a little at the ankle). They machine wash and dry just fine, but I don’t use powdered detergent or oxiclean in the wash as they often collect some of it on their surface, requiring them to be wiped down with a damp cloth to remove it. I’ve seen other reviews complaining of white residue after washing. I’ve found that this can be avoided by using only liquid detergent. I love these pants and highly recommend them.

Perfect length This brand specializes in longer lengths for tall people. I am 5'8" 132lbs. I ordered size M-Tall which was the 32in length. The length is absolutely perfect. The leg width and slight taper is very flattering, and the pants are well made and comfortable. I usually get a size small but based on the size chart, I ordered medium and I'm glad I did because the waist band is snug and fits like a small typically would feel on me, so check the size chart and you may also want to size up. The only thing I don't love about the pants is the cargo pockets with the big flap at the top. They add bulk to the legs and I don't find them flattering. I'd prefer no pocket or a flush, smooth zipper on top instead of the flap. In my experience, after washing, the flaps always look crinkled, crooked, and stick out more. Overall, I like the length and comfort of these pants and will look at this brand to find other styles with 32" length.

Fantastic pants for tall ladies! Tall ladies…you’re gonna love these pants! Why is it so hard for us tall gals, or just gals with a long inseam, to find pants that reach the foot instead of above the ankle? If you want a pair of cotton, yes cotton (another hard find nowadays), sweatpants these are awesome. Yes, a bit too long for me before washing but I expected that being about a 35 inch inseam myself. But I wanted to dry them and knowing cotton with usually shrink in the dryer, I went for it. And the fit is perfect a tad longer than floor length which is how I wear my pants. Keep them out of the dryer and you will have 36 inch inseam with no shrinkage from washing in cold water. The quality is exceptional with no loose threads and all seams sewn well. I appreciate the elastic waistband as it offers the wearer to not use the drawstring. The waistband feels soft against the skin and all the cargo pockets lie flat even after putting in the dryer. The fabric has good weight without being heavyweight - meaning the fabric is warm without the weight. The side pockets also lie along your silhouette without flaring out (quite unattractive!) and have good depth. I love these pants, and I rarely say such a thing but these are really high quality, well made athletic pants that can be worn out for errands or outdoors doing yard work or adventuring into the woods. Great product and well priced!

Finally--Sweatpants That Actually Reach My Ankles! As a 5'9" woman with a 27" waist and 37" hips, finding joggers that don’t awkwardly hover above my ankles has been a struggle. I ordered the Small Tall (32" inseam) and—cue the happy dance—the fit is spot-on. Not only do they hit the perfect length, but they also hug in all the right places without feeling restrictive. 🧵 Fabric & Feel: When I first pulled them out of the bag, I worried they might be too heavy. But once I slipped them on, I totally changed my tune. They’re functional, flattering, and versatile enough to wear from morning yoga to evening Netflix. If you’ve been let down by “tall” pants that still fall short—these get it right.

Tres Chic. Only a couple of downsides. Overall, I feel glad that I got these pants. They are lightweight, a beautiful blue color, the right cut on the waist, and match the upscale casual spring and summer style I'm looking for. I was glad that these pants were given sizeable pockets (thank you!) and even more surprised and delighted that there are actually about 6 inch slits at the outside bottoms of each pant leg, exposing the bottom of your calf and ankle. This was a nice touch that makes them more flowy and almost flare like when walking, vs. rigid straight leg. The two downsides I have: 1) They are slightly transparent in certain light, beware, as you can see in the photo- I hope that helps. 2) The other "issue" which is more of a personal issue is the length of the pant leg. I would prefer these to be longer. I would rather them almost drag on the floor when wearing flats and then be a bit highwater with heels. But instead they are at my ankle when standing, above the ankle when sitting, and seem a bit too short legged for heels at my 5' 7" stature with longer than usual legs. My wedge sandals were a bit off, but I persevered and owned the look. I'm ready for hell or high water. The stretchy waistband was very forgiving and easy to sit at any part of the waist without slipping and sliding about. I wore these all day and was at ease. Almost like wearing pajama pants out to the nail salon and dinner, but elevated. Score. Get these for upscale beachy, elongating and effortless, plus comfort and coolness in the heat of summer. These are a great value compared to linen style pants I've seen elsewhere and even cuter in many ways.

Well-made, thick, fits to size puffer vest. My colors never look right. This vest is a dark navy, not light blue.This is a great vest. Thick and well-made. Two external zip pockets and 1 interior hidden pocket. Good zippers. Water resistant. Nice and warm.The size chart is accurate. I'm a woman with a 50 inch chest and 54 inch hip/belly area. I decided to go up to the 3X and it fits loosely in the chest and comfortably in the belly. So choose the fit based on the largest portion of your body and whether you want to wear heavy shirts underneath.The one thing I don't like is that when the zipper is zipped all the way up, the neck is tight. A big and tall person is also going to have a larger neck size and the vest doesn't accommodate that well. If you had a hoodie on, or a thick gaiter, you might not be able to comfortably zip all the way up to your chin. But if you're wearing something else around you neck, you might not need to.

Very nice shirt, but mystery fabric This is a very nice quality, and well-made white button-down shirt. I am 5'2" and 135#. I ordered this shirt in Petite Medium because I was concerned that it might run small, but it doesn't. I actually wish I had gotten the Petite Small instead. The fabric is nice and smooth, almost a satin feel to it. It's light and drapey, but not thin, so the bra doesn't show through. It seems well made with nice details on the sleeves. I do wish the cuffs would be a little bit tighter. The only thing that bothers me about this shirt is that there is no info anywhere that says what the fabric content is, which is something I thought is a requirement for clothing in the US, including imports. Google tells me this clothing company is based in Korea. Beyond that, I like this shirt and I do recommend it.

Tall Girl Approved! It fits exactly as I hoped in all the right places. I’m a tall girl, 5’10”, and it’s so difficult to find dress shirts that fit like they should. Most of the time, sleeves are too short, and if they are fitted, the curve at the waist is too high and the shirt tails too short. Larger sizes just grow outward… however, THIS BBiBBi button down dress shirt is an absolutely PERFECT fit. I can’t even believe it. The shirt is exactly like shown in the product images, and the sleeves are long enough to wear under a blazer, hitting my wrists just as they should. The top is fitted, with no extra bulk under the armpits or through the waist. The fabric is smooth and has some stretch, while not easily wrinkling. It makes for a very comfortable fit. The shirt tails stay tucked in when I raise my arms, and it truly is everything I need in a dress shirt. I wish there were other color choices available, I’d buy more! For the price of this shirt, I feel like I got an excellent product for my money. The shirt has washed well, and it resists wrinkling if I take it out of the dryer while still a bit damp and hang it to finish drying. For reference, I’m about 160 lbs, about a 36 C/D and I ordered a size medium-tall.

LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

These Stelle fleece lined bootcut leggings are both warm and compressive in the waist/hips. I normally wear a size 16 and I ordered the XL. The XL fits me well and slightly more snug in the waist than an Old Navy brand legging XL. The fleece lining on the interior of these leggings is warm and cozy, without being too thick. In fact, I cannot tell that these are fleece lined by simply looking at them. I’m glad that the fleece does not add much bulk to the thickness of the pant. I ordered the 31” inseam and the leggings are true to size and length. I really like the boot cut because it is flattering on pear-shaped figures like mine. I also really like that. There is a phone pocket on both sides of the pant. While slightly snug in the waist for me, these boot cut leggings are a real winner. They are very nice material. They are warm and they are flattering. I definitely recommend these for the fall and winter months.

Beautiful cherry burgundy. Very comfortable. These knee high boots by CELLENZ are as comfortable as they are stylish. The heel is great for me. It's what the industry calls a "kitten" heel. The modern design of these boots slims and elevates your look, without breaking your back doing so because the heel is so doable and easy to wear. Though the heel is narrow, it's sturdy and balanced. I did not feel tippy at all. The toe box has a gentle point, but it has ample room for an average width foot, such as mine. I do not feel any pinching at the sides or in the tip when I'm still or in motion. My heel does not slip up and down. I can even run in these boots. As for size, I normally wear a US 8 or 8.5 in boots. I ordered these in an 8, and they fit me perfectly. I have thick, athletic legs and I was a bit worried that these might be too tight. But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

Beautiful cherry burgundy. Very comfortable. These knee high boots by CELLENZ are as comfortable as they are stylish. The heel is great for me. It's what the industry calls a "kitten" heel. The modern design of these boots slims and elevates your look, without breaking your back doing so because the heel is so doable and easy to wear. Though the heel is narrow, it's sturdy and balanced. I did not feel tippy at all. The toe box has a gentle point, but it has ample room for an average width foot, such as mine. I do not feel any pinching at the sides or in the tip when I'm still or in motion. My heel does not slip up and down. I can even run in these boots. As for size, I normally wear a US 8 or 8.5 in boots. I ordered these in an 8, and they fit me perfectly. I have thick, athletic legs and I was a bit worried that these might be too tight. But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

Runs WAY small and narrow. I generally love Sorels. I grew up in Canada, and we know great snow boots. 20+ years ago, Sorels were a high quality brand, and they made stuff that lasts. I've read that recently, their quality has suffered. I was intrigued. I live in California now, but I have been wanting snow boots because we've been going back to Canada during the Winter. And, I need good snow boots. I was stoked to get these. So far, although my initial impressions of these were being impressed with the quality, fit and finish, and looks. All of that was moot because of the sizing.I normally wear a size 7.5 womens. And, that's generally half a size up from my actual foot size. We decided to go with an 8 in these, because I told my husband that you go up another half size in snow boots because of thick winter socks. Wow, I'm glad I did, because otherwise the 7.5's would have been WAY too small and borderline abusive to my feet. Even the 8's are too small. The main reason for this is because of the super narrow forefoot and TIGHT toe box. The boots do not open up at the toes, but almost taper inwards like a ballerina shoe. It's barely comfortable at size 8, and I wish I could return these for a larger pair. But, I'm afraid as a Viner, I'm stuck with these. We can't return products.So, yeah, great looking boots, really nice and warm, but way too small and tight. Size up a FULL SIZE for these. I have normal wide feet, even on the narrow side, and these would be way too tight at my regular size.

Comfort & Style · The fabric is a soft, slightly stretchy ponte knit that is breathable and has a nice, substantial weight to it—it doesn't feel thin or cheap at all. The wide-leg design allows for complete freedom of movement, whether I'm walking, running errands, or just lounging at home. · The taupe color is beautiful—a perfect neutral, warm greige that pairs effortlessly with everything in my closet (blacks, whites, creams, navy, and even pastels). The front pleats and high-rise waistband are incredibly slimming and create a polished, dressy silhouette. They expertly hide any problem areas while making your legs look miles long. · UNEXPECTED AND AMAZING POCKETS: This is absolutely my favorite feature. The pockets are deep, functional, and seamlessly integrated. My large smartphone fits securely without creating a bulky look, which is a rare and wonderful find in women's pants. · Petite Size Available: As someone who is 5'2", I was thrilled that a Petite length was offered. It made all the difference in getting a better fit right out of the package. Things to Consider: · Length for Petites: I am 5'2" and ordered the Petite size. When worn high on my waist, the length is full and still grazes the floor, but it's perfectly manageable with sneakers or any kind of heel. For a clean, ankle-length look with flat shoes, someone my height or shorter would likely need to get them hemmed. · Waistband: The waistband is non-roll and very comfortable. It's a true high-rise, so if you prefer a mid-rise fit, that's something to be aware of. For me, it's perfect for tucking in tops. · Sizing: I typically wear a size Medium and found the Medium Petite to fit me perfectly—true to size. Overall, I am thoroughly impressed with the quality for the price. The substantial weight of the fabric makes these pants look and feel far more expensive than they are. Despite the minor length consideration for my height, they are an incredible value.

Five stars all the way! I have always struggled to find pants that are long enough for me, and I can't even imagine how difficult it would be if I were taller. I am 5' 9", which isn't exactly short either, and I have very long legs for my height. I can't shop in stores, especially for pajama pants because they're always too short. When I wash my clothes, I always let my pants air dry so I don't take the chance that they shrink. I have even gone as far as making my own pajama pants, just so I could have something long enough to wear. When I saw these pajama pants in a size tall, I was so excited! They come in a 32" and a 34" length. I ordered the 34 just in case, and I was absolutely ecstatic when I tried them on and found that they were too long. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on how much they'll shrink, but even if they do, they'll still fit me. The stitching on the pants appears to be good quality, and the fabric is nice and cozy. The pants have a pocket on each side, as well as one on the back. According to the measurements I was in between sizes, so I opted for the larger of the two choices. There is a drawstring if you need to tighten the waist. If you are someone like me who struggles to find pants that are long enough, buy these. Unless you need an inseam longer than 34", you will not be disappointed!

So cozy! I've been wearing these PJ pants for weeks now. They are so comfy and warm but not too hot. The fabric isn't stretchy so you'll definitely need to take that into consideration. I got the medium 32" inseam (not sure of my exact inseam measurements but I am 5'4") but I could have even gone up a size for a little extra room. I'm typically around a smaller and medium usually, but for this a large would have been just fine. While I'm stoked it has pockets at all and they are fairly deep, I wish there were two pockets in the back. Sometimes if I'm just standing and talking I like to rest both hands in the back pockets. And considering I'm wearing the literally everywhere it's kind of annoying. But overall they aren't bad sleep/lounge pants!

Super comfy, good quality, soft fabric Love these jeans! Perfect color, not too dark, not too light. They are not pull-on but they do have stretch, I am so glad I ordered a size down because this size gives me room but aren’t baggy anywhere, they fit nice, the next size up would have been big, everywhere. As a Levi’s wearer since I was a young teen, these are the same quality but are softer and not stiff like the ones I wore as a teen. Will be buying more! The material isn’t thin, it’s very decent. Soft, Lightweight and Breathable. Review of TTZ Women's Tall Pajama Pants These pajama pants have a warm, cozy look with their blue and green plaid pattern. They're made from 100% cotton, that feels like a soft, breathable, lightweight flannel. There is no stretch in the fabric, but the relaxed, loose-fit design makes them comfortable to lounge or sleep in without feeling restrictive. They have a 32" inseam, which works well for taller wearers. The elastic waistband is comfortable and easy to pull on, and the drawstring adds a bit of adjustability for a better fit. The legs are straight with very little taper. The thigh is about 13-1/2” across. The hem is just about 11” across. From waist to bottom of hem is approximately 44-1/2” long. From top of waist to point of crotch is approximately 16-1/4” long. Overall, the quality looks and feels good. The seams are neatly sewn, and I didn't notice any flaws.

They fit! I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

Really nice and affordable If you are tall like me you might feel it’s hard to find jeans that are long enough. These are really great. I got the Long version of my size and they are the perfect length. I am about 5”11 and they reach a little past my ankles which i love. The only downside (which is why i rated it only 4/5 stars) is that the color is not accurate. I ordered the lightest color and they look a little darker. Overall I am really happy with them. They are comparable to the american eagle curvy stretch jeans, very comfy.

Major Shrinkage & Poor Value – Not the TTZ Quality I Expected As a consistent TTZ customer, I expected a premium lounge pant, especially at a $30 price point. Unfortunately, these are a major disappointment in both durability and value. While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

Major Shrinkage & Poor Value – Not the TTZ Quality I Expected As a consistent TTZ customer, I expected a premium lounge pant, especially at a $30 price point. Unfortunately, these are a major disappointment in both durability and value. While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

Passable but I need longer. The waist is super comfy with good stretch, the fabric is soft but not cheaply thin. I'm 5'8" and 180 lbs. Very flattering and comfy mini-flared pants that seriously look great from all angles! Pros: - No front seam! - Soft mostly-matte fabric molds to your curves and is squat-proof - Super stretchy so you feel no restriction or tightness in the legs when you're moving around - Double lined waistband offers moderate compression and helps you feel held in - High rise waistband hits right at my belly button - Spacious pockets on both sides can easily fit a large phone - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - Flattering booty seam with hidden scrunch offers just a little extra definition and doesn't look scrunchy when you're wearing it - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing all day and washing once so far, but I'd say any fabric like this is delicate, so be careful around rough surfaces. Also, the scrunch hasn't lost it's scrunchiness after wearing all day. - Neat seams and no loose threads, overall quality feel to the fabric - I'm honestly surprised at how much longer my legs look in photos when I'm wearing these pants (Possible) Cons: - I have short legs despite being 5'5" and they're JUST the right length with sneakers that have a chunky sole. These pants have a 32" inseam and 31" might have been perfect on me. - These are mini-flared pants, so they might take some getting used to if you're more familiar with regular flared pants. I have 14" calves so I had mixed feelings about how snug they were on my calves at first, but they grew on me and I like them more now! I loved wearing these on a mini road trip and think they're great for any time you want to look cute and be comfortable. They'd also be fine for working out, but I usually stick with regular non-flared leggings for that.

Great Maternity Jeans!! I very rarely make Amazon reviews but these jeans are just too good not to say something! They truly feel like real jean material, not see through, and are nice and thick to hide any cellulite- which I appreciate. With the colder months here I’m confident they are thick enough to keep me warm, and once the temps REALLY drop (since I live in the northern U.S.) I’m sure a pair of fleece lined leggings will fit underneath well. At the same time, they are also very stretchy and comfortable. I am 25 weeks pregnant, 5’6 and currently 197lbs (185 pre pregnancy) I typically carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I got the large and they fit perfectly and are stretchy enough to allow me room to grow. It feels amazing to try on a pair of jeans and just smile bc everything fits so well after so many weeks/months of feeling like a frumpy blob lol pre-pregnancy I was used to buying brands like Bridge by Glee and Flying Monkey, which are designed to really lift and flatter the booty. These jeans are definitely not *that* flattering on the butt, but they don’t do me a disservice in that area either. I’m just glad to have something that fits and is on trend at this point. Giving bonus points bc they have real front pockets too! These jeans are definitely worth the cost and are something I plan to keep to wear through future pregnancies as well. Overall, they function well and will serve their purpose well through the rest of my pregnancy. I imagine they will even be nice to wear in my post-partum period also. The light wash that I have here is great for casual, everyday wear and I’m going to go and buy a second pair in a darker wash as soon as I’m done writing this post. If you’re curvy with hips and thighs like me, don’t hesitate to buy these jeans. They’re perfect! P.S. ignore what appears to be discoloration in one of the pictures. I took these photos in dim lighting and I think my phone was just trying to auto-adjust to make the photo appear brighter. These jeans are not discolored :)

Big Butts Beware To start, the material feels pretty thick and durable. It sort of has that kind of swishy polyester sweatpants feeling/sound when you walk. They are not super breathable and feel a bit “heavy”. Aesthetically they look pretty good and I love the amount of pockets! Now the downsides. (For reference I am 5’10” and 190). I want to love these, especially because they are so affordable, but these pants were not made with butts in mind. The material has some stretch, however, the way the pants are cut does not allow enough room for anyone with a butt. To accommodate, the waist line is pulled way up in the front and way down in the back. It REALLY accentuates the little belly pouch that us women have. When sitting, you have to be really lucky (or skinny maybe) for your underwear/crack not to show and for the front of the waistband not to sever your internal organs. Pictures included. I bend and squat and sit on the floor in my job so these factors really impact my ability to move comfortably. I just really wanted a pink scrub set for Valentine’s Day and I guess I should have known better than choose a cheap set. At least the top is able to be worn comfortably! I included photos to show the waistline and how it fits both standing and (I’m sorry you have to see me like that lol) squatting. Hope this helps a fellow big booty judy!

Fashion forward, cozy and flattering wide leg sweatpants These G4Free sweatpants are the first ones I will be wearing that first chilly fall day! The modal fabric is everything- substantial, silky smooth, sleek looking and wrinkle proof! There is a nice choice of inseam lengths. I probably could have used a 28” length but ordered 29” and I don’t mind them a little slouchy. For reference I am 5 feet tall 115 pounds.I love the more tailored, refined style of these sweatpants. The leg width is not overly wide, flattering for petites like me as well as talls. The pockets, both front and back, also lend a polished look if you wished to wear these as business casual attire. The waist band is high but lays flat. These pants may seem pricey, but I take into account the versatility. I would definitely wear them out for a casual dinner. Plus, all of the special features add up to a unique pair of slacks. Highly recommend these wide leg sweatpants and would definitely buy another pair from the wide selection of colors.

Comfortable and high quality pants I am so impressed with these pants. The material feels beautifully soft and silky and the manufacturing quality is excellent. They have pockets in the front although I think pockets are unnecessary. I don’t like how you can see the outline of the interior pocket so I will probably sew up the pocket entrance to create faux pockets and then cut out the pocket lining. The back has faux pockets and they look cute. In terms of sizing, I ordered an XS with a 27” inseam. I’m 5”4 and 122lb and these fit exactly how I would expect an XS to fit. The pant leg length was perfect for me but I do have short legs so I would say for someone with average length legs, these would be too short on anyone taller than 5’2”. Overall, I am extremely happy with these pants and I plan to order another pair in a different color.

Size up I originally bought these in the navy in a size 14 (5’6” 200# and an apple that carries most of weight in the belly). I really liked them but they were snug. So when I bought these blue, I sized up to a 16 and they fit perfect. Super stretchy with thick material, very slight VPL in the light color. Waistband is very comfortable. Front pockets are decent, back pockets are fake. The front leg seams give it a professional look. Length is perfect hitting me right at the top of the foot. The price is excellent at around $16 right now. A solid buy

Pretty good First the stats: I am almost 70, 5’4”, waist 27”, hips38”,inseam 29”, 112lbs. AI suggested size 8. I like my waist band a little loose because of a back issue. I probably should have gotten a 6. These are a little bit big; just a bit. I will definitely need to tuck in my shirt to help hold up the pants. I’ll probably use a belt as well. The rise comes even with my navel.They fit like a nice pair of slacks. I will be able to wear these when playing tour guide to visiting friends and I can wear a merino wool base layer underneath without feeling uncomfortable. I live in the Colorado mountains and it can get pretty cold. The length is what I like. The legs reach the top of my shoes. The fabric is very nice. Not thin. Pockets are a decent size and don’t show through the fabric. Rear pockets are fake but I don’t ever use those when pants have real ones. The sewn in crease makes them look dressy. I am satisfied with these pants and will keep them. If I find they are too big after wearing them for a while I will run a little bit of elastic through the waistband. I would recommend these pants if you want to look nice in cold weather.

Real Belt Loops & Great Drape So stretchy, comfy, just the right thickness PLUS with real belt loops!! I was looking for a pair of lightweight black pants for traveling and these are great. Attractive, comfortable boots with a flattering fit These boots are very nice and I’m happy with the purchase. I ordered an 8.5 and they fit well. Normally I wear a size 8, but opted to size up due to having slightly wide feet, and boots tending to be more narrow. They’re sleek, flattering, and the kitten heel makes them easy to style with both dresses and skirts without being too tall for everyday wear. The only thing I’ll note is the height. I’m 5’5.5”, and on me, they sit about 1.5–2 inches below my knee, rather than the 4 inches shown on the model. Still, it’s a good look and adds a dressier touch. The material feels nice, great color, they don’t look cheap, and they shouldn’t require much break-in time. Overall, a stylish and versatile pair of boots that work well for both casual and more polished outfits.

Yay for Look, Meh for Quality, Crazy Price Wow! These boots literally tell the truth and they go a good ways above my knee. They are very warm and fit well. They are super shaggy - the area at the bottom could use a little trimming as the hairs are a bit longer than necessary. The fur looks cheap at first glance, but the boots do look good on. It’s like wearing stuffed animals on your legs. When first putting on the Sweetheart Beauty thigh high shaggy fur boots, it was clear that the inside main part of the boots are made from either very thick cardboard or not-so-stiff interface/sizing; they bent a little. The second and third times I put them on, I was at houses where shoes must be removed. Actually, that means I’ve put them on 2x on those days. Putting the boots back on the last time I WORE them, but the 6th time I put them on, I heard a rip. Then the interface bent so much in both boots that my feet were not able to get all the way in to the bottom. Determined to wear these boots at least a couple more times, I figured I could use my boot horn to get back in and fix the interface. The boots are so tall, the boot horn wasn’t long enough! If this happens to you, push down the boot leg as far down as you can get it and flatten out the interface. NEVER pull on the boot from the top. Do it as carefully as you would pull on tights or hose. Back to the review. $99 is way too much for boots that have cheap faux fur (it’s called “fake” at this level) and zero arch support. Make sure the weather and the ground are dry before stepping out because you are wearing thick, furry socks with thick soles (but we knew that!) As of 12/31/25, they are on sale for $65 with a coupon. That should be that starting price point, and then some discounts for sales from there. I will continue to wear my boots until they fall apart. They get 2 stars because they are cute, temporary shoes that are way overpriced.

Soft, great stretch, perfect flare! These jeans have such a soft and comfortable fabric that still have durability to them with the perfect amount of stretch! Zipper zips easily. The color is classic. I am 5'3 and about 145lbs and the 6 short are perfect. I have decent curve to my backside and I love how these fit like a glove and don't leave that awkward gap at the back waist. They fit right to the back of the waist nicely. The value is at a great price for the beautiful quality. I actually bought a different pair in an upscale retail store with abbreviation BHWM, and I plan to return them since these are more comfortable and look even better for a better price. The bootcut is the perfect amount of flare to give a nice shape and elongate the look of the legs. I'm going to check out other jeans from Flying Banana since I love these so much.
