Super comfortable and looks amazing I absolutely love these pants. I’m 5’8 on a good day and 220. I purchased 18 x 32. I’ve got thick thighs and a tummy that likes to flaunt itself when I really would like it not to. These pants I feel are very flattering to us plus sized beauties. I’m not a fan of boot cut pants but these are truly barely bootcut. They are extremely comfortable and wrinkle resistance. They have not shrunk in the several washes they’ve gone through. I have them in black, navy blue and red. I was a little hesitant to get the red, but I wanted a little color in my life and these just wowed me. One of my favorite pairs.

Exactly What I Wanted I bought these to wear for boarding day on an upcoming cruise to match some custom shirts I'm making. The first thing I was attracted to was the rich color. It is exactly as I'd hoped and true to the description. The color is vibrant and these are well made. The fabric is thick so I wouldn't advise wearing in warm weather. I sized down per the reviews and it worked for me. They are are a tad bit long on me even though I ordered short but I am only 4'11. I love how these hug me and the stretch is good enough. These look high end and I appreciate the tailoring. There are small things that stand out for me from other brands I sometimes come across like the fact that threads and buttons match the pants. These seem like they will last a while in my wardrobe. I totally recommend.

COMFORTABLE!!! These pants are so comfortable! Amazon told me to order a 16, I ordered a 14 short and should exchange for a 12, but they are so comfortable I'm going to keep them. I have a 12 hour chaperone trip to NYC with high school students and need to look professional and stay comfortable and I believe these will be perfect. I will have to hem the length a little, but I'm used to that and just use some iron hem tape so I don't have to sew. I never tuck things in but did to show the waist. I like that the pockets don't gap but the might with a smaller size. The waist band is thick elastic and I wish they'd have skipped the zipper. The last picture shows how big my pair ran. I'm 100% apple shaped, 5'2" and 165 lbs and I wear my clothes loose.

5’6” 200# and an apple. I carry most of my weight in my belly. OK, so now that we got that out of the way, onto the pants. I really like these. I bought them in a size 16 medium because I find that Lee pants always run a little small and since they’re for work. I really did not want them to be snug. I got the black and white thin plaid pattern and I really like it. The rise on these is good. It’s a fairly high-rise, but it’s not super high-rise. The waistband is a bit big on me but I’d rather that then have it dig in. The length is perfect for me, but if you’re any shorter than 5’6” you may want to order the shorter size unless you want your pants really long for heels or something. The back pockets are sewn shut, but you can open them up, and the front pockets are pretty deep. Material has a good thickness and has a bit of stretch to it. Everything seems to be pretty good quality and I love the price at $18. These will be great work pants, they might be a little heavy for the summer, but we’ll have to wait and see. Between the fit the fabric and the price. I definitely recommend these.

They came with a hole in the pocket already. Not a wear and tear hole, but like the sewing machine went off track for a second haha. Honestly I’m not crazy about returning so I’m keeping them but good for others to note, could happen!

I wanted to love them. I really wanted to love these pants. I'm 5'8" and the high water trend right now is not for me. The length is PERFECTION. The fabric is great. It's soft, and comfy for my growing belly. Good for lounging, workouts, or casual. And the pants hug my butt in all the right ways. The issue: the pockets. I purchased a large (normally a small/medium) because my thighs have thickened with pregnancy. Not an issue for the fabric because its stretchy, but the pockets suction to your leg and I look and feel like I'm wearing football pads. I appreciate deep pockets, but all the extra fabric is too much for me. Hopefully the picture shows clear enough what I'm talking about. These pants without pockets?! Give me every color!

Super cute and soft wide leg yoga pants! I ordered a size large and am 5 foot 160 pounds. They are a little long on me. They are super nice and soft. It would be nice to see a shorter length in the yoga pants for us short people! lol overall, very nice and makes your butt look very nice.

I ordered a size small in these wide leg yoga pants (I am a US size 4, 5’7”, 135lbs) and they are a perfect fit. Honestly these pants are just as cute as they look online. I am IN LOVE! Sunzel continues to raise the bar when it comes to quality for your money.

A fantastic pajama set by Zylioo. It is so soft and comfy. You can lounge in it all day. I washed and dried on low and they came out even softer with no noticeable shrinkage. The fit is great. These are actually floor length on me which I love. And the sleeves actually go past my wrists which is rare. I do wish the top was a little more fitted but that's a preference and doesn't take away from the quality and comfort of the pajamas. I ordered the Small XTall and I am 5'10, 120 lbs, 31A-25-34 with a 32.5 inch inseam and 24 inch long arms for reference. These would make a great gift for those that love cute and comfy loungewear and pajamas.

Here’s a polished, insightful review you can use: --- This pajama set is a win for anyone who struggles to find comfy loungewear that actually fits a taller frame. The fabric blend (65% polyester, 30% rayon, and 5% spandex) makes it incredibly soft with just the right amount of stretch, so it feels warm and cozy without ever getting too hot. The color is beautiful in person, and the overall quality stands out right away. I’m 5'6" and 118 lbs and went with the size S. It fits really well—especially the top, which runs a bit longer in a flattering way. That length makes it perfect for tall people, but still wearable even if you're not super tall. The pants are also comfortable and stretchy, and while they’re obviously designed with longer legs in mind, I can still wear them without any issue. Overall, this set is thoughtfully made, incredibly comfortable, and a great option for anyone who wants pajamas that feel elevated and fit properly.

As a dental assistant, I’ve gone through plenty of scrub pants that claimed to be “long,” but these are the first that actually deliver. I’m 5 ft 8 in with long legs and have had challenges finding pants that properly fit. The inseam options on these Sevegos make it easy to get a true full-length fit, and the legs don’t creep up throughout the day. The jogger style looks clean and modern without feeling tight or restrictive. While I wish there was cotton in this blend, the fabric feels smooth, flexible, and comfortable enough. The waistband sits comfortably and stays put, and the cuffs at the ankles are soft but secure. The pocket layout is genuinely useful, with enough space for everything I need without adding bulk. The stitching and overall construction feel solid, and they wash well without losing shape. If you’re tired of scrub pants that come up short or feel stiff, these joggers are a major upgrade. They look good, move well, and actually fit longer legs the way they should. p.s. I don’t know why one reviewer said these are made in the USA, when both the packaging and tag say, “Made in China.” This is the information on the tag: 70% Polyester 20% Rayon, 5% Elastane Machine wash cold. Do not bleach. Do not tumble dry. Iron cool heat reverse side. Do not wash with other items with any drycleaning methods. Keep away from fire. Wash inside out. Wash with similar colours. Made in China.

As any tall girlie knows, it’s hard to find “tall” pants that are ACTUALLY tall, especially when it comes to scrubs. These are actually tall! I’m only 5’9” so I probably could’ve gone with the 32” inseam, but I’m traumatized from all the pants that have lied to me in the past so I ordered the 34”. They’re longer than needed, but thanks to the stretchy fabric around the ankle they just tuck under and look absolutely fine. I saw another review that said they aren’t made for curvy girls - but yes they are. I have a booty and these pants fit it well, you just have to be tall for them to hit you right. Five pockets total, two in the front, two in the back, and one on the right leg. The two on the front and the one on the leg are especially deep. Elastic waistband makes these extra comfy. They’re honestly so spacious, I can’t wait to wear these. You won’t be disappointed!! If you’re tall at least.

I ordered an XL and I love them. Disclaimer: I’m NOT tall but I love the extra material at the ankle feel so I chose these. I’m 5’0 and got exactly what I’d hoped for. Now they are made for taller people and I believe that generally we are built different (short vs tall) and I did notice less rear end space and a longer front space but it works for me - just a heads up. The material is so soft, flexible, and durable. The pockets are deep and hold up well. The ankle cuffs are so comfortable and do not irritate the skin. The waist band is comfortable and the adjustment strings are thick and sturdy. I 100% recommend these. They are worth their price for a good pair of high quality and durable scrubs.

I am 6 foot 2 so finding pants that are long enough has always been a challenge. These pants are amazing. They fit me comfortably. I love the deep pockets. They are made of great quality. I love how easy they are to wash. These pants come all the way down to my heel which is a nice touch.

I was looking for some inexpensive lightweight joggers to wear hiking or while cleaning the house or yard as I've always been afraid to wear my ridiculously expensive Athleta joggers doing either. I received this pair yesterday and here are my thoughts: Pro: 1. Color options are virtuously infinite and price point is excellent. I ordered the khaki, but was going to consider other colors if the fit was right. 2. Fabric is lightweight, but opaque, doesn't show panty lines, very stretchy, nice drape and soft to the touch, just like the Athleta joggers. 3. Lots of pockets: slant (not bulky) pockets in the front, one zippered pocket over the lumbar spine (for keys or a very tiny pet maybe? too shallow for a phone). 4. Length works for me. I am 5'2", the bottom sags some which is totally fine with me, even gives it a bit of a cool vibe. This pant should easily work for average height women, lengthwise. (Only one) Con: 1. The waist is a tad tight. I ordered a medium which is what Amazon recommended and, while the fabric is overall very stretchy, the very top of the pants where it falls on the waist has a double seam on the inside which gives that area much less give. Picture of waist unstretched is attached. I don't think I'd exchange it for a large, that would probably be too big. In all, I've decided to keep these and see if the waist thing becomes a non-issue later. For reference, my natural waist is 30 inches and I wear a size 6 in Athleta joggers.

These joggers are soft on your body and fit true to size. . Good sized pockets. They are a good spring or summer pant. You can wear them casually or dress them up. They are not see through even though they are lightweight. I wore them on a chilly day and discovered they are for warmer weather days. Great quality. Looks nice on. Length is good without being to long. I'm 5’5 and they fit nicely in length. I got the black color and it’s a really rich looking pant.

Wow! These slacks exceeded my expectations, because of the quality of the cloth, great fit, and slimming look. I am 5’8” 143 lbs, and ordered 10 tall. They are the perfect length, I think if you were any shorter, they might be too long. Also, they were very cinched at the waist, so I don’t think I could’ve gone down a size. These will be a wardrobe staple for years to come.

A beautiful pair of pants that are truly high waisted. The pants makes my waist look slimmer, legs longer, and overall taller. For reference, I got a XS. I'm 5'1" 114 pounds and these were perfect. They were a little long but in just rolled them a little to fit right. These thigh highs are so cute and sexy. I love the design which gives them a more dressy look to pair with skirts and dresses. I actually bought these on the black and beige. The beige is a color thats a little harder to match with outfits but the black pairs with just about anything. My husband absolutely loves these on me. I was worried about them being see through once they stretched out on my legs but that wasn't an issue. Of course if you have really thick legs they may stretch and end up being a little see through. My upper thighs are a little on the thicker side and there seemed to be a little more stretch available for anyone with thicker legs than me. These are made of a good quality thicker material and a really good deal. Highly recommend. Update: I now have these in the dark gray and white. The white is a little trickier to pair with outfits but the gray works with almost anything. I love these socks and the affordable price.

I NEVER buy pants online. I’m 5’3-1/2”, 145, almost 70 yo mom of 4 (including twins) grandmother of 6. I work remotely now and sit at a desk all day. Thick in the thighs. I was looking to replace a pair of Calvin Klein straight-legged cotton blend sweats I’ve loved and worn for years. I tried these based on other reviews and I knew I loved them the moment they hit my waist. These sweaties are buttery soft inside and out, no hang-up or pulling along thighs or across the butt. They’re opaque and perfectly dense enough for cold Arizona winter mornings with no added bulk or lining. I love pockets but usually forfeit the convenience for vanity’s sake, given my short stocky frame. I love these sweats even more because the pockets don’t accentuate my thighs. These pants fit like a soft cozy cloud from the waist down. They allow flowing, breathable movement, and the petit length is chef’s kiss perfect. I’m so comfortable in these I’m going to order a Medium just to test and see if the L is almost too roomy. Either way, I love everything about these.

These pants were beautiful, soft, and comfortable. I am 5‘8“ tall and weigh 162 pounds. I returned them because I would like them to be just a little bit longer.

I had such high hopes for the length for these. They are beautiful soft material (not for <50Temps but good for 60-70 AZ winters. Ugh just wish they were just 1-2 inches longer. I am 5"10 (all leg) a 35" inseam normally. Will be returning.

I absolutely love these! They are even more comfortable after washing on cold and very low heat. I am 5 ft tall. 120 lbs. I got a small. They are perfect. They may be an inch too long but nothing I didn’t expect or worth not keeping. Absolutely worth the price and I will be buying more. Perfect for the colder months or lounging around the house.

Everything I could ask for! Very supportive but still very comfortable. They are not too thick, but not thin either. I tend to get too hot working out in thick leggings. I am 5’6” and 140lbs and bought the medium in the 32.5” length. They just skim the floor barefoot, which is perfect for pilates, yoga, or everyday wear. For when I do wear shoes, I prefer the longer length. Pics are after a few washes. They hold up great, did not really shrink, and the best thing I love is that every piece of lint or pet hair does not stick to them. The material is fabulous for not being clingy!

These flare leggings are soft and comfortable and fit okay. I'm 5'2" so regular length pants are typically way too long on me--the size small graze the floor, so while they're a tad too long, I can still wear them. I like how supportive yet stretchy the waistband is, and they're supportive in the rear, even giving it a bit of lift. One issue I have is that they bunch up at the backs of my knees, and it looks weird. Maybe it's because they're a little too long on me.

I can’t remember the last time I had workout pants like these that are this comfortable. The fabric is smooth and buttery soft. It fits and looks amazing on. These are great for yoga, pilates, etc. I personally wouldn’t walk around in them for everyday wear but if you have to run into the store after working out in them I totally wouldn’t be embarrassed - just saying! I think having different lengths is such a bonus. I hate pants that are too short or too long so picking it according to your height is great. I think it runs true-to-size. For reference I’m about 5’5, about 140 lbs and my waist is a 27, hips 40. The medium fits very well. The waistband isn’t tight at all and it does have that tummy control ability which is like a miracle that it isn’t tight, yet it smooths the stomach. How the heck did they achieve that?!

Satisfied with my purchase, I wish the inseam was a bit longer for wearing heels. I’m a little taller than 5’7” height and I weight is 172 lbs. I ordered a size 14 long; the inseam on these jeans is 30-31 inches.

Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Still keeping tho because I think it’s one of those things only I’d notice. Comfy & cute - really excited about this purchase! I wore them with a tube top yesterday & it was super cute!

Cute but remind more of sweatpants/pajama type pants than pants youd like wear out and about or to work. They are comfortable and the sizing and length is amazing. Im 5'8 and usually pants end up being high waters on me but these are perfect with a little extra legnth

Love love love these pants!! Im 5’7” and I ordered the XL long and they’re so great. they are pretty long because I like it that way but maybe I could’ve done the regulars? but they’re super comfy and so great. I wish they were in other colors cause I would be ALL over it!!
Perfect for tall girlies! I’m 5’9 and the large was perfect waist and length wise!

I took a chance and ordered these pants for my wife, and I'll be honest, buying clothes for her online is always a bit of a gamble. But this time it totally paid off. She loves them! The biggest challenge is always getting the size right, so I want to share what worked for us. My wife is 5'4" and about 150 lbs, and we ordered the Large. She said the size was true and the fit is perfect—not too baggy and not too tight. She's been raving about how soft and comfortable the material feels. Her favorite feature is the adjustable ankle cuffs that you can cinch up. Its always a good sign when she wears something I bought her more than once, and she's been living in these for casual stuff and lounging around the house

This review is for the “ Tall Women's Workout Leggings 32"/34"/36" Inseam Seamless High Waist Tummy Control Scrunch Gym Athletic Yoga Pants” I was so excited to get leggings that for a change reach past my calves and ankles. And these leggings are really that long, I got the XL in 34”inseam. But….nowhere in the listing can you see that they have a cinched or scrunched up back seam. they call it butt lifting. for me it is crack squeezing. I very much dislike it. I am in my sixties and I don’t need to look sexy, I just wat to lift my weights. i havenseveralothertallpantsfrom this company and this is my first disappointment with them.I highly recommend to include a picture of the backside of the leggings straight out of the bag so ladies understand what they are getting. In addition the bottom seams of the legs are sewn very uneven, not he best craftsmanship. My actual rating is 2.5 stars that gets automatically rounded up to 3 stars

These leggings are nice, comfortable, and have a smooth feel to them. Somehow I hadn’t noticed that the product listing says they’re made for tall people, and since I’m 5’3”, they turned out very long on me (as you can see in the pictures). Still, they look very pretty overall. The back design seemed a little strange before I put them on, but once worn, it actually made sense and looked flattering. The waistband is snug with a good tummy control feature, though the very top of the waist does roll down slightly when bending. The color is beautiful in person, and I really like how they look once on. They don’t have any pockets, which is a drawback, but overall I’d recommend them if you don’t mind the extra length.

I didn’t realize the name of the brand or read the description. I received these and didn’t realize how long these were. I’m 5’1 and will be wearing this in the house so please be mindful of this haha. Overall, the quality is great, VERYYY stretchy and great for activewear. It’s fitting and well. Bunched up because obviously it’s not made for petite people (my bad). I’d recommend if I were taller!

I have a 35” inseam and have struggled my entire life to find pants that fit. These leggings are amazing. I wore them on a hike to really test them out and they stayed in place without needing to be pulled up, and the thickness is just right. The high waist gives some support (not extreme tummy control, but enough to feel comfortable). I ordered the 36” inseam, and when they say they’re designed for women 6’9” and taller, they are NOT kidding, they are long! For the first time ever, I’ll actually be hemming leggings shorter, and I couldn’t be happier about that. Other details I love: no front seam (huge selling point), and the scrunch seam in the back gives a nice lift to the bum. Where have these been all my life? Tall ladies, if you’ve been frustrated by too-short leggings, these are the real deal.

These really are for tall women. I am 5'9 and even with their shortest 32-in inseam it is slightly long on me. However, I do love these for wearing with boots or sandals since I can bring the pant all the way down past my ankles. In addition, it does not pull on the waistband since there's plenty of length in the legs. The material itself has a great tight stretch to it. I really love the extra wide waistband which also has quite a good cinch. They are very thick and do have quite a good compression.

The actual fit of the Jean was fine and pretty true to size, but giving 3 stars because they were too short. For reference, I am 5’9 and ordered the longs and they were still too short. I would save the money and get a different pair if you are 5’9 and above.

I’m 5’7 on a good day and about 135 pounds. Bought I size 6. I have long legs. Literally no ass muscles I honestly don’t know how I get around… my gluteus Maximus has been compared to a pancake. I just keep waiting for that to become cool. As a 90s teen, I prefer my jeans dragging on the ground but these are close enough since people wear pee wee Herman floods these days (what have we come to ). Super comfy, nice and stretchy. High wasted —almost a bit much but better than the alternative. 10/10 recommend

These are by far my most favorite pants ever!!! I am a teacher but can get away with wearing them to work. They look dressy enough that people don't know they're yoga pants. The fake back pockets are great. I have 2 black and 1 navy. I tried a lighter color but I think it shows too many bumps (cellulite) on the legs and back side. I'm a size 6-8 (135lb) and love the medium fit of these. I get them when they go on sale that way I feel like I'm getting more bang for my buck. They wash great too. Just comfortable and nice looking. I've even worn them out to a nice dinner before with a sweater. They look cute with sneakers (adidas type) or I'll often wear them with black loafers.

These pants are amazing! At 165 lbs and 5’9”, I usually struggle to find the right length, but these fit like they were made for me. They cinch my waist perfectly.my picky teenage daughter told me she loves the way these fit on me, they are so very flattering,

I've been wearing these pants around the house and out on errands for a couple weeks, and all-in-all, I do enjoy wearing them. I like the wine color. The feel of the fabric isn't particularly high quality (it feels a bit like material I'd expect on a budget swimsuit, but thicker). The fabric is comfortable, but doesn't offer much warmth. I'm 5'8" with long legs, so I'm always excited to see pants with a long/tall inseam option! These pants don't have a gusset sewn in so I wear underwear with them, and the material isn't terribly forgiving as far as Visible Panty Line goes. I do like the relatively large pockets, and I wish there were back pockets, too. As you can see from the photo, these aren't terribly flattering on me, but that's the case with most pants unfortunately. The waistband is comfortable, and with a longer top on, I can still hide my belly. Overall, these pants are pretty average. I got them because of the long inseam option, and I'll continue to wear them, but I think you could likely find something higher quality at a similar price point.

I'm a 5'11", just over 200lbs, curvy girl. These jeans are fabulous!! The length in the photos are at the original cuffed lenght. If you need something really long to wear with heels they can be unruffled, but have a great tall cuff to be worn with flats.

I am 6ft tall & 145 pounds long torso, long legs! Uncuffed, I would say these are 36-37 inseam and perfect for me with a 2.5 inch heel. I sized up from my usual 6 to an 8 per other reviews and I think I could’ve still done a 6 but an 8 is very comfortable and they may shrink a bit after washing! Quality is great. Very heavy and not cheaply made. 10/10!

Obsessed! Great dupe for the high end yoga-like work pants. (Love those but at $100+ a pair, oof!) Hard to find pants that work for my Tory bitty inseam (26”). Do you know how hard it is to find pants that are actually long enough?!? It’s basically the quest for the Holy Grail, but these SEVEGO 36" inseam leggings have answered my tall-person prayers. They fit as expected, the dark gray color is sleek and versatile, and the material is soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to make me briefly forget I’m actually wearing pants. The high waist is supportive without feeling like a corset, and the deep pockets are a life-changing gift!! I can carry snacks, my phone, and maybe even the remote if I’m too tired to move. Quality-wise, they feel solid and worth the money. Value-wise, also a win! it's basically getting comfort, style, and functionality in one stretchy package. My only complaint is that the back seam sits just a little lower than my waist, which makes me tug at them occasionally, but it’s nothing deal-breaking. Honestly, I’m already planning to buy these in every color and build an army of tall leggings...so I never have to feel betrayed by “capri-length” imposters again. Pros: Fit tall people without accidental ankle exposure, soft and stretchy material, high supportive waist, deep pockets, nice color, good quality, good value. Cons: Back seam rides a little lower than ideal, may cause occasional adjusting.

I've gained some weight recently and regular jeans just don't work for me anymore. I find them to be too stiff or too tight or too restrictive so I've purchased a couple "jeans" from this brand before because I love how comfortable they are. This pair is no different even though the style is not exactly the same. These "jeans" are not the usual jeggings that are totally fake and look fake (jeans print). They actually look and feel like real jeans but only thinner, softer, and more stretchy. They are pull-on pants with the crossover wrap high waist like yoga pants and flare legs like bell-bottoms. I'm 5'1" and 130lbs and I usually get the shorter 29" inseam so I can wear them with sneakers. These are a little long for me (31" inseam), but I can wear them with boots or other heels. Also, all the pockets are functional and I'm a sucker for pockets!

I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

Love these! I usually wear an 8L but ordered a 10L after reading reviews that they feel small. I think it’s because it’s not the stretchy denim most jeans are made out of lately. I love the structured waist that doesn’t roll at the button. I have a 34” inseam and these are perfect.

Update: At time of order, sizing included choosing inseam length. It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

I’m 5’7” and 260lbs for reference. I got a 2xl and if I would’ve ordered a smaller size these would’ve been too tight everywhere else. The length is a little shorter than I like too. I’m very disappointed in the overall design and fit of these. As someone who is a very physically demanding medical field these are not it. If you don’t want to be disappointed I wouldn’t buy these.

First, the boots arrived faster than promised and were perfectly packaged to survive the rain at my doorstep! I ordered a half size bigger (7) than my shoe size (6.5) so I would have room for a layer or two of warm socks. The fit is roomy but not so much that my foot slides around when I walk. These boots will be perfect for walking the beaches of Northern California where the weather is often damp and chilly. I wanted tall boots to keep lapping waves from splashing inside and these come right up to my knee. I'm only 5'1" with proportionally short legs, so that might not be the case for those with long legs. The sturdy construction and materials look like they will last for many years. The buckles are a little hard to adjust, but I wasn't planning on cinching or loosening each time I pull them on or off. I am very happy with my purchase! *Updated: I saw some negative reviews by people who think their boots weren't genuine Hunter so I included a shot with the official red box and the Hunter stamp on the sole. Mine are the real deal!

I'm confused by the reviews that recommend going a size down. I am between a 7.5 and 8 in all my shoes and ordered an 8 and it fits perfectly. Without socks, yes, it's a bit loose--but I'm not sure why one would not wear socks with boots. The boots are definitely tall so if you're on the shorter side, they may not work for you. I'm 5'8" and the boots come up to just below my knee. (See photos--I took them in shorts so the boot placement in relation to my knees is clear.) They appear authentic and I got them for a great deal. I like the navy blue better than the black (returned a black pair from another seller) and am keeping the navy. Took one star off because they are a bit bulky and fold awkwardly at the ankle when you walk, distorting the shape of the boot (but then again, I'm not sure you can expect anything different from tall, rubber boots). I'm looking forward to trying them out in the fall.

Fit: I typically wear a 7.5 and I went with a 8 which fit perfectly. I'm 5'2.75 and 110 lbs and the calf opening is perfect. The height of the boots is tall, hit right below my knees. I've had no problems wearing these with jeans, dress pants, no pants etc. Quality: All the seams are really well constructed. The packaging came in the original hunter box and was wrapped very well. Some folks received defective products with odd films on it. I definitely did not have this problem. Brand new, no defects! I've used the rain boots several times in heavy rain and had no issues with leaks or wet socks. Keeps my feet nice and warm and I could care less if I step into a huge puddle! I will say these boots are slightly heavy. I don't think I would walk all day in them. Color: The navy is matte and is a true navy. Not too dark. Pairs really well with pretty much any color rain jacket. Price: I got these for right around 107 which I thought was a good deal for Hunter boots! I've seen several pairs for way more.

These are exactly what I was hoping for. They are quite warm, comfortable, and stretchy. I live in freezing Ohio, and these provided the warmth and look I was going for. I ordered a medium and I’m about 5’5 150 lbs. I ordered black/brown. The color on the inside looks quite light but once you put them on, they are very brown girl friendly. They look as if I have sheer black tights on and it was perfect.

Being anemic I am always on the look for clothes that keeps me warm and comfortable. Usually when winter comes the dresses go away because I couldn't find a legging that was cute AND warm enough for me. The entire night out it felt like I was wearing warm, cozy leggings that offset having all my arms and chest exposed for this body paint and sip event! I am 5'2" 125lbs. I bought the black brown in medium which was kinda loose on me, but I was able to pull them up and they never slipped down. I really appreciated that the skin tone underneath was a good match for my complexion as that is challenging to find. I had no rips or tears and they were easy to hand wash, even withstanding the black light paint that got on them. For the cost (especially if you get them on sale) they were worth every penny and I intend on purchasing a couple more pairs.

Absolute game changer! These look amazing and they are so warm. I have worn them several times now. The fit is amazing. I’m 5’8” and had tried other brands that felt like they weren’t long enough. These fit amazing, are truly high waisted, support your stomach area, and keep you warm. Shoot they had me sweating wearing them in Church on Christmas… but there were a million people there so I can’t blame the tights. When I tell you these go with anything… I mean anything! And other brands I tried had a clear line where the fleece lining stopped so you had to wear socks or boots to cover that area. This is not true with this brand!! Love them- have 2 and will get more!

I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

So soft and perfect for short girls. I’m only 4”11 and it is so hard to find flare leggings for short girls. These are so comfortable and have tummy control. The cut in the waist and back in a v-shape that flatters your body. These are great for everyday wear or the gym. I wish more brands made clothes suitable for short people. I love that Sunzel caters to all body types. If you are short and looking for some great slightly flare leggings, these are it!! Affordable too. No need to spend 100 on a pair of high quality leggings. Also, the waist band can be worn high waisted or you can roll it down for a more low waisted fit. I did both for the photos.

First off UA do NOT resell items that have been worn! So gross! I just read that ookie crotch review - what a nightmare! Second, I got two definitely new joggers 🙂 just did my first wash and dry. I’m 5’2” and usually buying jeans feels like choosing between tripping over extra fabric or looking like I accidentally borrowed a child’s pair. But these? The “short” length is actually short enough for me—no impromptu denim hemming project required. 🙌 The fabric is a little thicker than what I expected for stretch jeans, but honestly, I’ll take it. They hold everything in place without feeling like I’m wrapped in shrink-wrap. Let’s just say I can bend over without hearing the distant threatening rip sound that some other brands give me. Bonus: they’re well-made, comfy, and flattering. My husband said, “Nice jeans,” which in husband language is basically a standing ovation.

Let me start of by saying these boots are 🤩 amazing! I read some reviews and my experience is different. They didn’t come in a box 🤔🤨😏😒 I ordered these the same time I ordered another pair of furry boots these were due to come today which is Sunday. They came yesterday a day earlier that’s a win for that company in my book! Any I ordered a size bigger I think. I’m a true 8 but I ordered a 9 (41) and I’m glad. I have fat toes and I tried them on with the thickest sweat socks and my feet felt amazing. They were very easy to put on, the color is beautiful. No smell, they look like are very durable like they will last for years. The sole of the boot reminds me of bear paws 🐾 collection. I was a little disappointed bcuz I thought the fur would would be a lil more kinky. I wasn’t expecting the fur to be kinky straight if that make sense. I have to say it again, and the price is well worth it! For ref I’m 5’6.5” and I weigh 151 lbs slightly curvy with muscular tones.

I am 5'5", 160lbs, (small fupa & not curvy) have 4 pairs. They sit at my waist/belly button & hit right at my ankle. They are warm & comfy durable material. The elastic waistband is strong, has a drawstring, but these thick, long ankle cuffs are awesome to me, a snug fit for a good look. These have been worn & washed a few times each and there is No Pilling, No Color Fading. **I have 1 Varsity Blue, UA is stitched (great, true color), 2 Grey, UA is iron on (true color), 1 Purple, UA stitched on (more of a Pink, still love them). I hope this was to some!

I have been searching high and low for something that reminds me of the old cargo pants I used to wear back in the 90s, and these are it. For fit... I am 5' 2", 140lbs, average size 6, with a body somewhere around an athletic build. After some trial and error, I ordered these in an 8, as the 6 was tight on my rear. Once I found my fit in these, the went from meh to great. Pros: The elastic waistband is forgiving for anyone that struggles with bloating/gut issues. The rest of the fabric has some stretch, and I'm able to pull these up and down without unbuttoning. I'd say they're high rise and they don't irritate my sensitive stomach. They can be dressed up or down and work with a variety of tops. They're even good for rigorous hiking. Also. SIX FUNCTIONAL POCKETS! Not those itty-bitty teases they call women's pockets nowadays. We are talking almost on par with men's pockets (but not quite, guess they gotta keep us humble or something). The length for me is good as well. Although I'm vertically challenged, most of my height is in my legs, so I tend to wear a regular inseam. These wash just fine, no fading or bleeding. I can't speak to drying, as I hang dry. Cons: If you are super leggy, these may be too short. If you carry weight in your midsection or rear, size up. They may (?) shrink in the dryer. Overall, I highly recommend these pants. I'm wearing the darker grey in the photos, and I've come back and ordered them in multiple other colors. I basically live in these now. And I like them as much as I do my Le.vi 724s, and that is saying something.

I’m 5’-8”, 155lbs, usually a size 12. These pants are supposed to be straight cut but they are not. It’s more like a barrel cut with way too much fabric at the hips and generally around the crotch. The first photo is with the extra fabric pulled out. I am not shaped like that. I don’t think anyone is. The fit is weirdly baggy at the upper thighs and more straight cut at the calves. The back side is horrible with extra fabric in all the wrong places. They stretched out to a super baggy fit after working in them for 1 day. Not attractive or flattering.

These fit true to size. I’m 5’4, 135lbs. I’m in a size 6. It is almost impossible to find good cargo pants for women and these happen to be perfect. The elastic in the waist band makes them super comfortable. The pockets are deep, generous and plentiful. Some times at work I may have 3 phones, box cutter, markers and a set of keys and it’s no problem with these pants. I bought 2 pair, black and the blue and workmanship on both are excellent. Highly recommend. (no smell)

The current style of longer length pants is a welcome trend for 5'10" height, and these pants are no exception. The elastic waistband with drawstring sit just above my natural waist; again, a welcome trend to hide a not-so-trim belly. The wide barrel-style legs come right to my ankles. The vibrant red color is just as the depicted, and the side pockets are a convenient feature. I struggle to describe the fabric, since it isn't the cotton fabric of traditional sweatpants, nor is it polyester; yet, I wouldn't call it a cotton-poly blend. The pants arrived with wrinkles, but those didn't last long. They are machine washable and should be line dried, but I put the new pair in a dryer with a slightly damp towel on low heat, and the wrinkles came out. These sweatpants are very comfortable and I love the way they fit. Since my size varies quite a bit (between 138lbs to 152lbs), I ordered a Large, but could have also gone with a Medium due to their design. My advice is to order your typical size. *If you're tall female who has gibmven up on finding reasonably priced sweatpants, Ihope you give these pants a try!*

I still loved the fit and added a belt for style. I ordered another color in size L to compare the fit around waist. Although the XL is already extremely comfortable but I do anticipate losing more weight. They are gorgeous!!

I only bought this pair bc I wanted to make sure I like them. Im definitely getting more!

The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

This is my new favorite lounge set. I wore it 5 days in a row before my husband made a comment about how he hadnt seen me wear anything else in a week. I ordered the pants and matching crew pullover, both fleece lined. Im 50 years old and starting to get regular hot flashes. The inside fleece lining is just a thin layer to soften the texture, but not enough to make me feel overheated. The outside modal fabric is so soft to the touch. Im 5'2" and 140 lbs and I ordered size small for the top and small with a 27 inseam for the pants. Ive washed the set numerous times without any shrinking or fading. Ive noticed some light pilling inside on the fleece fabric on the top/back part of the top. My only complaint would be that the navy color is muted, which makes it look faded. I wouldve preferred a richer, darker navy.

I was looking for the “soft and comfy” wide leg dupe pant similar to lulu. Other pants that I had purchased were either too short, or not the exact material I wanted. However these are great! I was looking for more of a wide leg; however I paired these with an oversized sweater and they were perfect. The color was a true cream color which doesn’t look yellow (I feel like other brands always offer a white color option but not many have cream). The pants are a medium thickness (enough to keep you warm) however not too thick where they still have some movement. Lastly, the fabric is the soft lululemon type of material (no sheen). I’m 5’9 and weigh 155-160 depending on the day. I would describe my thighs as thicker/average and I hate when pants are too tight around the thighs, but these weren’t.

Surprisingly Flattering and High-End Quality! I couldn’t believe how great these jeans are! I’ve always been loyal to high-end denim brands, and these totally exceeded my expectations. Right now I’m in between sizes—I’ve gained some weight but am already on my way back down. I ordered both the size 14 and the 12 in short length. I normally wear a 14 (I’m 5’2” and 180 lbs), and the 14s fit comfortably, but I was shocked to find that the 12s fit too! They were snug, but the material is stretchy, soft, and extremely forgiving—surprisingly comfortable even in the smaller size. Both sizes looked amazing in the mirror. The short length hits just right for me when I’m wearing 3–4 inch heels, which is my go-to look. In these images I’m wearing heels, about 3.5 inches. The jeans graze the floor in a flattering way and have that perfect bootcut silhouette that works beautifully over high-heeled boots. If you look at the product photos, you’ll see they fit like a dream and give that sleek, elongated leg effect. One thing to note: the short length runs a little longer than expected, so I wouldn’t wear them with sandals unless I hem them slightly—but with heels? Perfection. I highly recommend these jeans, especially if you’re curvy and love a high-end look at a great price. I’ll probably keep the size 12 as my motivation jeans, but honestly, both sizes are a win. 👖✨

The G4Free AiryBlend Wide Leg Sweatpants are well made and very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric, it has a nice drape to it that makes the fit very flattering and it doesn’t show every little bump and bulge. The fabric has a very slight shine that elevates the look of these pants. All the seams are neatly finished both inside and out. These sweatpants are a step up from your average sweatpants and to me, they seem more refined and slightly dressier. You could wear these pants anywhere. They are not just for the gym. There is a cinch cord at the waist that works so much better than the usual ties. The cinch cord allows you to quickly and easily adjust the size of the waist and it holds firmly in place. The pants are just the right fit, not too loose or too baggy. I ordered the 31" inseam and the pair I received is actually a 32" inseam. I like that these come in regular and tall sizes because we all know that one size does not fit all.For reference, I am 5’9” and I weigh 195 lbs. I normally wear an XL and I ordered these in an XL. These pants fit me well but the waist does seem a little smaller and tighter than my usual XL sweatpant's waist. But they are still comfortable enough to wear all day. The length for me was perfect and they don’t require any hemming. These sweatpants are well made, look great and feel great.

I got the medium petite, I am about 5'2". I am currently 24 weeks pregnant. I have been struggling to find good maternity scrubs that fit but don't break the bank. I found these because of other reviews talking about using these during pregnancy. These are the best option I have found so far because the waistband goes below the belly, but still stays up. The waistband is stretchy and comfortable. I am hopeful that they will work for the whole pregnancy - fingers crossed. I appreciate the size of all the pockets, although I'm not sure what I would realistically use the waist and pocket for - I think it goes all the way around the waistband, so I would be afraid I would lose anything I put in there. The pants are good quality, the material is lighter-weight which is great because I tend to get hot easily. The fit is good except for the length. Things that would have made it 5 stars for me would be back pockets, shorter pant legs (typically petite works well for me but these were still a little long, even with my shoes).

I have been in the healthcare field for 12 years I bought these pants because I’m finally in nursing school and the scrubs that they gave us at the school are terrible they’re just so uncomfortable they are the drawstring unisex scrubs, like the black sheep of all scrubs. I bought these pants and tried them on and in the 12 years that I’ve worked in hospitals these are the MOST COMFORTABLE, MOVABLE, STRETCHY scrub pants I have EVER worn. I have three children, my twins and my baby girl all via C-sections so my scar is very uncomfortable with different pants and I still have a little mom pooch from my last one I hate when scrub pants roll down or when you do find pants that are “high waisted” but are so tight it’s just uncomfortable however these pants stayed in place all 12 hours I wore them and they are extremely stretchy but they stay where they need to be. They definitely are made for the curvy women. I can’t say enough that these pants are the most comfortable pants I have ever worn moving around pulling on patients bending over and squatting down you can move so freely in these pants if I could give these pants 10 stars I would I added pictures to show you how they look and on the pant legs as well. You can see that there’s a drawstring so if you buy them Long they’re definitely long enough to look professional with Dansko‘s or clogs but also if you like joggers you could probably buy them short and pull on the drawstring and they’ll kind of be against your ankle rather than hanging over your shoes (check out my pictures) either way these things are the bomb they are so comfortable I cannot get over how comfortable they are I only ordered one pair for clinical and after wearing them for one day I ended up ordering more and I ended up ordering a bunch for my actual job because I don’t think I’m gonna wear any other pants for work & school! THE ONLY SLIGHT DOWNFALL that some people may find is the material it’s like the polyester material it’s not cotton and I don’t think there’s any cotton except for the waistband I know some people sweat while these pants are good for comfort they might not be the best option if you tend to sweat let’s be honest I’m just gonna say it, if you tend to easily get “swamp booty” then I would recommend wearing 100% cotton panties with these bc honestly the material is so clingy and soft it’s won’t ride up your butt. I’m just keeping it real ladies.All in all I love these pants I’m so glad I ordered definitely 100% worth every penny!!SD: I am 5”10 200Ibs “slim-thick”

I really really really want to love these pants. But they are just not for my body type. I’m about 5’2 120lbs, 34-27-36 with a short torso and long legs. And for me these pants are wayyyy too long. But the worst part is that the torso is really long and I either have to have the waist up to my bra line or have the crotch sagging like the 90’s style Mc. Hammer pants (shown in 3rd to last pic). So I would say that these may be more fitting for those that are curvy and really tall. There were also a few odd strings hanging but over all I feel like the material is good quality and if you are tall and curvy then these will look amazing on you.

These lounge pants are well made. The style looks nice. The material is a 66% polyester/34% cotton blend, that is about a medium thickness. I'm not a fan of the feel of the material. It reminds me a lot of my mom's polyester slacks she wore (and made me wear too!) back in the '70's. This pair of pants is not what I would term as sweatpants. There is no fleece inside. To me they feel like a pair of slacks that got styled to be lounge pants. There is nothing wrong with the quality of these pants. The quality is great. It's just a personal preference for me, because of the way the material feels. I did really like the style/look of them. I ordered them in a size XL, my normal size. The waist fits nicely and is quite comfortable. Plenty of hip room with the bagginess of the legs. However, the length was a little long for my 5' 4" height. They sort of sweep the floor for me. The waistband is wide and elastic with a drawstring for size adjustment as needed. I will recommend these lounge pants as they are great quality for those who like or don't mind the full on polyester feel of the material. They are quite comfortable to wear, though a little long for 5' 4" and shorter if ordering an XL size. There are several colors to choose from. The price of $34.99 (8-31-2025) is a bit on the high end and needs to be lower to make them a good value, in my opinion. I gave them a 4 star rating because they are well made and look nice.

I ordered a size 8 (regular) based off of reviews. After trying them on I should have gone with a 6. The material is very nice and comfortable, not scratchy in any way. They're just way too big and baggy for me. I am 5'1 and about 118lbs. They fit nicely around my waist but not my knees and they do the weird ruffle thing when I sit or bend. They are also very stretchy so even though they fit nicely on my waist I can still stretch them out.

I just got these jeans so I might update as I actually start wearing them day to day but so far I like the fit. I am 5’9 174 lbs and I ordered a 14 long. These pants are AMAZING. The material feels thick, but with some stretch. The waistband is wide enough to not feel too pinchy, and I LOVE how it made my butt look. I’m 5’9, hourglass build with wide hips, a fuller bust, and long legs. I got a size 8 Long, and they fit beautifully. They fall just above the ankle, so if my fellow tall ladies want your ankles covered keep that in mind. They look polished and keep you looking snatched. I would say these fit true to size. I vary between a 6 to a 12 depending on the clothes but usually an 8-10 is where I fit best. I’ll be getting these in more colors! These are great jeans to wear to the office or on a fun night out, and the price point is great for what they are. :) I didn’t notice a smell and I haven’t gotten to wash them yet, but I’ll see how they fare in the washer. Overall though, they get five stars from me. :)

These pants are THICK and suuuper soft. Really heavy weight fabric with a smooth exterior and soft fuzzy interior, triple stitched elastic for no rolling, and a nice slightly flared leg. I got the petite size large and they are maybe still an inch too long for me at 4’11”, and the waistband is a tiiiiny bit tight so I probably could have swung an XL for maximum comfort, but overall these are amazing quality and extremely warm and soft and will look great dressed up or down

These are soooooo comfy and soft inside. I got the longer length (32” inseam) in XS and they fit perfectly, fitting right on my waist (high-waisted on me). For reference, I am 130lbs and 5’7 and have a shorter torso but longer legs. They are the perfect length to go past my ankles. The side pockets are super comfortable and flattering and the inner pocket lining is made of this silky smooth fabric. The taupe color is more of a muted periwinkle brown which I actually like better. These pants feel really high quality. Something to note though is that the waistband is pretty thick and there is no string to adjust the waist. Make sure to pay attention to the size chart for waist measurements especially. If my experience with these being high-rise is more universal and others also felt these were more high-waisted pants, then it’s all the more reason to make sure to adhere to the size chart for waist size. There are no back pockets but the side pockets are really deep and easily fit a phone and a wallet/ keys. I think the price for these pants is honestly worth it. They feel super high quality and you can tell just based on the seams and stitching alone. I’m honestly keeping an eye out for a matching sweatshirt from them in the same color because I love these so much! 100% recommend.

I am 5'7 146 lbs with a curvy figure. I ordered a medium and the fit perfectly. I compare these to the "comfrt" brand sweats that everyone is wearing. They definitely are comparable with a couple of differences. The first is the sweat material weight. It is slightly and I mean just slightly less thick than the comfrt brand which for me is good since they are not so heavy and hot to wear. It also allows the waistband to be less bulky and sits more comfortably than the comfrt brand. The rise is great! I like that the rise is not too long so you don't have to roll the band down to be comfortable. The sit just above the waistline and are slightly contoured for your hips so less bulk at the hip area. The pockets are nice side slits and are designed not to gap. The pockets are lined with a thinner material so no bulking at the hip area. The length was great for my 5'7 height in medium regulars but they also have petite and tall sizes too. This is the second pair of "G4Free" brand pants I ordered. I also have the thinner drawstring yoga pants which I love and have more on order. This brand is great and the price reflects the quality. If you are sitting on the fence about them just buy them you won't regret it!

These pants are so comfortable. Really impressed with the quality because I wasn't sure what to expect and the price point was a little high, but they are nice and thick, SO soft, and a good fit. Definitely comparable to other name brands. I ordered the taupe in Small and they are perfect. They hit right above the floor with sneakers and drag a tiny bit when I'm barefoot. I'm 5'5", 125 lbs with athletic frame and small hips. These have a relaxed cut but not so baggy that they are unflattering. They're so cozy for lounging at home but have a nice finish that I'm fine wearing out of the house. I've worn them working from home, to physical therapy, and running errands. The pockets are also nice and big so my cell phone fits when I'm chasing my toddler around the house or walking the dog.

It’s getting cooler outside, so it’s definitely sweatpants season. I’m 5’6” tall and my measurements are 30 inch waist and 42 inch hip. My inseam is about 32 inches. I ordered a size large and they fit snuggly. The length was perfect. The fleece on the inside is warm and the counting on the outside is very soft. These are exceptionally thick. They’re perfect if you wanna wear a pair of long johns or fleece tights underneath if you live in freezing temperatures. My climate is on the warmer side so I can get away with just wearing these as is. I love the pockets and overall the quality of material. These are super comfortable. They’re a little on the pricey side for a pair of sweats, but I guess we live in times of inflation.

Firstly, review and trust the size chart. I should have ordered a small instead of medium; that’s my fault completely. I’m hoping they’ll shrink a bit after a couple of washes. Besides that, these are soft, warm, thick and flattering. The leg is not too flared, they have just the right width. Quality stitching and fabric. A tiny bit long for me at 5’2”, but they don’t touch the floor, so no hem adjustments will be needed. Generous size pockets. They are definitely sweatpants but with a dressier lounge aesthetic, fine to wear out and about.
They feel like great quality jeans. I love how they hug hips and thighs. They fit perfectly. I am 5'2 and prefer the 32 length vs the 30. It was short for me.
I think they were popular lol they fit really well. Love the 36” inseam, tall girl friendly. They wash well! Need more colors!

I am a little on the taller side (5’9”) and have been trying to find jeans that I can wear with heels. It has been a trying time in my life lol. I ordered the 28x36 and found the jeans to be comfortable and long enough to wear with a heel without a platform, although the inseam actually measures 35”. The biggest problem is that the rear pocket placement is really unflattering and makes your butt look like your cheeks are trying to fly off in opposite directions. I can’t get past this so unfortunately I’ll have to return the jeans and continue my search.
5’11” 209lbs 14 The pants are made well, seem to fit nice but short without ordering long.

My measurement are approximately 41 inch hips, 30.5 inch waist, and pants size 8-10 or 29 depending on the brand. I am kind of in between sizes per their chart and went with large. These don't hug my curves and are somehow a bit loose in the butt but a bit tight across the front hips, causing the pockets to flare. I'm not sure if sizing down would have given me a more flattering fit since they already look tight in the front. I got the 31 inch inseam and at 5'6" find them pooling a bit at the bottom. They're passable for "dress" pants if your office is casual or you're only concerned with meeting the letter of your business casual dress code and not the actual vibe, which is exactly how I picked my work clothes when I had an office job, so I'd wear them to work. Overall I don't find them flattering, but I do think they're very cozy. I'll use them as lounge wear or for my current job in pet care. I laundered in cold water and dried on low heat with no problems.

I'm 5'6", 175# and ordered an XL. These pants are a nice weight -- not too thick. I was concerned because they are described as fleece lined, but it's not a heavy material at all. I love the pockets and think they are of a high quality design and make. I did find them to be extremely long so had to bring them to a seamstress. The fit felt comfortable, but as I look at the rear view, ahem! I would consider them to be winter/fall pants but wouldn't consider them to be particularly warm -- more cozy.
I’m 5’7” and 160lbs and ordered the 28 x32” originally. I can squeeze into it but I’m going to try the 29 x 34 next to see if that’s a better fit. Seems like a decent pair of jeans.

These are perfect!! They are comfortable and stylish. They keep water out. Good grip on the bottom. I walk around a very dusty gravel parking lot for my job, and these boots are great because they can just get dirty (and they can easily be rinsed off) and so you don't have to worry about ruining nice shoes. I'm very tall, 6 ft, and these were the tallest rain boots I could find. Be aware that these boots have no ventilation at all, so your FEET WILL DEFINITELY SWEAT if you wear them for hours. I recommend 100% cotton socks to avoid odors and absorb the moisture.

I'm in my first year of nursing school, so I decided to try these out. My size varies depending on brand and style, so I got a medium and large to compare. The first two mirror pictures are large, second two medium. I am 5'6-7 and about 165 lbs. on a good day- pretty normal proportions. I also get a bit bloated depending on the time of month, so I took that into consideration as well. The medium fit perfectly- as in it was snug in the right areas and didn't make me feel too constricted. I tried squatting/bending over a few times and there was no movement around the waist or ankles. I hate when my pants fall down throughout the day, so this was definitely a plus. The large was more comfortable in my opinion since I lean more towards baggy clothes in my day-to-day style. However, they fell slightly when I bent/squat and rustled when I walked. They also were not flattering at all. Mediums were the right choice. Both fell right at my ankle bone but of course, the large was baggier. It's just up to preference. Other things to note- Very soft, big pockets, sturdy elastic. However, they could've been a bit stretchier. That's the only downside I've noticed so far. I have not worn these to clinical yet, so we'll see how they hold up.

Nice scrubs! 5’8”’172 lbs. Large long in the pants, and XL in top. I’m a 38C and broad shoulders. I don’t like tight scrubs, so these sizes fit well on me. Full high waist which I love. These scrubs won’t break your heart! Just get them already!!

I'm 5'3" at 250 lbs and I knew to order up a size. I can wear 1x/2x depending on material and designer. I ordered a 20W petite and a 22W petite. The material was comfortable and the thread count made it very durable. It has the feel of a solid work pant for sure. I love that there are real pockets that are of a good size. However, the 20W did not fit. The 22W did but I had to suck in a bit... Thankfully I am squishy. I recommend anyone who has my body type to order up twice. ( I also bought a shape wear that I was trying on so that's why the one pic has it... News flash, it didn't help)

I am 16weeks, bought 2XL, 5'8, 230 pounds and growing. Only reason I'm giving them a 4 star is the god awful smell. My husband said it smelt like bad cigarettes. I think it had a hard chemical smell that gave me a bad headache... but at least it came out in the wash (mostly, not bad now). They are buttery soft, every breathable but still keeps the belly warm. Also pass the husband test. No see-through unless you're touching your toes, but even then he said not unless you are looking directly at your butt trying to see it. I'd say tall girl friendly, and thick girl friendly. They have an extra seem down the leg to give more room for us thick thigh girls. I'll definitely buy again.

Update: 6 weeks post c section and I love these more than ever. Feels like a gentle abdominal binder and makes sneezing not hurt - like a little elastic hug lol Let me preface this by saying, I just got this today. Will update if something weird happens in the future 😅 Stats on me in case it helpa anyone else with sizing - im 5ft even. 125 pre pregnancy weight but about 154 right now at 32 weeks pregnant. Usually I wear a size 6 pants. I ordered the small, knowing a wanted something that would give me a little more support, and theyre snug in a supportive way without being uncomfortable. I love it, feels like I'm being held in lol. Every time I sneeze I have to grip my belly or it feels like my abs are going to burst open (yay diastasis recti!) but when I have these on, I don't need to! I imagine they'll also be nice for making me feel held-in after my scheduled c section. Yes, they come nearly to my boobs but I personally prefer that
Actually very cute, and extremely soft. For reference I am 5’3”, 130lbs, small fits perfect. Need to roll waist up once to mae length right, but I like doing that anyways. Good value for the money!

I have tried Figs, Mandala, Greys Anatomy, the list goes on… and none of them compare to these. Those brands fit my body (5’4” and 107 lbs) like a trash bag and I was feeling discouraged that there was a brand out there for me. I took a chance on Med Couture and I’m really glad I did. I bought an XS top and bottom (regular). They are very comfortable and lightweight and fit me the best out of any brand I’ve ever tried. If I look good I feel good! I already bought 3 pairs and plan on getting more. Make sure you wash them before wearing them, I bought the wine color and the color leaked onto my underwear but it came out in the wash, just be aware. They are a little pricey but in my opinion totally worth it and still less expensive than Figs. Highly recommend!

Let me influence you !!! This was a yes 100% yes I was so nervous about ordering these pants however after receiving them and feeling the material they are fantastic. I’m in love with these boots! I’ve been using them for about a month now, and as a full-time RV traveler they’ve quickly become essential. They’re flexible, comfortable, and unlike most rain boots, they don’t flare out awkwardly at the calf. On me (5'2") they fit like tall boots and honestly look super stylish, not clunky at all. The quality is excellent, clearly made to last, and they feel much lighter than traditional rubber boots without sacrificing durability. Cleaning is a breeze, and I really appreciate the thoughtful details like the pull tab and reflective strip. At a fair price, these are a no-brainer if you want rain boots that combine fashion with function. Highly recommend!
