For reference, I ordered an XL/31" inseam based on the sizing chart - I can't confirm if they're actually XL since the tag looks like it was chewed away or something. The pants are very comfortable and fit well, they are just not made that well. As others have noted, they're covered in lint/hair, so keep a lint roller handy. I loved that this has front and back pockets, but the front pockets weren't sewn properly so there are holes in them. I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

I can have my phone or something big in the right pocket, since that hole isn't too big, but I wouldn't risk the left since my whole hand fit through it. I would've sent them back but I needed them for the next day, so I made it work. If they were made better, I'd definitely order another pair, but I don't know if I'd want to risk it.

Alterations would still worth the cost to me, given that these were not expensive to begin with, and I love the fit otherwise.

Finally, a nice quality cargo pant for tall/curvy women! If you have been looking for a good cargo pant, look no further! The high waist sits just above my belly button, and the inseam is a great length for me- I am 5’9”, regular inseam 31-32, ordered a size 14 in black. The material feels sturdy while not being too heavy. I wanted something mostly made out of cotton for durability, so this pant was a great choice for the price. Other cargo pants just did not have all the criteria I was looking for, but this one did! The pockets are all functional, and they lay flat without sticking out awkwardly. The stitching is neatly done and seems to be good quality. I like the straight leg silhouette, it’s flattering on a fuller figure. You can easily tuck a shirt in if you wear these pants for work. I am wearing a belt in these photos, although it’s not necessary to wear a belt with these pants. They fit true to size and are comfortable. They do pick up animal hair pretty easily, but I can live with that. If they hold up good after washing them, I will definitely order in some more colors.

At first I didn’t like the look of these out of the bag, with the half-elastic waistband and slightly stiff cotton. However, on? They look cute and they feel great. I’m 5’5” and the length is perfect. Without shoes they skim the floor and with shoes they fall just over the laces. I tried them on after I washed them, to see if they shrink. I used cold water but imagine they could/will shrink over time so I air dry (since right now the fit is perfect.) I normally wear a medium top and a 6 or 8 bottom. I ordered the 6 and they fit just right. Pockets are placed well and are actually useful (not just for show.) My iPhone fits easily in the two larger front/side pockets. Back pockets are a bit smaller than typical jeans’. The waist is very comfortable and from the waistband down to the tummy, it’s slightly fitted and supportive. From there down it’s loose and wide legged—overall very flattering, even for my height. My only complaint is that it has a slight sheen to it and the cotton isn’t soft, it’s stiff and can make a bit of noise while I’m walking. Just wish it was softer and a little thicker. But since it’s thinner, it makes for a great pant for Spring or mild days.

I’m 5’6 and about 120lbs, 26” waist and 36” hips, I have a 28” inseam. I’m normally a size 2 but ordered the recommended size 4. These pants are a little looser on my waist then I like, I probably could’ve gone with a size 2. This fabric is kind of a thinner slightly stretchy twill. It feels fairly durable but also lighter weight. The cargo pockets have snaps, The rear pockets have buttons. The backside of the waistband has elastic, which kind of makes me feel like a four year old. The legs are plenty long. The stitching is mediocre at best, but good enough. They’ll work fine for being work pants as I intended it to be.

Height: 5’2, weight 110 lbs, waist 27.5”, Hips 35”, inseam 29, ordered XS, 29 inseam, color Navy Blue. Let me start by saying that the main reason for the 1-star rating is the condition in which I received these pants—they were FULL of dog or cat hair. While I do love animals, when I pay for a "new" item, the least I expect is for the item to be clean. I understand that some people with pets may try on items and return them, but I believe it's the company's responsibility to inspect returned items before sending them to another buyer. The condition I received these pants in is simply unacceptable. Putting that aside, I'd have given these pants 3 stars. Fabric: I have purchased 5 different colors of pants from this same brand, but the ones with bands around the waist area. The fabric is very comfortable and flattering, and I wear them almost every day for my small business. The fabric of these ones is the same. Size: I'm 5'1" and weigh 110 lbs. I ordered an XS with a 29 inseam, the same size I ordered in the other pants. The waist area is a bit tight, but not too bad. Considering only the waist, I might have ordered a size S instead. However, the legs are noticeably wider than the other pairs I own, about 1 to 1.5 inches wider, which I don't really like. The 29-inch length is perfect, as it's the same length as the other pants, and it looks equally good. Color: The navy blue color is very pretty. In general, I like this brand because the pants are comfortable and can be dressed up or down. However, I'm extremely disappointed with receiving pants covered in hair and not being entirely satisfied with the style. I'm not sure if I would specifically recommend these pants to friends or family.

These are so amazing and great quality- I want to keep them so bad, but the tiny elastic waistband (thickness of a hair tie) at the very top is the most annoying thing in the world. Because of the high waist, the top band hits just below the ribs, causing an unnecessary fitted feeling in a sensitive area, and I’m reminded of it every time I breathe. For some bodies it may serve a purpose, but these fit absolutely perfectly without it, and the drawstring is there to control how tight of a fit you want. If they were to remove that band or redesign, I would buy them in every color and wear every day during this New England winter! I tried a few size/length combos but the 28” Small worked best for me (5’6, 130 lbs).

I love these leggings. They aren’t see thru! They fit comfortable. I’m 5’1 and weight 145 lbs, I was hesitant to buy the small/medium but they fit amazing. They are also stretchy too. Took these to New York with me and they were perfect under my jeans, tights and anything I needed some warmth for! Hourglass/Pear approved! I’ve been searching for work pants for-ev-er and finally found a pair that fits my shape. I’m 5’7, 170lbs with wider hips and a smaller waist. These fit like a glove! They’ll be so useful in Wisconsin winters too. The length is perfect for me. I did order a size Large Tall. My natural waist sits pretty high so the extra length is necessary. There is a tight band around the very top of the pants that doesn’t stretch, but once the pants are on, they aren’t sliding down because of it! I can sit down and stand up without having to readjust my pants. The material is a tad shiny but not AS shiny as typical workout pants. Pockets are functional and hold my iPhone 14 without issue. Back pockets are NOT functional. Very stretchy but will hold their shape. NO tummy compression but the wide band helps a little. Will definitely order another pair for work.

TALL GIRL FRIENDLY for real !!! Im 5'9 and bought the extra tall. I LOVE THEM. I am100 % buying these pants in more colors. They help suck in the tummy, while being super comfortable and warm ! Perfect to dress up or down. My new favorite pants !!

These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

These pants have been my go-to for years. I have to say I think they run slightly large, as my measurements are an inch or two over the size listing and the pants are comfortably loose. I just bought a 16 and love the way they fit, but I definitely could have opted for the 14 if I wanted a more form-fitting look. I’m 5’3” and prefer to shorten the length about 1 inch, but I have worn them with the 29” short inseam as is, and they look okay that way too (just pool a little at the ankle). They machine wash and dry just fine, but I don’t use powdered detergent or oxiclean in the wash as they often collect some of it on their surface, requiring them to be wiped down with a damp cloth to remove it. I’ve seen other reviews complaining of white residue after washing. I’ve found that this can be avoided by using only liquid detergent. I love these pants and highly recommend them.

I love pull-on pants (no buttons/zippers) and have been on the hunt for a great straight leg pair. I was initially turned off by how they looked on the models but took a chance anyway & I'm so glad I did! The quality is great: very well-made, comfy pants with a nice, stretchy material that can be worn in all seasons, ideal length for me (I'm 5'5") & the waist band has a thickness to it that gives you some tummy control. I immediately ordered more and now have these in black, gray, plaid (see video) & just got the dark brown ones. Some pull-on pants can look cheap & these definitely don't- VERY happy with this purchase!

This brand specializes in longer lengths for tall people. I am 5'8" 132lbs. I ordered size M-Tall which was the 32in length. The length is absolutely perfect. The leg width and slight taper is very flattering, and the pants are well made and comfortable. I usually get a size small but based on the size chart, I ordered medium and I'm glad I did because the waist band is snug and fits like a small typically would feel on me, so check the size chart and you may also want to size up. The only thing I don't love about the pants is the cargo pockets with the big flap at the top. They add bulk to the legs and I don't find them flattering. I'd prefer no pocket or a flush, smooth zipper on top instead of the flap. In my experience, after washing, the flaps always look crinkled, crooked, and stick out more. Overall, I like the length and comfort of these pants and will look at this brand to find other styles with 32" length.

Tall ladies…you’re gonna love these pants! Why is it so hard for us tall gals, or just gals with a long inseam, to find pants that reach the foot instead of above the ankle? If you want a pair of cotton, yes cotton (another hard find nowadays), sweatpants these are awesome. Yes, a bit too long for me before washing but I expected that being about a 35 inch inseam myself. But I wanted to dry them and knowing cotton with usually shrink in the dryer, I went for it. And the fit is perfect a tad longer than floor length which is how I wear my pants. Keep them out of the dryer and you will have 36 inch inseam with no shrinkage from washing in cold water. The quality is exceptional with no loose threads and all seams sewn well. I appreciate the elastic waistband as it offers the wearer to not use the drawstring. The waistband feels soft against the skin and all the cargo pockets lie flat even after putting in the dryer. The fabric has good weight without being heavyweight - meaning the fabric is warm without the weight. The side pockets also lie along your silhouette without flaring out (quite unattractive!) and have good depth. I love these pants, and I rarely say such a thing but these are really high quality, well made athletic pants that can be worn out for errands or outdoors doing yard work or adventuring into the woods. Great product and well priced!

As a 5'9" woman with a 27" waist and 37" hips, finding joggers that don’t awkwardly hover above my ankles has been a struggle. I ordered the Small Tall (32" inseam) and—cue the happy dance—the fit is spot-on. Not only do they hit the perfect length, but they also hug in all the right places without feeling restrictive. 🧵 Fabric & Feel: When I first pulled them out of the bag, I worried they might be too heavy. But once I slipped them on, I totally changed my tune. The cotton-poly blend is soft, breathable, and surprisingly cozy—ideal for cooler months. 🎒 Pockets Galore: The cargo pockets are generously sized, which might feel a bit bold for some, but they’re super practical. I’ve stashed my phone, keys, and even a small snack bar in them while out walking. Whether you're hiking, running errands, or just lounging, these pants are ready for action. 👟 Styling Ideas: I plan to wear them with a fitted long-sleeve tee for a sporty vibe and also will wear a denim jacket and white sneakers for casual errands. Another option I see me wearing them with a slim turtleneck and ankle boots for a cozy fall look. 🎁 Final Thoughts: These joggers are perfect for tall women. They’re functional, flattering, and versatile enough to wear from morning yoga to evening Netflix. If you’ve been let down by “tall” pants that still fall short—these get it right.

These are great casual pants for hanging out around the house. They almost remind me of scrubs. I like the straight leg styling but with the ability to tighten the legs at the ankle. The drawstring is a working string so can easily tighten the waistline. I also like that although they look like they are ribbed, it is more of a pattern on the fabric. The fabric itself is flat and thin. They are VERY thin, though. It almost feels like a tensel fabric. Which could be nice on hotter days? Just know they don't provide any support and may show imperfections. I really love this brown color! It is rich and dark and provides a great break from black. FIT: True to size. I'm 5'6", 138 lbs and size 4 wearing a small.

Overall, I feel glad that I got these pants. They are lightweight, a beautiful blue color, the right cut on the waist, and match the upscale casual spring and summer style I'm looking for. I was glad that these pants were given sizeable pockets (thank you!) and even more surprised and delighted that there are actually about 6 inch slits at the outside bottoms of each pant leg, exposing the bottom of your calf and ankle. This was a nice touch that makes them more flowy and almost flare like when walking, vs. rigid straight leg. The two downsides I have: 1) They are slightly transparent in certain light, beware, as you can see in the photo- I hope that helps. 2) The other "issue" which is more of a personal issue is the length of the pant leg. I would prefer these to be longer. I would rather them almost drag on the floor when wearing flats and then be a bit highwater with heels. But instead they are at my ankle when standing, above the ankle when sitting, and seem a bit too short legged for heels at my 5' 7" stature with longer than usual legs. My wedge sandals were a bit off, but I persevered and owned the look. I'm ready for hell or high water. The stretchy waistband was very forgiving and easy to sit at any part of the waist without slipping and sliding about. I wore these all day and was at ease. Almost like wearing pajama pants out to the nail salon and dinner, but elevated. Score. Get these for upscale beachy, elongating and effortless, plus comfort and coolness in the heat of summer. These are a great value compared to linen style pants I've seen elsewhere and even cuter in many ways.

These can be great pants for the office (they look professional but feel stretchy) with a few adjustments. The navy pants run smaller so go up a size. The black pants are true to size. Make sure to try them on with the shoes you’ll wear (not barefoot) to judge length.

My colors never look right. This vest is a dark navy, not light blue.This is a great vest. Thick and well-made. Two external zip pockets and 1 interior hidden pocket. Good zippers. Water resistant. Nice and warm.The size chart is accurate. I'm a woman with a 50 inch chest and 54 inch hip/belly area. I decided to go up to the 3X and it fits loosely in the chest and comfortably in the belly. So choose the fit based on the largest portion of your body and whether you want to wear heavy shirts underneath.The one thing I don't like is that when the zipper is zipped all the way up, the neck is tight. A big and tall person is also going to have a larger neck size and the vest doesn't accommodate that well. If you had a hoodie on, or a thick gaiter, you might not be able to comfortably zip all the way up to your chin. But if you're wearing something else around you neck, you might not need to.

This is a very nice quality, and well-made white button-down shirt. I am 5'2" and 135#. I ordered this shirt in Petite Medium because I was concerned that it might run small, but it doesn't. I actually wish I had gotten the Petite Small instead. The fabric is nice and smooth, almost a satin feel to it. It's light and drapey, but not thin, so the bra doesn't show through. It seems well made with nice details on the sleeves. I do wish the cuffs would be a little bit tighter. The only thing that bothers me about this shirt is that there is no info anywhere that says what the fabric content is, which is something I thought is a requirement for clothing in the US, including imports. Google tells me this clothing company is based in Korea. Beyond that, I like this shirt and I do recommend it.

I was pleasantly surprised by the quality and fit of this BBIBBI Women's Stretch Long Sleeve Button Down Dress Shirt! It came in pristine condition, with impeccable stitching, buttons securely attached, and a quality medium weight fabric that holds it's shape, is not see through, and is a true bright white. All the things I look for in a quality dress shirt!Prior to wearing it, I washed the shirt with other whites on a medium warm cycle and dried it on the regular dryer cycle. It did not shrink, most of the wrinkles came out and it was still a beautiful bright white. I ordered my usual size small and it was a perfect fit. For reference, I am petite (5'1") 125lbs, 32B/C. There was enough length in the body and sleeves for taller women. I liked that it had figure flattering seams and was not boxy. While there was plenty of room for comfortable movement, there was also some stretch for those a bit more busty then me.This shirt is very versatile and looks great on it's own, but would also look great under a sweater, blazer or vest. It would look great with a scarf or tie or open at the neck with a necklace. This type of shirt is a classic and never goes out of style. Considering the quality it should be a staple in your closet for several seasons! I think it is worth the pricepoint.

I’m a tall girl, 5’10”, and it’s so difficult to find dress shirts that fit like they should. Most of the time, sleeves are too short, and if they are fitted, the curve at the waist is too high and the shirt tails too short. Larger sizes just grow outward… however, THIS BBiBBi button down dress shirt is an absolutely PERFECT fit. I can’t even believe it. The shirt is exactly like shown in the product images, and the sleeves are long enough to wear under a blazer, hitting my wrists just as they should. The top is fitted, with no extra bulk under the armpits or through the waist. The fabric is smooth and has some stretch, while not easily wrinkling. It makes for a very comfortable fit. The shirt tails stay tucked in when I raise my arms, and it truly is everything I need in a dress shirt. I wish there were other color choices available, I’d buy more! For the price of this shirt, I feel like I got an excellent product for my money. The shirt has washed well, and it resists wrinkling if I take it out of the dryer while still a bit damp and hang it to finish drying. For reference, I’m about 160 lbs, about a 36 C/D and I ordered a size medium-tall.

These are a thick heavyweight wide leg sweatpants. The cotton is soft and smooth but not as soft as other pants that may be a cotton blend instead of 100% cotton. Haven’t tried washing yet but the care instructions state they can’t be put in the dryer and instead to line dry in the shade. Not the most practical for lounge wear, but hopefully if wash in cold water and dry on low heat they won’t shrink. Slightly snug in waist but it does have a decent bit of stretch still.

These sweat pants are very comfortable. They felt a bit heavy when I first took them out of the package, but I wore them today and they actually weren’t. The wide leg is pretty wide for me, I’m not used to that. But since I only wore them while I was home it was OK. Although I don’t think I will have any issues wearing them out. I had a pair of white sneakers on with them and they looked good. I like the light brown color, it’s very neutral and perfect for the fall. The waist band is a bit wider than I was expecting, but isn’t a bad thing. I like that they have both front and back pockets. They are long, which doesn’t bother me since I’m only 5’2” and all my pants are long. Overall I like the fit and feel of these pants and would recommend them.

Comfy and soft! I’m 5’3 175lbs and wearing size XL. The waist is a snug XL, while everything else is nice and loose. These pants are long on me, i fit 29” inseams or size short best. The length is still great indoors, keeps the feet warm! The material is really comfortable and flexible, plus the loose fit style you do not feel constricted at all for activities. Pockets can fit a cell phone easily. 4 pockets total on these pants. Can tell it is made of really high quality material. Would recommend, especially if you are a regular or taller build getting the XL.

These are an awesome purchase!! I am curvy/plus size and have a hard time finding a pant that fits proportionally let alone is flattering! These fit me so well and are pretty comfy on top of it. These pants are perfect for the office. They don’t show any lint or other material which is very nice. They fit perfectly and as expected. I got a size 4, I am 5’ 7” and 160lbs. I hate scrubs. For 30 years, I would run home to change before I was seen outside of work in them. They never fit right, they’re uncomfortable, the material sucks and is so thin that I’m always cold. I found these and gave one pair a try, without having much hope. Well, let me tell you, they are amazing. They are so comfortable and feel and look so great that I’d wear them outside of work, too! I will never wear anything else. Every time they go on sale, I buy another pair or two because I’m afraid that someday, they’ll disappear and I’ll never be able to find anything like them ever again - they are that great! Some stats - I’m 5’8 and 185 or so, somewhat curvy with a belly and the kind of waist that lends itself to muffin tops. I wear a size L for scrubs and a 12 for jeans. I bought these in both XL - which is a wee bit too long and slightly loose, and a L - which is the perfect size for me and fits like a glove, makes my legs and butt look good, and doesn’t pinch or pull anywhere or give me the dreaded muffin top. LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

I did not realize these are like glorified sweatpants. I was trying to gift my daughter a pair of nice work slacks. I also didn’t realize they had an elastic waistband. These boots are super cute and wide calf friendly! They are not the most comfortable boot ever but as long as you’re not standing for long periods of time they are a yes! I’m 5’3 and they come just below my knee.

I am not used to wearing scrubs, but these seemed nice. I am 5'4" 145lbs and bought the medium petite. The elastic waistband is nice, but if I was a little bigger, they would probably start to be snug on the hips. They aren't tight around my thighs which is nice since they are a little on the large side. They are a little rough and stiff out of the package, but hopefully a wash or two helps that. I paired these with the Cherokee women's v-neck top and the color matched perfectly.

These lined leggings are awesome! Oh my gosh, I’ve always been hesitant about trying them because I am very hot-natured, but even though they are thicker and warm, they are not suffocating. The fabric is very high quality and could easily pass as dress pants if needed. I am 5’5” and go between medium and large (apple shaped), and medium fits perfectly although very little room to spare. I could have probably fit into a large as well but the medium is not uncomfortable.

i don’t recall seeing any plus sized girls review these boots yet, if i missed it, oh well. i am 5’3, 215 lbs. my calves measure at 17.5in around. these AREN’T the most wide calve friendly boots! i was able to get one boot to fully zip up all the way after using rubbing alcohol and some heat (haha). it didn’t take me long though and no damage was caused! they’re stretchy for sure. regardless, i can make these work. they’re SUPER cute, SO so comfy, the heel is the perfect height! i definitely recommend and have already shared the link to so many friends. love these!

I’m always weary of buying clothes online especially pants but with me being pregnant with my second child and not having a maturity store nearby has forced my hand. That being said I am already in love with these pants. They are perfect for the women who are on the taller side of life. I am 5’6 and extremely tiny so it’s extremely hard to find pants that fit in general. I highly recommend these comfy pants and will be purchasing a second pair.

I have to give these pants 5 stars. They are incredibly comfortable and stretch well without losing their shape. I forgot until I tried them on that they were flared, and they sure do have a fun silhouette! The stomach panel is really soft, and I love that it is a silky material that will slide nicely underneath shirts as opposed to bunching. At 5’9 I kind of wish they were slightly longer, but honestly they look exactly like they do on the model in terms of length. Will definitely be getting plenty of wear out of these!

First of all,I expected to have to return these because I’m supposedly 5’4 but I have never had a pair of flares that didn’t drag the ground. So just on a whim I was like let me just try 27 inch inseam bc I’m just now realizing I should have been wearing petite. But other women my height got the 29inch inseam and said it fit perfectly and I’ve never noticed a difference between 29 and 30 both drag the floor. I was assuming because of 4’9-5’0 women ordering the small 27 inch inseam that I would be returning this tomorrow…but somehow these fit me better than any flares ever have. Including waist. A little stretchy but will be perfect with a belt. Go figure 😅Absolutely love these.Worth a try.

They look like expensive work pants! They are made of quality material, very stretchy. I'm 5'0 and have a short torso - I can't remember what I ordered whether it was small petite or xs. I measured the inseam of the Express pants I have with the same flare and they fit perfectly. I am wearing a 3" heel in this picture and they do not drag when I walk. I took my heels off to change into flip flops and they were way too long. Highly recommend! I sat comfortably in an 8 hour conference, then wore them to walk around for another few hours. The pants kept shape.

These knee high boots by CELLENZ are as comfortable as they are stylish. The heel is great for me. It's what the industry calls a "kitten" heel. The modern design of these boots slims and elevates your look, without breaking your back doing so because the heel is so doable and easy to wear. Though the heel is narrow, it's sturdy and balanced. I did not feel tippy at all. The toe box has a gentle point, but it has ample room for an average width foot, such as mine. I do not feel any pinching at the sides or in the tip when I'm still or in motion. My heel does not slip up and down. I can even run in these boots. As for size, I normally wear a US 8 or 8.5 in boots. I ordered these in an 8, and they fit me perfectly. I have thick, athletic legs and I was a bit worried that these might be too tight. But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

I generally love Sorels. I grew up in Canada, and we know great snow boots. 20+ years ago, Sorels were a high quality brand, and they made stuff that lasts. I've read that recently, their quality has suffered. I was intrigued. I live in California now, but I have been wanting snow boots because we've been going back to Canada during the Winter. And, I need good snow boots. I was stoked to get these. So far, although my initial impressions of these were being impressed with the quality, fit and finish, and looks. All of that was moot because of the sizing.I normally wear a size 7.5 womens. And, that's generally half a size up from my actual foot size. We decided to go with an 8 in these, because I told my husband that you go up another half size in snow boots because of thick winter socks. Wow, I'm glad I did, because otherwise the 7.5's would have been WAY too small and borderline abusive to my feet. Even the 8's are too small. The main reason for this is because of the super narrow forefoot and TIGHT toe box. The boots do not open up at the toes, but almost taper inwards like a ballerina shoe. It's barely comfortable at size 8, and I wish I could return these for a larger pair. But, I'm afraid as a Viner, I'm stuck with these. We can't return products.So, yeah, great looking boots, really nice and warm, but way too small and tight. Size up a FULL SIZE for these. I have normal wide feet, even on the narrow side, and these would be way too tight at my regular size.

Love these pants. Super soft, stretchy and very comfortable AND they come in tall girl friendly options!!! Wish more brands on Amazon would take not. Fit is TTS. For reference I’m 5’7” 145 lbs wearing a small.

Yoga pants are so variable! These are not tummy control. The front waist height is exactly as pictured - just below the belly button. Feels a bit like the front is longer than the back - I prefer the reverse. The biggest negative is the material is alint/dust magnet and it doesn't brush off like some others. See picture - I was working in the shop as my usual and I have like 15 different yoga pants for working in the shop and these are the worst for dust magnet. We also have 4 dogs and 2 cats = the cat hair especially loves these pants! The regular length is perfect for me (5'7 30" waist 40" hips) and I usually wear 32" inseam like on Levis jeans. 31" is always just a bit too short for me. These are great length even when sitting down. The medium is slightly looser than other mediums. The iron on label is not irritating like some other brands. Overall I will not get another pair of these because of the lint magnet issue but the construction and quality of fabric is good and non-see thru for working out.

I got the XS olive green yoga pants in the 29" inseam length and they are PERFECT. I have a heck of a time finding pants that fit little me. I'm 5'2" (approx) and weigh under 88 lbs. These look nice and I'm able to get away with wearing them to work (for the Federal Government). They are also comfortable. I need to get these in more colors. The only thing I've noticed is that they aren't super warm if you're in a cold climate. But I can deal with that. I took the pictures yesterday when the pants arrived and am wearing them for real today for the first time.

These are the perfect length! I was worried which length to choose because I’m short, but I ended up ordering the xs, 29inch inseam. It’s perfect! Feels very supportive and not thin at all, and has great tummy control! great for athletic or yoga stuff. Not super soft so I wouldn’t reccomend as much for lounging, it’s more of an athletic material.

***tall ladies ***I’m 6’ 1” and more towards plus size vs a super model build. Lol I absolutely LOVE these pants! I ordered the 37 inch inseam and they are perfect with extra length to spare. I have ordered two more pairs because they are so comfy and have such a flattering fit, plus they are not see through at all with bending and moving. I recommend 100% for fellow tall women. When it comes to pant.... The struggle is real. Lol

These are so comfortable! I am about 130 pounds and 5 ft 3. I got the size Small short. They fit me perfectly in size and length! No need to get them tailored like I usually do. The material is thick and soft. The seams are a little more visible than I typically like but don’t make the pants look too athletic in style. · The fabric is a soft, slightly stretchy ponte knit that is breathable and has a nice, substantial weight to it—it doesn't feel thin or cheap at all. The wide-leg design allows for complete freedom of movement, whether I'm walking, running errands, or just lounging at home. · The taupe color is beautiful—a perfect neutral, warm greige that pairs effortlessly with everything in my closet (blacks, whites, creams, navy, and even pastels). The front pleats and high-rise waistband are incredibly slimming and create a polished, dressy silhouette. They expertly hide any problem areas while making your legs look miles long. · UNEXPECTED AND AMAZING POCKETS: This is absolutely my favorite feature. The pockets are deep, functional, and seamlessly integrated. My large smartphone fits securely without creating a bulky look, which is a rare and wonderful find in women's pants. · Petite Size Available: As someone who is 5'2", I was thrilled that a Petite length was offered. It made all the difference in getting a better fit right out of the package. Things to Consider: · Length for Petites: I am 5'2" and ordered the Petite size. When worn high on my waist, the length is full and still grazes the floor, but it's perfectly manageable with sneakers or any kind of heel. For a clean, ankle-length look with flat shoes, someone my height or shorter would likely need to get them hemmed. · Waistband: The waistband is non-roll and very comfortable. It's a true high-rise, so if you prefer a mid-rise fit, that's something to be aware of. For me, it's perfect for tucking in tops. · Sizing: I typically wear a size Medium and found the Medium Petite to fit me perfectly—true to size. Overall, I am thoroughly impressed with the quality for the price. The substantial weight of the fabric makes these pants look and feel far more expensive than they are. Despite the minor length consideration for my height, they are an incredible value.

Machine washable it says with like colors and tumble dry low. Iron on low.

I have always struggled to find pants that are long enough for me, and I can't even imagine how difficult it would be if I were taller. I am 5' 9", which isn't exactly short either, and I have very long legs for my height. I can't shop in stores, especially for pajama pants because they're always too short. When I wash my clothes, I always let my pants air dry so I don't take the chance that they shrink. I have even gone as far as making my own pajama pants, just so I could have something long enough to wear. When I saw these pajama pants in a size tall, I was so excited! They come in a 32" and a 34" length. I ordered the 34 just in case, and I was absolutely ecstatic when I tried them on and found that they were too long. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on how much they'll shrink, but even if they do, they'll still fit me. The stitching on the pants appears to be good quality, and the fabric is nice and cozy. The pants have a pocket on each side, as well as one on the back. According to the measurements I was in between sizes, so I opted for the larger of the two choices. There is a drawstring if you need to tighten the waist. If you are someone like me who struggles to find pants that are long enough, buy these. Unless you need an inseam longer than 34", you will not be disappointed!

I've been wearing these PJ pants for weeks now. They are so comfy and warm but not too hot. The fabric isn't stretchy so you'll definitely need to take that into consideration. I got the medium 32" inseam (not sure of my exact inseam measurements but I am 5'4") but I could have even gone up a size for a little extra room. I'm typically around a smaller and medium usually, but for this a large would have been just fine. While I'm stoked it has pockets at all and they are fairly deep, I wish there were two pockets in the back. Sometimes if I'm just standing and talking I like to rest both hands in the back pockets. And considering I'm wearing the literally everywhere it's kind of annoying. But overall they aren't bad sleep/lounge pants!

I ordered the 32” inseam pants and was impressed that they were exactly that! It can be difficult to find pants to fit my inseam, unless I want capris. I would consider these a good quality lightweight flannel, they’re soft to the touch and didn’t pill after washing. All that being said, these have zero stretch and if you’re on the larger end of a size, consider going up one size if you like a more relaxed, comfortable fit. These are made for tall, slim figures - if you’re like me and are curvy through the hips or thighs or plus size, I’d encourage you to look elsewhere. Slim figured and wonderfully long legged? These could be a great fit for you! THEY HAVE POCKETS!

Love these jeans! Perfect color, not too dark, not too light. They are not pull-on but they do have stretch, I am so glad I ordered a size down because this size gives me room but aren’t baggy anywhere, they fit nice, the next size up would have been big, everywhere. I am 5’7” but have always worn long or tall inseams and these fit perfect in length but they bunch a little at the bottom. As a Levi’s wearer since I was a young teen, these are the same quality but are softer and not stiff like the ones I wore as a teen. Will be buying more! The material isn’t thin, it’s very decent. The legs are straight. I have muscular calves and these were not tight on my calves. The pockets are small so you can’t get a cell phone in them, a few keys or maybe a credit card would fit. I didn’t really notice the shaping much but they felt comfy when I put them on.

Review of TTZ Women's Tall Pajama Pants These pajama pants have a warm, cozy look with their blue and green plaid pattern. They're made from 100% cotton, that feels like a soft, breathable, lightweight flannel. There is no stretch in the fabric, but the relaxed, loose-fit design makes them comfortable to lounge or sleep in without feeling restrictive. They have a 32" inseam, which works well for taller wearers. The elastic waistband is comfortable and easy to pull on, and the drawstring adds a bit of adjustability for a better fit. The legs are straight with very little taper. The thigh is about 13-1/2” across. The hem is just about 11” across. From waist to bottom of hem is approximately 44-1/2” long. From top of waist to point of crotch is approximately 16-1/4” long. Overall, the quality looks and feels good. The seams are neatly sewn, and I didn't notice any flaws.

I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

If you are tall like me you might feel it’s hard to find jeans that are long enough. These are really great. I got the Long version of my size and they are the perfect length. I am about 5”11 and they reach a little past my ankles which i love. The only downside (which is why i rated it only 4/5 stars) is that the color is not accurate. I ordered the lightest color and they look a little darker. Overall I am really happy with them. They are comparable to the american eagle curvy stretch jeans, very comfy.

As a consistent TTZ customer, I expected a premium lounge pant, especially at a $30 price point. Unfortunately, these are a major disappointment in both durability and value. While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

Pros: - No front seam! - Soft mostly-matte fabric molds to your curves and is squat-proof - Super stretchy so you feel no restriction or tightness in the legs when you're moving around - Double lined waistband offers moderate compression and helps you feel held in - High rise waistband hits right at my belly button - Spacious pockets on both sides can easily fit a large phone - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - Flattering booty seam with hidden scrunch offers just a little extra definition and doesn't look scrunchy when you're wearing it - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing all day and washing once so far, but I'd say any fabric like this is delicate, so be careful around rough surfaces. Also, the scrunch hasn't lost it's scrunchiness after wearing all day. - Neat seams and no loose threads, overall quality feel to the fabric - I'm honestly surprised at how much longer my legs look in photos when I'm wearing these pants (Possible) Cons: - I have short legs despite being 5'5" and they're JUST the right length with sneakers that have a chunky sole. These pants have a 32" inseam and 31" might have been perfect on me. - These are mini-flared pants, so they might take some getting used to if you're more familiar with regular flared pants. I have 14" calves so I had mixed feelings about how snug they were on my calves at first, but they grew on me and I like them more now! I loved wearing these on a mini road trip and think they're great for any time you want to look cute and be comfortable. They'd also be fine for working out, but I usually stick with regular non-flared leggings for that.

Pros: - No front seam! - Soft mostly-matte fabric molds to your curves and is squat-proof - Super stretchy so you feel no restriction or tightness in the legs when you're moving around - Double lined waistband offers moderate compression and helps you feel held in - High rise waistband hits right at my belly button - Spacious pockets on both sides can easily fit a large phone - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - Flattering booty seam with hidden scrunch offers just a little extra definition and doesn't look scrunchy when you're wearing it - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing all day and washing once so far, but I'd say any fabric like this is delicate, so be careful around rough surfaces. Also, the scrunch hasn't lost it's scrunchiness after wearing all day. - Neat seams and no loose threads, overall quality feel to the fabric - I'm honestly surprised at how much longer my legs look in photos when I'm wearing these pants (Possible) Cons: - I have short legs despite being 5'5" and they're JUST the right length with sneakers that have a chunky sole. These pants have a 32" inseam and 31" might have been perfect on me. - These are mini-flared pants, so they might take some getting used to if you're more familiar with regular flared pants. I have 14" calves so I had mixed feelings about how snug they were on my calves at first, but they grew on me and I like them more now! I loved wearing these on a mini road trip and think they're great for any time you want to look cute and be comfortable. They'd also be fine for working out, but I usually stick with regular non-flared leggings for that.

I wear these to work out and on those days when I just don’t feel like wearing jeans—ladies, you know exactly what I mean 😉 The fit is just right with amazing tummy control that’s both flattering and comfortable. I’m 5'7", and the length hits perfectly!

I very rarely make Amazon reviews but these jeans are just too good not to say something! They truly feel like real jean material, not see through, and are nice and thick to hide any cellulite- which I appreciate. With the colder months here I’m confident they are thick enough to keep me warm, and once the temps REALLY drop (since I live in the northern U.S.) I’m sure a pair of fleece lined leggings will fit underneath well. At the same time, they are also very stretchy and comfortable. I am 25 weeks pregnant, 5’6 and currently 197lbs (185 pre pregnancy) I typically carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I got the large and they fit perfectly and are stretchy enough to allow me room to grow. It feels amazing to try on a pair of jeans and just smile bc everything fits so well after so many weeks/months of feeling like a frumpy blob lol pre-pregnancy I was used to buying brands like Bridge by Glee and Flying Monkey, which are designed to really lift and flatter the booty. These jeans are definitely not *that* flattering on the butt, but they don’t do me a disservice in that area either. I’m just glad to have something that fits and is on trend at this point. Giving bonus points bc they have real front pockets too! These jeans are definitely worth the cost and are something I plan to keep to wear through future pregnancies as well. Overall, they function well and will serve their purpose well through the rest of my pregnancy. I imagine they will even be nice to wear in my post-partum period also. The light wash that I have here is great for casual, everyday wear and I’m going to go and buy a second pair in a darker wash as soon as I’m done writing this post. If you’re curvy with hips and thighs like me, don’t hesitate to buy these jeans. They’re perfect! P.S. ignore what appears to be discoloration in one of the pictures. I took these photos in dim lighting and I think my phone was just trying to auto-adjust to make the photo appear brighter. These jeans are not discolored :)

To start, the material feels pretty thick and durable. It sort of has that kind of swishy polyester sweatpants feeling/sound when you walk. They are not super breathable and feel a bit “heavy”. Aesthetically they look pretty good and I love the amount of pockets! Now the downsides. (For reference I am 5’10” and 190). I want to love these, especially because they are so affordable, but these pants were not made with butts in mind. The material has some stretch, however, the way the pants are cut does not allow enough room for anyone with a butt. To accommodate, the waist line is pulled way up in the front and way down in the back. It REALLY accentuates the little belly pouch that us women have. When sitting, you have to be really lucky (or skinny maybe) for your underwear/crack not to show and for the front of the waistband not to sever your internal organs. Pictures included. I bend and squat and sit on the floor in my job so these factors really impact my ability to move comfortably. I just really wanted a pink scrub set for Valentine’s Day and I guess I should have known better than choose a cheap set. At least the top is able to be worn comfortably! I included photos to show the waistline and how it fits both standing and (I’m sorry you have to see me like that lol) squatting. Hope this helps a fellow big booty judy!

These G4Free sweatpants are the first ones I will be wearing that first chilly fall day! The modal fabric is everything- substantial, silky smooth, sleek looking and wrinkle proof! There is a nice choice of inseam lengths. I probably could have used a 28” length but ordered 29” and I don’t mind them a little slouchy. For reference I am 5 feet tall 115 pounds.I love the more tailored, refined style of these sweatpants. The leg width is not overly wide, flattering for petites like me as well as talls. The pockets, both front and back, also lend a polished look if you wished to wear these as business casual attire. The waist band is high but lays flat. These pants may seem pricey, but I take into account the versatility. I would definitely wear them out for a casual dinner. Plus, all of the special features add up to a unique pair of slacks. Highly recommend these wide leg sweatpants and would definitely buy another pair from the wide selection of colors.

I am so impressed with these pants. The material feels beautifully soft and silky and the manufacturing quality is excellent. They have pockets in the front although I think pockets are unnecessary. I don’t like how you can see the outline of the interior pocket so I will probably sew up the pocket entrance to create faux pockets and then cut out the pocket lining. The back has faux pockets and they look cute. In terms of sizing, I ordered an XS with a 27” inseam. I’m 5”4 and 122lb and these fit exactly how I would expect an XS to fit. The pant leg length was perfect for me but I do have short legs so I would say for someone with average length legs, these would be too short on anyone taller than 5’2”. Overall, I am extremely happy with these pants and I plan to order another pair in a different color.

I originally bought these in the navy in a size 14 (5’6” 200# and an apple that carries most of weight in the belly). I really liked them but they were snug. So when I bought these blue, I sized up to a 16 and they fit perfect. Super stretchy with thick material, very slight VPL in the light color. Waistband is very comfortable. Front pockets are decent, back pockets are fake. The front leg seams give it a professional look. Length is perfect hitting me right at the top of the foot. The price is excellent at around $16 right now. A solid buy

First the stats: I am almost 70, 5’4”, waist 27”, hips38”,inseam 29”, 112lbs. AI suggested size 8. I like my waist band a little loose because of a back issue. I probably should have gotten a 6. These are a little bit big; just a bit. I will definitely need to tuck in my shirt to help hold up the pants. I’ll probably use a belt as well. The rise comes even with my navel.They fit like a nice pair of slacks. I will be able to wear these when playing tour guide to visiting friends and I can wear a merino wool base layer underneath without feeling uncomfortable. I live in the Colorado mountains and it can get pretty cold. The length is what I like. The legs reach the top of my shoes. The fabric is very nice. Not thin. Pockets are a decent size and don’t show through the fabric. Rear pockets are fake but I don’t ever use those when pants have real ones. The sewn in crease makes them look dressy. I am satisfied with these pants and will keep them. If I find they are too big after wearing them for a while I will run a little bit of elastic through the waistband. I would recommend these pants if you want to look nice in cold weather.

Before you look at my photos, keep in mind that I am an overweight (size 16/XL) senior citizen. I think these fit me perfectly. I rarely wear pull-on pants because for some reason they always slip down on me, so adding belt loops to pull-on pants was a genius move on the part of the designer. I am 5’7” tall and usually wear a 31” length, but based on other reviews which mention that they shrink, I ordered the 33” length and I love the way the pants buckle onto my shoes. I will do everything to keep them from shrinking, even if I have to figure out how to hang dry them in the basement. As soon as I tried them on, I sat down and ordered two more pairs !!!

Perfect for my work. So professional, and so comfortable. They feel like yoga pants. Fit perfect. Will be buying more! I am 5’4’’ and was worried about the length since I am short, but they are perfect length wise.

These are nothing more than cheap bootcut leggings with faux buttons and pockets. I am all for comfort, but these ARE NOT dress pants and look ridiculous with business attire. I am 5’5” and they are too long and not exactly the type of pants you would hem. The second picture compares a pair of my leggings with the pants, which are so thin that you would either freeze to death in the winter or they would be drenched in sweat in the summer. Plus they would snag easily. If you are looking for dress pants pick something else.

I absolutely love these pants. For reference, I am 5’7.5, 160. I got the medium in the long. They fit perfectly and were so comfortable and soft but still look like business pants. They are easy to iron and wash nicely too. I won’t dry them because they’re the perfect length for me with heels and o do not want them to shrink. I am buying 2 more colors because I loved them so much. I didn’t get very many pictures. Wish I had taken more!

So stretchy, comfy, just the right thickness PLUS with real belt loops!! I was looking for a pair of lightweight black pants for traveling and these are great. I don’t recommend them for summer temps but every other time they’re great. The fabric isn’t as thick as the original name brand yoga pant. The pockets lay flat and they look as close to a “real” pant as possible. I usually buy short length but I purchased a Large 30” inseam. I’m 5’2” and 164 lbs with a pear shape & tummy. I usually wear a 12 short (sometimes 10 on a great day). My inseam is 28.5” but I like to wear 1” wedges, so the 30” looked the best on me.

I'm 165lbs at 5' 5 1/2" & got 2 pairs size 28/30/32. I loved the first pair so much that I got a second pair. Being a bit spendy, I have to wait before I can get a 3rd pair. These boots are very nice and I’m happy with the purchase. I ordered an 8.5 and they fit well. Normally I wear a size 8, but opted to size up due to having slightly wide feet, and boots tending to be more narrow. They’re sleek, flattering, and the kitten heel makes them easy to style with both dresses and skirts without being too tall for everyday wear. The only thing I’ll note is the height. I’m 5’5.5”, and on me, they sit about 1.5–2 inches below my knee, rather than the 4 inches shown on the model. Still, it’s a good look and adds a dressier touch. The material feels nice, great color, they don’t look cheap, and they shouldn’t require much break-in time. Overall, a stylish and versatile pair of boots that work well for both casual and more polished outfits.

Wow! These boots literally tell the truth and they go a good ways above my knee. They are very warm and fit well. They are super shaggy - the area at the bottom could use a little trimming as the hairs are a bit longer than necessary. The fur looks cheap at first glance, but the boots do look good on. It’s like wearing stuffed animals on your legs. When first putting on the Sweetheart Beauty thigh high shaggy fur boots, it was clear that the inside main part of the boots are made from either very thick cardboard or not-so-stiff interface/sizing; they bent a little. The second and third times I put them on, I was at houses where shoes must be removed. Actually, that means I’ve put them on 2x on those days. Putting the boots back on the last time I WORE them, but the 6th time I put them on, I heard a rip. Then the interface bent so much in both boots that my feet were not able to get all the way in to the bottom. Determined to wear these boots at least a couple more times, I figured I could use my boot horn to get back in and fix the interface. The boots are so tall, the boot horn wasn’t long enough! If this happens to you, push down the boot leg as far down as you can get it and flatten out the interface. NEVER pull on the boot from the top. Do it as carefully as you would pull on tights or hose. Back to the review. $99 is way too much for boots that have cheap faux fur (it’s called “fake” at this level) and zero arch support. Make sure the weather and the ground are dry before stepping out because you are wearing thick, furry socks with thick soles (but we knew that!) As of 12/31/25, they are on sale for $65 with a coupon. That should be that starting price point, and then some discounts for sales from there. I will continue to wear my boots until they fall apart. They get 2 stars because they are cute, temporary shoes that are way overpriced.

I bought these to wear for boarding day on an upcoming cruise to match some custom shirts I'm making. The first thing I was attracted to was the rich color. It is exactly as I'd hoped and true to the description. The color is vibrant and these are well made. The fabric is thick so I wouldn't advise wearing in warm weather. I sized down per the reviews and it worked for me. They are are a tad bit long on me even though I ordered short but I am only 4'11. I love how these hug me and the stretch is good enough. These look high end and I appreciate the tailoring. There are small things that stand out for me from other brands I sometimes come across like the fact that threads and buttons match the pants. These seem like they will last a while in my wardrobe. I totally recommend.

These are hands down my favorite wide-leg jeans right now. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 127 lbs, wearing a size 24, and the fit is excellent. They sit comfortably at the waist, are fitted through the hips and thighs, and then fall into the perfect wide leg. No pulling, no stiffness, and no awkward bunching. The fabric is SO soft with just the right amount of stretch. They feel broken-in right out of the package, which is rare for denim. I can wear these all day without feeling restricted, and they keep their shape really well. One of the biggest surprises is how flattering they are in the back. I have a flatter bottom (so I don’t say this lightly), and these actually give shape and lift without feeling tight or compressive. The soft black wash is rich and versatile, easy to dress up or down. I’ve worn them with boots, heels, and sneakers, and they work with everything. They instantly make an outfit look polished and intentional. If you’re looking for wide-leg jeans that are comfortable, flattering, and actually wearable for real life, these are a must-buy. I’d absolutely repurchase.

Updated Review (2025.12.09) - I ordered 3 pairs—dark wash, regular wash and khaki. Out of the 3, I kept the dark wash (pics 4&5) and returned the other 2. While the leg opening is larger than the waist, it’s not as extreme with the dark wash. I will say these are very comfortable and provide just the right amount of stretch, and did not stretch out at the end of the day. If you have a lower belly pooch, these will cover/slim you but if you eat anything, you’ll definitely feel these being tighter (due to hitting right below the belly button). I am 5’4”, 140lbs and the 26x32 fit like a glove, but I do need to either wear platform shoes or shoes with a small (1”) heal (I’m wearing wedges in pics 4&5), otherwise, they drag the ground. As much as I love wide leg jeans, these are giving me mid-90’s JNCO vibes. I love the fit as they have stretch, good fit in the waist and thighs and sits just below belly button but no plumbers crack); sadly, since the leg opening is wider than the waist, I am returning these.

I’m 30 weeks with 4th baby, about 175lbs 5’5”. These pants are comfy. I ordered a L. I honestly probably would prefer a M, but I’m at the very end and I think a M would truly be too tight over these last 2 months. I think the L is a little bit loose on my belly but feels really good on my hips since they are pretty ready to go and have moved a ton. LOL. The belly fabric feels like butter but maybe not quite compressing enough to keep them from slipping down a little. I think a little elastic adjustment band at the top would help with this issue or a more compressing fabric. Everything else is super comfortable. I really enjoy the pockets. I do have concerns that there will be pilling on the insides of the legs based on the fabric, but it hasn’t happened yet. I couldn’t get my belly to stop casting shadows on the crotch area, but I will say that the way these are cut, and how the belly part is, there is possibility for camel toe, all in all, I like them, and I’m honestly just happy to have clothes that fit. Hallelujah!

I'm 6 foot tall with long legs and on the hefty side. finding big and tall pants is always a problem. When these pants arrived I tried them on and fell in love with them. the fit perfectly, the material is soft and stretchy, and they look professional enough to wear to work. I like them so much I immediately ordered a second pair. But my joy turned to disappointment after I put them on after the first wash and dry. they were now frustratingly just short enough to look and feel awkward. I laid them out ontop of the new unwashed pair and sure enough, they were about 2.5 inches shorter! So disappointing. I really hope the manufacturer reads this and does something to correct it in the future for all of us big and tall women out there struggling to find good clothes.If it were not for the shrinkage I would defintiely gives these pants 5 stars.

These are okay pants. They’re not exactly bad I just know I’m not going to want to live in them like I was hoping. First of all, they’re much shorter than the picture. I’m a little above average height and my inseam is typically 31-32” so it will fit shorter girls better I just can’t get behind it for me. I recently picked up these brown corduroy pants in size medium, and they’re an absolute delight! The corduroy is of exceptional quality—medium thick with deep, velvety grooves that give it a soft, flexible texture and a gorgeous sheen that’s even richer in person than in the photos. At 5’1” with a 28-29” waist, the high-waisted, wide-leg design fits beautifully, reaching my ankles for a relaxed yet stylish look. The elastic drawstring waist adds comfort, and the six pockets are a practical bonus, perfect for carrying essentials. The fit is oversized and relaxed. The stitching is sturdy and well-crafted, suggesting these pants will hold up through plenty of wear. Planning to pair them with oversized peplum blouses and sweaters, they transition effortlessly from casual outings to slightly dressier moments. The brown color has a warm, luxurious vibe that’s ideal for fall. My only note is that the wide legs can feel a bit voluminous on me, but they’re so comfortable and chic that I don’t mind. Great value.

These dress pants are the most comfortable I’ve had in decades! They fit beautifully and feel amazing on. At 5’9”, I opted for the 32-inch length, which is perfect for my height. I’m always hesitant to order pants online because of my height and weight distribution, but these are a definite hit, and I am so glad that I got them. I just find them to be really attractive. I tend to carry my weight in my hips and thighs, and the material has a slimming effect that I really appreciate. The fabric is a blend of nylon and Lycra, which I believe makes them so comfortable. They also have two convenient front pockets and two artificial pockets in the back, adding to the style without compromising the sleek fit. I absolutely love these pants and know they’ll get plenty of wear. I’m even planning to bring them on my upcoming European trip because they’re easy to pack without wrinkles and how versatile they are. They can easily be dressed up with heels for a polished look or dressed down with flats, and even dark sneakers for a more casual vibe. These pants are a true wardrobe staple, suitable for any occasion.

I clipped them and the seams themselves are still strong and not unraveling. Idc though as long as they last another 20 weeks I’ll have no complaints.

Omg. First pair of cowboy boots, was always intimidated by breaking them in. These are so comfortable right out of the box!! And they look AMAZING! I can’t believe how they look for the price. Perfect width and height for me; I’m 5’7” 140lbs with athletic calves - they hit right under my knee and hug my calves in a flattering and comfortable way! I love everything about them - the price, the color, the stitching, the fit - obsessed!

I’m 5’6. I wear 7.5 in all shoes. But 8 in boots with a heel. . I have a narrow foot, really skinny ankles and calves. Purchased 7.5 becausee most reviews said it ran a little big. With socks on it was a little snug but boots stretched a tad and they got more comfortable by the end my night. Wore for eight hours at a concert. Danced & walked so much. Zero blisters. My toes were a little sore the next morning but normal for first time wear. There was a deep crease down the center/front of the boot. I blow dried on a low setting to soften the material then put a 5 lb weight on it to flatten for a hour or so to get crease out. It worked a lil bit. They did scrunch up a tiny bit by the ankle (you can in in photos). Honestly the only negative about the boots. But overall they’re great quality boots. They are so so so affordable! I own $200+ boots and I swear these boots are neck n neck. Plan on getting different colors!

These are nice looking pants. The material feels nicer than what it is, not flimsy and not see through. I really like the color - a warm brown.They fit really well at the waist. I’m 5’7” 120lbs, with a 26”waist. The pants are a tad too short, they are not crop length either. They would work nicely with flats but that’s about it. When I put on sneakers that tend to have a thicker sole, I’d need the pants to be about an .5 -1 inch longer. Forget about wearing these with heels, way too short. There’s about 1/2 inch that can be unhemmed if needed, but is it worth the time/$$?However, they did cut some corners with the construction, for example the seams on the hem are typically invisible on dressy pants, however on these, they didn’t bother with that level of detail and just sewed a basic stitch on the hem all the through which makes it look cheap and low quality. All my other dress pants from Ann Taylor, Bebe, Cache, even old Express pants all have invisible hems which is a much cleaner look.Second issue is with the waistband tab - the tab once closed sticks out quite a bit which it is not supposed to do that. It seems that here again whoever made these pants was not a professional because on a waistband tab that’s as wide the one on these pants, they should have used two hook + bar closures instead of just one. Plus, the hook + bar closures should have been placed closer to the end of the tab as well to prevent it from sticking out. Again comparing to my existing pants that have the same waistband closure detail, these are clearly “cheaply” made and it shows.

The flower pattern featured on this set of shoes is classically adorable. I normally wear a size 8.5 in all shoe brands. Since there are no half sizes available, I decided to order a size up to be safe. I ordered the size 9 for my 8.5 feet and I will say that it seemed to be a perfect fit. The shoes run a little bit more narrow than most of my other shoes. They were very snug around the width of my feet. I ordered the short version of these boots which covers a small portion of my calf muscles. I have extremely thick legs, but the circumference of the boots is big enough to accommodate my thick calves. The material and build of the boots is waterproof. I tested this out by walking through puddles of water after it had rained outside. The boots did an excellent job at keeping my feet dry. For very heavy storms, I would recommend the tall version of the boots. If I were to splash around a lot, it’s possible that water would go up into the inside of the boots with the short version of this product. There are adjustable straps to tighten or loosen around the calf muscle. I find the shorter height to be extremely cute. The boots are fashionable, functional, and comfortable. I do wish the width was a bit bigger, but my feet still do fit in snuggly. The current price of $70 seems like a fair value for the money based on the quality and usefulness of the item.

I have two pairs and I am quite satisfied with them. I purchased one in gray a long time (years) ago, and the navy just 2 weeks ago. I'm not sure but the materials feel different to me. The gray is softer, body-hug, while the navy is cooler, straight-fit, still a little body-hug. Pros: 1. versatile for casual and formal enough for office wear. I always want multi-setting functional items like this. 2. Good for a petite body. I'm 5 ft, 102 lbs, with a short torso and prefer mid-high waisted (10 in from belly button to crotch). I got XXS, it fits well generally. Cons: 1. Weird shape at the crotch. It may or may not be a camel toe. To me, it's more like the extra fabric formed to one side and gives a weird look at the crotch. Darker color (navy) helps to hide this. 2. Wide ankles 3. Will pill over time on areas around the pockets and butts

I bought the dark wash and I’m honestly really impressed. For reference, I’m 5’3”, weigh about 161 lbs, and I’m 50 years old. I’ve always struggled to find jeans that fit properly without making me look bigger than I already feel. I have a short torso and longer legs, so jeans are usually a challenge for me. I’m currently in a weight-loss phase, exercising and watching what I eat, so I didn’t want to invest in a whole new wardrobe. I just needed a few pieces that fit now and actually look appropriate and flattering. I’m also very picky and self-conscious about how I look, so it took a lot for me to even post vid/photos, but I’m hoping this helps someone else in the same boat. I ordered a Size 10, and these fit EXACTLY how I wanted. They’re amazingly comfortable, well-proportioned, and don’t dig in. I even had leggings on underneath (it was cold today), and they still fit comfortably no struggling, no holding my breath. That alone sold me. The color is great, and while the material isn’t “Levi’s-level premium”, these jeans are far from cheap or scratchy. They’re soft, comfy, and feel like good everyday jeans. For the price, the quality is more than fair. Also follow the care instructions. These should be washed in cold water and dried on a low setting. If you take care of them properly, they hold up really well. A lot of fit and fabric complaints come down to not reading the care label. Since these are discontinued colors, I’ll probably grab one or two more pairs, but that’s it. I’m not building a full wardrobe while I’m losing weight. For context, I was 167 lbs at the end of November and hadn’t bought anything for myself until now, so I’m really happy these fit and did more than just “the job.” If you’re shorter, curvy, or in between sizes and looking for something comfortable and flattering without breaking the bank, these are definitely worth trying.

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.
